summary: "JavaScript.RequestRegistration - File Order Issue"
description: """We're running into issues with the core implementation of JavaScript.RequestRegistration.  We have installed a newer version of Knockout.js thus creating a newer entry in the JavaScriptLibraries folder. Due to the default nature of how these are registered - it's the file order priority that screws this up.      Example:  Knockout.js 3.3.0 is installed.  Also added are Kncokout.Mapping 2.4.1, and we install Knockout.validation.js 2.0.3  During development - we upgrade to knockout.js 3.4.1 - install the extension as per the rules.  Because Mapping and validation are not also updated (they don't need to be) we run into issues.    It's in the JavaScript.cs file ([located here|https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/blob/development/DNN%20Platform/Library/Framework/JavaScriptLibraries/JavaScript.cs]) at lines 449 - the private static in GetFileOrder function.    If the javascript library isn't one of the default core (jquery, hoverintent, etc) - it gets the DefaultPriority (100) PLUS the JavaScriptLibrary table's Package ID.    Thus - but upgrading our knockout version - it has a Primary key ID of 165 (plus 100 = 265) which is higher than the validation package id.    This breaks our custom modules even though we are following DNN best practices.  The work around is to change the knockout.js extension to load in the HEAD But this is not the preferred solution!!!    It would be ideal if we could pass a priority into the JavaScript.RequestRegistration call - that takes the overall highest priority(?) or something similar.  Or best if because the dependencies are known by knockout.mapping and knockout.validation - that it would load knockout.js first.    Here's a screenshot of the JavaScriptLibraries table: https://www.screencast.com/t/zbi1ISQE"""

summary: "Persona Bar iFrame disables page when edit is toggled"
description: """After the Persona Bar Edit button is toggled, clicking on links or buttons in the main content area and the lower part of the PB has no effect. This is because the PB iFrame is resized on clicking the Edit button. An attribute """"width:100%"""" is applied to the iFrame which extends it over the page content area.    *Steps to reproduce*  # Create a new DNN9 installation  # Log in as host  # In development tools observe the width attribute on the Persona Bar iFrame  # On the home page, click the Persona Bar Edit button  # Move the mouse over the PB Close button and click  # Observe application response  # Move the mouse over any other link on the page other than in the PB left panel and click  # Observe application response  # In development tools observe the width attribute on the Persona Bar iFrame    *Expected results*  3. width:80px  6. Button changes appearance as the mouse passes over; on click, the panel closes  8. As 6  9. width:80px    *Actual results*  3. width:80px  6. Button does not respond  8. As 6  9. width:100%  """

summary: "Cannot create a new page from Pages > Add a New Page if Languages/Localization is enabled "
description: """This Issue is a follow up to DNN-8487 (and my copy of it DNN-10722)    *Problem*: In a multi language environment adding pages does not always work.  *Reproduction*:  1) Create a DNN environment with TWO Languages (eg. EN / FR)  2) Create a parent page in two languages (eg. EN / FR)  3) Create a Child Page with two languages (EN / FR)  4) Now ADD a third Language to the portal (eg. DE) with """"Add missing languages"""" - Button  5) go to the CHILD page and try to add missing language --> Page reloads, language is not added, error as in  DNN-8487    *Workaround / Fix:*  After above Step 4:  4a) Go to PARENT Page, edit localisation and add missing translation  5) go to the CHILD page, edit localisation and add missing translation  6) CHILD Page is successfully created in 3rd language"""

summary: "Folders Missing Permissions after creating a Portal"
description: """On a clean install of 9.2.2 I have folders created for the portal but there are no permissions created for the folders.    The DNN Url control displays a warning about not being authorized. This is down to the code in the framework which uses a SP GetFoldersByPermissions. This has an inner join on the Folders and FolderPermission tables. The FolderPermission table is empty for me on a root portal and also any new portals I created.    A work around is to open the folder properties in the file manager and click save. This then creates the permission but only on the folder and not any child folders.  This did work in previous versions of DNN without issue. It would appear the code init the portal folders is not setting the permissions in the table."""

For e.g  https://dnndev.me/DependencyHandler.axd?s=filekey&t=Javascript&cdv=51    In our case, we are using urlType=""""Base64QueryStrings"""" for file processing provider so the dependency handler url is generated with query string parameter.     {code:c#|title=Web.config|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#F7D6C1}      <compositeFiles defaultFileProcessingProvider=""""DnnCompositeFileProcessor"""" compositeFileHandlerPath=""""~/DependencyHandler.axd"""">        <fileProcessingProviders>           <add name=""""CompositeFileProcessor"""" type=""""ClientDependency.Core.CompositeFiles.Providers.CompositeFileProcessingProvider, ClientDependency.Core"""" enableCssMinify=""""true"""" enableJsMinify=""""true"""" persistFiles=""""false"""" compositeFilePath=""""~/App_Data/ClientDependency"""" bundleDomains="""""""" urlType=""""Base64QueryStrings"""" />          <add name=""""DnnCompositeFileProcessor"""" type=""""DotNetNuke.Web.Client.Providers.DnnCompositeFileProcessingProvider, DotNetNuke.Web.Client"""" enableCssMinify=""""false"""" enableJsMinify=""""true"""" persistFiles=""""true"""" compositeFilePath=""""~/App_Data/ClientDependency"""" bundleDomains="""""""" urlType=""""Base64QueryStrings"""" />        </fileProcessingProviders>      </compositeFiles>  {code}    Please note that this is only happening in post back event of partial rendering. We think that when the js and css are added in server script manager to render dependencies, it doesn't need to replace & with & because the value is literally kept as string and doesn't need to do html escape in this case. We also attached the files that we made fixing.   Let us know if you need more info.   Kind regards,  Nang Nang   """

summary: "All pages except home page return 404"
description: """Hi there,  We've noticed that sporadically our sites will return a 404 response for all pages except the home page. We've witnessed that the behavior always starts to be exhibited directly after the application pool is recycled. Once  the 404 results start occurring the only resolution is to recycle the application pool again which then clears the tab dictionary from cache so that it can be reloaded again.    I've reviewed the code & believe that the root cause of the issue is due to the fact that the code that builds the tab index, portalDepths dictionary &  tabPaths dictionary is not thread safe. I can see code in the method TabIndexController.FetchTabDictionary is using SharedDictionary classes to store the tab dictionaries, however the code is not threadsafe when adding the dictionaries to the cache. Therefore when multiple threads are executing the FetchTabDictionary method it's possible for an empty dictionary to be added to the cache.     To resolve this issue the code needs to be updated so that only one thread can add the dictionaries at a time.  I have provided an updated version of the TabIndexController class with the FetchTabDictionary method modified as an attachment. I've also added another private helper method called PortalExistsIndex in the TabIndexController class, that is used by the FetchTabDictionary to determine if the portal has already been added to the index.     This is an issue that seems to have been in DNN for some time. Here are a couple of links to others that have experienced similar behavior which seems like it might be due to the same issue.  http://www.dnnsoftware.com/forums/threadid/525944/scope/posts/404-error-for-all-pages-except-home-page  http://www.dnnsoftware.com/forums/threadid/524447/scope/posts/http-error-code-404-page-not-found  """
comments: "New version of code attached with fix to issue where exceptions could occur in a situation where the tabpath dictionary was dropped from cache and the portal depths dictionary was still in cache. In this case an exception was raised when adding the portal depths info because the item already existed in the dictionary. The call to the Add method on the dictionary has been replaced with a call to the indexer so that if the item is already in the dictionary it will be replaced." [SEP] "instead of attaching a fix, it would be even better, if you could create a pull request for development branch at https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform" [SEP] "Thanks Sebastian,    A pull request has now bee submitted.     Regards,  Cameron." [SEP] "Cameron,  thank you very much, we'll review it,  Best,  Sebastian" [SEP] "What’s the status of this PR? Anyone have a link? A project of mine just suffered the consequences of this bug. Thank you." [SEP] "Hi Patrick,  This PR was merged into source for the DNN 9.3.0 RC release

summary: "Redirect to Login page fails when accessing a page with permission ""Registered users"" in a multi-language portal when navigating with the second languge"
description: """This issue is to isolate the remaining issues in the original ticket:https://dnntracker.atlassian.net/browse/DNN-7812.  {color:red}*+Remaining issues:+*{color}  Using Evoq_Content_9.2.0.102-818. & Evoq_Content_9.2.0.121-879.  *+Prerequisite data:+*  - Site with two locals installed correctly.  - Login page created, published, localized for both of the locals with view permission set for All users.  - Login page is set in the site settings for both of them.  - Restricted page created, published, localized for both of the locals with view permission set for registered users only.  *+Covered Scenarios:+*  # (x) Main site, with two locals, while logged out, trying to refresh the url with no language parameters or pages specified, should redirect to home page of default language. Bug: It redirects to the home page of the 2nd locale.  https://www.screencast.com/t/JxjtLJOr  # (x) For a child site, same above mentioned case, redirects after refresh to the same language that was loaded before the refresh, while it should redirect to the default language >Bug.    {color:red}*+The Original customer issue was:+*{color}  Brief Description of Issue.  When accessing a page with permission """"Registered users"""" of a multi-language portal using the second language (that is different from the default one), the user is redirected to the home page of the default language instead of the login page of the current language.    Steps to reproduce the issue.  1: Install a DNN 7.4.2. blank website in English (United States).  2: Add a second language (ie. it-IT).  3: Enable """"Allow Content Localization"""" in Host > Host settings > Other settings.  4: In Admin > Languages press button """"Enable Localized Content"""". In the opened popup window set """"Make all pages Translatable"""" (English - United States will be set as Current Site Default) and press """"Enable Localized Content"""".  5: In Admin > Languages set """"Enabled"""" check box of the added second language in """"Enabled"""" column.  6: In Admin > Languages click on the icon """"Create pages in this language"""" in """"Pages"""" column.  7: In Admin > Languages click on """"Active"""" check box in """"Active"""" column.  8: Translate and publish the home page of the second language  9: In English - United State language, add a Login page. Add """"Account login"""" module to this page. Set page permission to """"All users"""" for this page.   10: In second language, copy """"Account login"""" module, translate and publish the Login page.  11: Set the Login page for both languages in Admin > Site settings > Advanced settings.  12: In English - United State language, add a new page (ie. """"Reserved page""""). Set page permission to """"Registerd Users"""".  13: In second language, translate (ie. """"Pagina riservata) and publish the page added in step 12.  14: Switch to the home page of second language and try to access the page added in step 13 (ie. /it-it/Pagina-riservata) as a not logged in user.    Actual result:  Redirection to the home page of the default language (ie. /en-us)    Expected result:  Redirection to the login page of the current laguage (ie. /it-it/login?returnurl=/it-it/Pagina-riservata)    Central Jira Link: https://jira.devfactory.com/browse/DNN-17938"""

summary: "KO Validation JS Library and Use Javascript Libraries CDN setting"
description: """The issue is the logic check with using the 'Use Javascript Libraries CDN' setting in Host Settings.  When using the ko.validation library with this feature, it double injects the script tags and validates twice.  Looking through the source code, this appears this is the problem:    {code:C#}  public override void RenderControl(HtmlTextWriter writer)          {              writer.AddAttribute(HtmlTextWriterAttribute.Type, """"text/javascript"""");              writer.RenderBeginTag(HtmlTextWriterTag.Script);                if (ObjectName.Contains("""".""""))              {                  //generate function check                  writer.Write(""""if (typeof """" + ObjectName + """" != 'function') {"""");              }              else              {                  //generate object check                  writer.Write(""""if (typeof """" + ObjectName + """" == 'undefined') {"""");              }                writer.Write(""""document.write('<script src=\"""""""" + FileName + """"\"""" type=\""""text/javascript\""""></' + 'script>');"""");              writer.Write(""""}"""");              writer.RenderEndTag();          }  {code}    The ko.validation object contains a """"."""" so the check ends up seeing if it is a function, when it is really an object.  The check fails and then injects the script tag again causing double validation.    We would like to use this feature, but we depend on knockout and ko.validation.  Without changing the source code or the ko.validation library this cannot be resolved.  """
comments: "Reviewing the documentation:    Specifically, the library will indicate its preferred location (from page head, body top, and body bottom), and can provide a URL to the script on a CDN (*along with a JavaScript expression to use to verify that the CDN loaded the script correctly,* so that DNN can fallback to the local version if the CDN is down)    I could not find any way to implement a Javascript expression to use the verify that the CDN is loaded.  There are no options in the Javascript Library edit window, and I didn't see anything that might be pulled from the zip file on install.  Let me know if I missing something."

summary: "Subresource Integrity (SRI) support for loading JS/CSS via CDF"
description: """Allow developers to specify the `integrity`attribute when loading CSS or Javascript files via the CDF.    [MDN says|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Subresource_Integrity]:  {quote}Subresource Integrity (SRI) is a security feature that enables browsers to verify that files they fetch (for example, from a CDN) are delivered without unexpected manipulation. It works by allowing you to provide a cryptographic hash that a fetched file must match.{quote}    The need for this was highlighted by when a major provider of accessiblilty scripts was compromised, meaning all of the customers (including US goverment sites) ended up with an unwanted crypto-miner on their sites. See https://www.troyhunt.com/the-javascript-supply-chain-paradox-sri-csp-and-trust-in-third-party-libraries/    A good example in the wild is loading Bootstrap 4.x from the official CDN. The example code is:    {code:html}  <link rel=""""stylesheet"""" href=""""https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/bootstrap/4.0.0/css/bootstrap.min.css"""" integrity=""""sha384-Gn5384xqQ1aoWXA+058RXPxPg6fy4IWvTNh0E263XmFcJlSAwiGgFAW/dAiS6JXm"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous"""">  <script src=""""https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.2.1.slim.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-KJ3o2DKtIkvYIK3UENzmM7KCkRr/rE9/Qpg6aAZGJwFDMVNA/GpGFF93hXpG5KkN"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>  <script src=""""https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/popper.js/1.12.9/umd/popper.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-ApNbgh9B+Y1QKtv3Rn7W3mgPxhU9K/ScQsAP7hUibX39j7fakFPskvXusvfa0b4Q"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>  <script src=""""https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/bootstrap/4.0.0/js/bootstrap.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-JZR6Spejh4U02d8jOt6vLEHfe/JQGiRRSQQxSfFWpi1MquVdAyjUar5+76PVCmYl"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>{code}    Note the `integrity` attributes on each of the `link` and `script` elements.    See also: [The W3C specification|https://www.w3.org/TR/SRI/]."""
comments: "I think this can be done as part of 9.2 using the {{HtmlAttributesAsString}} attribute."

summary: "Subresource Integrity (SRI) support for loading JS/CSS via CDF"
description: """Allow developers to specify the crossorigin attribute when loading <script> elements using the CDF.    When applied to <script> elements being loaded from a different origin, the crossorigin attribute allows for easier debugging:    {quote}Normal script elements pass minimal information to the window.onerror for scripts which do not pass the standard CORS checks. To allow error logging for sites which use a separate domain for static media, use this attribute. See CORS settings attributes for a more descriptive explanation of its valid arguments.  {quote}    When applied to <link> elements, it indicates whether CORS must be used when fetching the related resource.    See:   * https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/script#attr-crossorigin  * https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/link#attr-crossorigin   * https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/CORS_settings_attributes    A good example in the wild is loading Bootstrap 4.x from the official CDN. The example code is:    {code:html}  <link rel=""""stylesheet"""" href=""""https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/bootstrap/4.0.0/css/bootstrap.min.css"""" integrity=""""sha384-Gn5384xqQ1aoWXA+058RXPxPg6fy4IWvTNh0E263XmFcJlSAwiGgFAW/dAiS6JXm"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous"""">  <script src=""""https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.2.1.slim.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-KJ3o2DKtIkvYIK3UENzmM7KCkRr/rE9/Qpg6aAZGJwFDMVNA/GpGFF93hXpG5KkN"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>  <script src=""""https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/popper.js/1.12.9/umd/popper.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-ApNbgh9B+Y1QKtv3Rn7W3mgPxhU9K/ScQsAP7hUibX39j7fakFPskvXusvfa0b4Q"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>  <script src=""""https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/bootstrap/4.0.0/js/bootstrap.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-JZR6Spejh4U02d8jOt6vLEHfe/JQGiRRSQQxSfFWpi1MquVdAyjUar5+76PVCmYl"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>  {code}"""
comments: "I think this can be done as part of 9.2 using the {{HtmlAttributesAsString}} attribute."

summary: "Spaces in manifest file cause module installation errors"
description: """Email from client:    we just discovered a bug in DNN 9.1.1 (Evoq Basic). Upon uploading a module, we got an error message, even though the (almost) same module had been installed without error before:        !image-2018-02-14-09-04-41-072.png|thumbnail!        We compared the faulty module with the error-free version installed earlier and discovered just one minor difference:    The faulty version had spaces in seemingly empty lines (hex: 0D 0A 20 20 20 20 0D 0A), whereas the error-free version had not (hex: 0D 0A 0D 0A):      !image-2018-02-14-09-04-53-274.png|thumbnail!     So obviously, the spaces in lines without XML elements caused the .DNN package XML parser to fail.    Please report this to your developers."""

summary: "Dnn tab sort order always goes to the end on save"
description: """Steps to reproduce.    1. Edit an existing page from Page Setting (note down the sequence of the sort order of that tab)  2. Click Save button  3. The page is re sequence at the end of all the pages.  sort order of the tab is updated as max(sortorder) +2 of the tabs under the same parent    The root cause is that the page is MovePage() is being called even when the old parent id is the same as the new parent id. So the tab's sort order is always updated regardless. Please check the attached for the suggested fixing. !MovePageIfNeeded.png|thumbnail!     Please note that the suggested fixing should be on top of the suggested fixing on the reported issued DNN-10757."""
comments: "this should already been fixed in DNN 9.2.0"

summary: "Use Friendly Title in Search Results"
description: """DNN Platform allows a page to have both a {{Name}} as well as {{Title}}. Likewise, Evoq allows a file to have both a Name as well as Title.    {{Name }}is usually meant for internal notation. The presence of title indicates that user intends to provide a friendly name to the entity.    Currently search results page shows the {{Name}} field, not the actual {{Title}}. The API though returns as a {{Title }}field in JSON, but internally it returns the {{Name }} field.    We need to add a new setting in Search Results page.    * Type: Checkbox  * Label: Show friendly title  * Help: Show the page's title instead of it's internal name  * Default: True    *Internal Implementation*  The {{Title}} field is stored as {{keywords -> title}}. We should check if this is present, then we should use it in place of Name     !image-2018-02-11-14-56-06-845.png|thumbnail!         """

summary: "Installer breaking installation if error in Cleanup"
description: """I regularly get customers that have a failed upgrade because:    Failure ExceptionSchwabenCode.QuickIO.UnmatchedFileSystemEntryTypeException: FileSystemEntryType not matched! at SchwabenCode.QuickIO.QuickIOFile.InternalFileExists(String uncPath) at DotNetNuke.Common.Utilities.FileSystemUtils.DeleteFiles(Array arrPaths) at DotNetNuke.Services.Installer.Installers.CleanupInstaller.ProcessCleanupFile()    The installer should *NEVER* fail the install due to *ANY* error in Cleanup. Cleanup is a non-critical part of installations. You can report them to the user if need be. But to break the entire installation is unhelpful and is a major headache for developers."""

summary: "CROSSORIGIN attribute support for loading JS/CSS via CDF"
description: """Allow developers to specify the crossorigin attribute when loading <script> elements using the CDF.    When applied to <script> elements being loaded from a different origin, the crossorigin attribute allows for easier debugging:    {quote}Normal script elements pass minimal information to the window.onerror for scripts which do not pass the standard CORS checks. To allow error logging for sites which use a separate domain for static media, use this attribute. See CORS settings attributes for a more descriptive explanation of its valid arguments.  {quote}    When applied to <link> elements, it indicates whether CORS must be used when fetching the related resource.    See:   * https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/script#attr-crossorigin  * https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/link#attr-crossorigin   * https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/CORS_settings_attributes    A good example in the wild is loading Bootstrap 4.x from the official CDN. The example code is:    {code:html}  <link rel=""""stylesheet"""" href=""""https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/bootstrap/4.0.0/css/bootstrap.min.css"""" integrity=""""sha384-Gn5384xqQ1aoWXA+058RXPxPg6fy4IWvTNh0E263XmFcJlSAwiGgFAW/dAiS6JXm"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous"""">  <script src=""""https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.2.1.slim.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-KJ3o2DKtIkvYIK3UENzmM7KCkRr/rE9/Qpg6aAZGJwFDMVNA/GpGFF93hXpG5KkN"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>  <script src=""""https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/popper.js/1.12.9/umd/popper.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-ApNbgh9B+Y1QKtv3Rn7W3mgPxhU9K/ScQsAP7hUibX39j7fakFPskvXusvfa0b4Q"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>  <script src=""""https://maxcdn.bootstrapcdn.com/bootstrap/4.0.0/js/bootstrap.min.js"""" integrity=""""sha384-JZR6Spejh4U02d8jOt6vLEHfe/JQGiRRSQQxSfFWpi1MquVdAyjUar5+76PVCmYl"""" crossorigin=""""anonymous""""></script>  {code}"""
comments: "I think this can be done as part of 9.2 using the {{HtmlAttributesAsString}} attribute."

(Scenario 1 & 2 in DNN-10736) >>>Reported as a separate issue DNN-10750    *+Expected:+*  - To match the behavior for 9.2.0 with that for 9.1.1. summary: "DEFER and ASYNC Options for Loading JS/CSS via CDF"
description: """From [~accountid:<USER>    Currently, there is no way for a theme or module developer to avoid render-blocking JS and CSS using the CDF.  The option to specify DEFER or ASYNC would allow the developer to utilize these features.  Most of the time, the reasoning for needing this is to optimize performance and improve Google PageSpeed Insights scores.  Right now, Google PSI dings DNN sites heavily due to WebResource.axd, ScriptResource.axd and DependencyHandler.axd."""

description: """If a module contains an empty cleanup list, the installer tries to delete all existing files.  Observed by a user (see https://www.facebook.com/groups/dnnconnect/permalink/1284668921679745) using https://github.com/weggetor/BBImageStory/releases/download/v1.0.0/BBImageStory_01.00.00_Install.zip package, which contains two cleanup lists, containing comments only.  (reporting on behalf of the FB user, who is unable to post here).    Steps to reproduce:  1. install DNN Platform 9.1.1  2. download and install the module mentioned above.  Expected:  module get installed  Actual result:  necessary platform files get deleted.  """
comments: "After more investigation, I found this issue already handled in DNN-10117 and fixed in 9.2.0.    I also tried with 8x to see why the issue not exist. it caused by the cleanup file contains many linux format new line char(\n), but not windows standard(\r\n), in 8x it will not recognize the linux format char so treat them as one single line and check it.    but in 9x we fixed that issue and recognize linux newline char, so the cleanup file actually show as:   !2018-01-29_114047.png|width=800!     and there have some lines with char ""//"" which means delete all files in root level, and that cause the problem. so in DNN-10117, we prevent this scenario to avoid delete whole site. this item can close as ""Duplicate""." [SEP] "IMO, an extension should not be allowed to delete any DNN Core file. AFAIK the installer keeps a list of files per package and delete should be restricted to these files or specific folders (Ideally /desktopmodules/vendorname and /provider/vendorname)" [SEP] "Tested in v. 09.01.01 (129)-235

summary: "SI: Messaging Dispatch: Pass Portal Admin's Display Name along with email address"
description: """Currently whenever a message/notification is sent through the messaging dispatch, the from address is only composed of the portal e-mail address, while the sender address is a mix of the """"Display Name"""" of the user who triggered the notification/message + the portal e-mail address.     This results on some confusing names for e-mails coming from the website since password resets and other tasks will have the Portal administrator's display name + portal's email in the from address so the proper name is displayed on the headers; while on the other e-mails only the e-mail address for the Portal is displayed.    To reproduce:    1. Request a password reset and look at the e-mail headers. From / Sender - both of these will have name + e-mail.    2. Trigger a notification message and review the headers. From only has the e-mail, while sender has both the name + e-mail."""

From only has the e-mail, while sender has both the name + e-mail."

summary: "SI: Error parsing dates on 'ar-sa' culture"
description: """A number of issues occur when using dates on the 'ar-sa' locale. For example, search results do not return, and module settings cannot be opened.    To reproduce:    1. Enable localization on the site.  2. Add the Arabic(Saudi Arabia) 'ar-sa' locale and publish it.  3. Go to a page in the 'ar-sa' locale and edit mode.  4. Attempt to open the module settings for any module.  *Expected Result:*    The settings modal should load without any issues and the time displayed on created/last modified is not parsed at all, it displays the date in the Gregorian Calendar.    *Actual Result:*    The time is parsed with the 'ar-sa' culture which translates the date to the Hijri Calendar where the current year is 1439 and this triggers an error since it is out of range for the Gregorian Calendar (1900-2077)    *The following error is thrown:*      """

summary: "Users/ Prompt: Manipulating host users in a site other than the one it is originally created on, causes some faulty behaviors"
description: """*+Steps:+*  # For some platform installation (DNN_Platform_9.2.0.323-661).  # Login as host.  # From PB, Go Manage, sites.  # Add new child site (site2) (Will get ID=1) with type: Domain (yourMainURL/site2)  # Login to the child site, add new user; host4. (has ID= 1006 in my case)  # Click on the more icon on the users list for that new user  # Make that new user a superUser.  # Get back to the *main site *and login.  # From PB, go to Manage users.  # Notice the users list not containing the host4 user.  # Change the users filter to super users. >> You'll find host4 user there.  # From PB, go to settings, Prompt.  # Enter list-users  # Press Enter >> you'll not find the host4 user in the list.  # Try to type """"get-user 1006"""" (the host4 userID).   # Try to delete it """"delete-user 1006"""" >> Will get-deleted  # Try to restore it """"restore-user 1006"""" >> Will error however, user will actually get restored.    *+Result:+*  - You'll get your user data because hosts are shared across all portals.    *+Issue:+*  1- According to the following link, host users have access to all portals.  http://www.dnnsoftware.com/docs/administrators/user-accounts/create-host-account.html.  However, they are not listed in the authorized users list or portals other the one it is created on And they don't get in the """"list-users"""" command, yet they can be manipulated through other commands.  2-Restoring host that is originally created from anther site through error but actually gets restored    *Expected:*  - This is not correct behavior, I think remote hosts should be listed under the authorized list and should get retrieved under the """"list-users"""" command, for portals other than the creator sites. Unless of course there is some limitation or prior intention to make that behavior.  - To fix the restore issue.  https://www.screencast.com/t/KvQDFUuj5SVm   !image-2018-01-19-12-20-23-564.png|thumbnail!     """

summary: "Prompt: Cross-portal scripting: ""set-page"", ""delete-page"", ""restore-page"", ""purge-page"", ""get-page"" are working regardless of the CrossPortalId specified as parameter"
description: """*+Steps:+*  # For some platform installation (DNN_Platform_9.2.0.323-661).  # Login as host.  # From PB, Go Manage, sites.  # Add new child site (site2) (Will get ID=1) with type Domain (yourMainURL/site2)  # Login to the child site, add new page  (In my case it has ID=52).  # Use fiddler  #  Pass the url of you main site +""""/API/PersonaBar/Command/Cmd/0"""" as POST (This targets scripting over the main site with id=0)  # Add all required headers (From the main site's instance)  # Pass this in the request's body: {""""cmdLine=delete-page 52""""}  # Execute the request.  # Repeat with set-page, delete-page, restore-page, purge-page, get-page commands.    *+Result:+*  - You'll get in the body of your request that the your action is done successfully.    *+Expected:+*  - Targetting site2 in the crossPortalID should allow these actions on that targeted sites not all users in all sites (Unless they are in a site group), so I am suggesting validation on that.   If the sites are in a site group this is when they could share users.   !image-2018-01-19-11-14-30-317.png|thumbnail!   """
comments: "[~accountid:<USER>  All api calls passing cross-portal parameter have been fixed in DNN-10709, for PROMPT. However, if you use direct console of PROMPT or fiddler(without passing crossportalId parameter) for cross-portal commands for pages like set, restore, delete etc, then you would succeed in cross-portal page's operations. Either make change in this ticket  or (if not) create a new one.  In short, if one passes crossportalid then it will fails, (fixed in DNN-10709) which is correct behavior." [SEP] "Using Evoq_Content_9.2.0.137-1007  Having 3 sites:   - Main site, Id=0, (has page with id 22) (In site group1)  - Site 2, Id= 1, (In site group1)  - Site3, Id =2 (Not in site group)    Crossporatl Id=2 & targetting page in site 0 action got completed (restore-page).   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   This fix needs to be revisited, we are going to with the rule that only modules and users are going to be shared in case of site groups.  Site groups only affect the resulting data"

summary: "Prompt: Cross-portal scripting: Modules commands work regardless the supplied portalID and moves/copies modules even while they are not in a site group"
description: """*+Steps:+*  # For some platform installation (DNN_Platform_9.2.0.323-661).  # Login as host.  # Add new page with new Module on it (Module ID=416, pageid=55 in this e.g and exist on the main site only).  # From PB, Go Manage, sites.  # Add new child site (site2) (Will get ID=1) with type Domain (yourMainURL/site2)  # Use fiddler  #  Pass the url of you main site +""""/API/PersonaBar/Command/Cmd/1"""" as POST (This targets scripting over the site with id=1)  # Add all required headers (From the main site's instance)  # Pass this in the request's body: {""""cmdLine"""":""""get-module 416 --pageid 55 """"} (Note that the portal parameter refers to the child site, while this module exist on the main site)  # Execute the request.  # Repeat for module commands and try copying and moving from site to another: copy-module, delete-module, restore-module, purge-module, move-module, get-module    *+Result:+*  - You'll be able to get modules on main site while on site 2 and vise versa.  - And you'll be able to copy and move modules from one site to the other while these sites are not inside a site group.  - However, list-modules command only lists modules inside the target portal only correctly.    *+Expected:+*  - Modules should be accessible to all sites only if they are inside same site group, otherwise each portal should handle only modules of its own.  - Please, fix the module commands: copy-module, delete-module, restore-module, purge-module, move-module, get-module.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   """

summary: "Community PR for cross site prompt "
description: """*Background*:  After DNN-10583 """"Pormpt needs to be allow hosts to script across portals""""  we start to see some observations that might be issue in the prompt implementation, not the above ticket - so created this story to log and track the history and investigation notes by development team     # By general cross portal - utilize the current child site implementation and had the same limitation that (roles - users ) are not cross site accessible   # For prompt cross portal please check the above ticket"""
comments: "+The logic that we are adopting in the needed fixes:+    *Prompt* is a feature the allow command (cmd) line access for the application's functionality.  - Thus what you see in the prompt cmd *result *should match what you get from the application normal functionalities done using the the UI.    *Prompt cross portal access* is a way to execute the usual prompt commands but with targeting a site other than the one in the URL, for e.g. ""Mainsite/API"".  - Thus the *result *will be similar to executing the prompt command on that remote targeted site.  - Targeting a site to execute commands on is done by adding ""/siteId"" at the end of the API call, for e.g. : ""Mainsite/API/ 2"".    *Site-Group* is a feature in DNN that allows specific sites ,which exist in a specific site group, to share users & modules.  - This should affect the *result * of running commands inside the *basic prompt* to extend the allowed accessible items to be retrieved or manipulated only for features affected by siteGroups (Users & module

summary: ".NET Foundation On-Boarding"
description: """h2. On-Board DNN Platform project to the .NET Foundation We're getting ready to bring the project into the .NET Foundation, but before we do we need to do the following: h2. Getting into the .NET Foundation - (/) Make sure the project has a good name that is easy to remember and spell. Check that it doesn't [conflict with another existing project|http://ivantomic.com/projects/ospnc/] - (/) Make sure the people involved in the project want to join the .NET Foundation - (/) Make sure the project has a clear understanding about how it accepts contributions and the process it follows when selecting new committers to the project. - (/) [Register an interest|https://dotnetfoundation.org/get-involved] in joining the .NET Foundation - (/) Work with the .NET Foundation to fill out an On-Boarding Questionaire - (/) .NET Foundation sends out completed questionaire to .NET Foundation Advisory Council for comments - (/) .NET Foundation Executive Director submits new project proposal to the .NET Foundation Board - (/) Configure a CI build for the project and ensure build status badges are available from the README h2. Once accepted into the .NET Foundation - (/) Sign the contribution / assignment agreement - (/) Agree a date to move into the .NET Foundation - (/) Prepare a guest blog post announcing the move on the .NET Foundation Blog - (/) Read the [code of conduct|https://dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct], [link to it in your code|https://github.com/dotnet/home/tree/master/guidance/be-nice.md] and understand what to do if you are concerned about any behaviour or have concerns reported to you. - (/) Tell the world we have joined! - (?) Get CLA Automation enabled to ensure contributors can easily sign the [Contribution License Agreement|https://cla2.dotnetfoundation.org/cladoc/net-foundation-contribution-license-agreement.pdf] - (/) Send a PR to add the Project into the .NET Foundation [list|https://github.com/dotnet/home/tree/master/projects] - (/) Ensure the repo contents are up to date with [.NET Foundation guidance|https://github.com/dotnet/home/blob/master/guidance/repo-guide.md] - (/) Review the [README guidance|https://github.com/dotnet/home/tree/master/guidance/readme-guide.md] and update if necessary - (/) If applicable [update the LICENSE file|https://github.com/dotnet/home/tree/master/guidance/copyright.md] to show Copyright has been assigned to the .NET Foundation -and look to update any file headers.- - (?) If applicable, update any copyright statements in websites owned by the project to reflect assignment to the .NET Foundation - (?) If applicable, update any websites associated with the project to include """"Supported by the .NET Foundation"""" or similar link back to the .NET Foundation in the footer. If applicable, please include image links from [our swag repo|http://github.com/dotnet/swag]. (We think a link in the footer using the horizontal logo looks very nice.) - (?) [Sign up for Project Leader news|http://eepurl.com/bOCfJP] - (?) Configure any resources requested from the .NET Foundation (SSL Certs, Code Signing, Secret Management, Build Servers etc) *Note:* completed items are marked with (/) while the remaining ones are marked with (?). """

summary: "PortalSettings.UserMode injects dnnEditState unnecessary "
description: """When the site settings switch: """"Control Panel Mode"""" (aka PortalSettings.UserMode) is set to """"EDIT"""", the class: """"dnnEditState"""" is injected in the body tag for ALL registered users, even though the user has absolutely no edit rights.  This is unexpected behavior and can lead to the problematic situation in sites upgraded to DNN9.x because the setting is not avalaible anymore. So my suggestion would be to add an update script to 9.2.x to set the default value for PortalSettings.UserMode to """"VIEW"""".    update script:    update {databaseOwner}{objectQualifier}PortalSettings set SettingValue='VIEW' where SettingName='ControlPanelMode'    """

summary: "Improve performance of GetAvailableUsersForIndex stored procedure"
description: """While working on sites with large number of users, I noticed the site indexing scheduled task often fails due to timing out while executing the *GetAvailableUsersForIndex* stored procedure. This procedure needs optimization. Also the batch size used in the *UserIndexer.cs* file is constant and fixed at 250. This should be enhanced to be overridden with an optional host or scheduler setting entry (i.e., if the entry exists in the tables, it overrides whatever this constant is)."""

summary: "PersonaBar - Scheduler returns the same NextStart date"
description: """*Steps:*  # Login as host to DNN Platform _v. 09.01.01 (129)_.  # From Persona Bar, go to Scheduler.  # Check the history of some task.    *Result:*  Some history records have the same _NextStart_ date, ajax call to the Api _/API/PersonaBar/TaskScheduler/GetScheduleItemHistory?scheduleId=<ID>&pageIndex=0&pageSize=50_ returns wrong _NextStart_. See attached screenshots.    *Expected:*  _NextStart_ dates should be different.    *Note:*  I suppose the issue in _Dnn.Platform\DNN Platform\Library\Services\Scheduling\ScheduleItem.cs_    in    {code:c#}  public DateTime NextStart  {      get      {          if (!_NextStart.HasValue)          {              _NextStart = MinNextTime;          }          return _NextStart.Value > MinNextTime ? _NextStart.Value : MinNextTime;      }      set      {          _NextStart = value;      }  }  """
comments: "Hello [~accountid:<USER>, thank you for you submission. The object displayed on this screen is actually *{{DotNetNuke.Services.Scheduling.ScheduleHistoryItem}}* and turns out that the *{{NextStart}}* property is never hydrated when loaded from the database.    You are right to notice that it actually is included in the dataset coming from the stored procedure but IMO it should be ignored on the UI. After all this is just a historical view.    I propose to show it only for the most recent execution, if at all, and hide for all previous runs. "

summary: "SI: Module Settings - Publish Date: Allow Roles with Edit Rights to view module"
description: """Currently, if a specific role has access to view/edit/manage settings for a module then the role would be able to see the module during its publication date. Nevertheless, if the Start date is in the future or if the end date has already passed (in other words if the module is no longer published), then the module would not be visible for the role with edits right and will not be able to work on it.    The use case where this feature is needed is when one has a custom role managing important information/announcement banners which are not always visible; and the users in this role should have the rights to edit and publish these modules. However, once the publish date does not match the ranges between start and end dates, users in that role are no longer able to view nor edit the module."""

summary: "PB Users search: search fields in addition to Display Name"
description: """When performing a search of users, it seems that only the Display Name property of the user is searched, and users are only returned if the search text matches the beginning of the Display Name.    It is often required to be able to search by username, email address, or by text that is not at the beginning of the display name (e.g. last name).    Consider expanding the search functionality to cover these use cases."""
comments: "Search in DNN 9.2 will search the following user fields:  * username  * displayname  * first name  * last name  * email address  all search will be performed from the left upon typing, I see really little benefit in searching in the middle of a field - this will list numerous unwanted results in most cases and will always slow down search significantly."

summary: "PB Users: Newly authorized users not shown on Authorized list for some time"
description: """If an administrator sets an unauthorized user to authorized and then does a search for that user under the authorized list, (i.e. by changing the “SHOW:” field to “authorized”) that user is not located.  In order to see the newly authorized user the administrator must change the “SHOW:” field to “All” and then the search function will locate the newly authorized user.    To recreate the scenario do the following:  # Logon to any site as Administrator or Super-Admin  # Go to Manage > Users  # Change “SHOW:” field to UNAUTHORIZED to list all unauthorized users  # Select one and change that user to be “authorized”  # System responds with appropriate “User has been authorized” message  # Change “SHOW:” field to AUTHORIZED to list all authorized users  # Enter the authorized user’s name in the “Search Users” field  # Issue:  System will NOT locate the newly authorized user  # Change the “SHOW:” field to “all” and then Enter the authorized user’s name in the “Search Users” field  # Result: the system will locate the newly authorized user-id.    Note: it has been noticed that after a few minutes (5?), the newly authorized user does become locatable under the Authorized users list.  """
comments: "[~accountid:<USER> It seems to be performance issue, currently for 4-5 users, this problem is not producible for 9.1.1 & 9.2.0" [SEP] "this is caused in DNN 9.1.1 by user search being based on Lucene search index, i.e. users need to be indexed first.  DNN 9.2.0 will return to a database search, which shall solve this issue." [SEP] "Thx guys! Moved jira to QA" [SEP] "Verified and CNR this in current 9.2.0 build"

Each user is listed twice, one under the other  """

summary: "No view permission to Admin entries added to Persona Bar via module manifest"
description: """DNN can actually automatically create a Persona Bar entry with just a few lines of metadata added to the .dnn manifest. These lines go into the <desktopModule> node of the module that needs to be added to the admin page:    <desktopModule>      ...      <page type=""""Admin"""" common=""""true"""">          <name>Configuration Manager</name>          <icon>~/Icons/Sigma/Configuration_16X16_Standard.png</icon>          <largeIcon>~/Icons/Sigma/Configuration_32X32_Standard.png</largeIcon>          <description>Modify configuration settings for your site</description>      </page>      ....  </desktopModule>    (more details [here|http://www.dnnsoftware.com/community-blog/cid/155470/custom-admin-entries-in-persona-bar-the-easy-way] and [here|https://dnntracker.atlassian.net/browse/DNN-7485])    However, under DNN 9.1.1, menu entries created this way are only visible to Super Users. As the menu entries are added under """"Admin"""", the expectation is that users in the Adminstrators role can see the menu entries. Also, the module installation creates rows in the PersonaBarMenuDefaultPermissions for the menu entry with RoleNames = 'Administrators', which also seems to indicate this is intended.    If the following script is run, users in the Adminstrators role can see the menu entries:          """

summary: "Member Directory: Filter By Group not working"
description: """This is a fairly trivial issue from code mechanics point of view but its a show stopper for us.    Root of the problem lies when you configure Member Directory module to filter users/members by Group. And you select an appropriate group from the list.    That works fine for the group you've selected. But as soon as you try to view a list of members of any other group you get a list of members for the group you initially configured because thats the group reference that is stored as [FilterValue] in [ModuleSettings] table.    OK, I won't go into an argument that there is supposed to be logic to look for query string parameter [groupid] which then used to filter out users because that is true. But there lies our problem.    MemberDirectoryController.cs line 126      Can someone correct this function so I won't have to recompile this module every time.     All that needs to be adjusted is an argument for <int filterRoleId> from <Int32.Parse(filterValue)> to <groupId> in the call to <UserController.Instance.GetUsersAdvancedSearch()> and everyone becomes happy.    I am not sure if this affects earlier versions of DNN (prior to v7.2.2) as I was lazy to check. But I can confirm this issue to be persistent between 7.2.2 right up to 7.3.3."""

summary: "Thread Safe URL Portals (IndexOutOfRangeException)"
description: """Fix to prevent the application from returning 500 errors due to the non-thread safe object being manipulated by multiple threads.    https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1932    *Copied from the PR in github:*  {quote}  I've been unable to link my JIRA account, it seems like something is wrong with the dnntracker self-login process.    Background:    We have a fairly large DNN system with thousands of portals, running in a web farm setup  nBrane is used as a caching provider to distribute the memory cache across servers  We've run into an issue where periodically our websites will start serving 500 errors on a server, because what appears to be invalid cache objects.    Attached is a stack trace of what we would typically see. Note: The originating source of this stack trace has many different variations.        There seem to be a few StackOverflow threads about this problem. The following one seems to express it the best: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/43915717/index-out-of-bounds-on-list-contains    I'll continue to attempt to get a proper JIRA ticket filed for this issue, as well as working on testing it on a larger scale.    This change was forked and tested off of 8.0.1, but is valid for all versions.  {quote}"""

Also, performed basic browsing JMeter tests for multiple site pages for a copy of dnnsoftware.com site and verified there is no degradation due to the new changes as shown in the attached report  [^DNN-10596 Performance.docx] .      QA testers should test that they don't see the errors described in the github comments after applying the fix. This might be a tough task though, as they need to test using concurrent requests to the site through a tool such as JMeter." description: """*Scenario 1*  Enter fullstop (.) to validate as page name.  Result: It says page name already exists. Which is not the case.  Expected result: Produce proper validation message.  ------------------------------------------------------------------------  *Scenario 2*  Enter same page name thrice to validate.  Result: Validation message passes 3 pages. But when press Add page button; only one page is getting added.   Expected result: Validation button should detect same name pages entered thrice and pass correct message.  ------------------------------------------------------------------------  *Scenario 3*  Enter prepend symbol (>) to validate as page name. Result: Validation says only one page gets validated. And creates a page with the paragraph as the title.  Expected Result: Each line has to be considered a page name.    """

summary: "Export_GetAllUsers doesn't cover all changes"
description: """Stored Procedure Export_GetAllUsers has a logical twist:  - it first finds all records from users, which have been modified within a time span, specified by parameters, and next, for just these users, it returns all modified records from userportals, user profile, user authentication, userroles and aspnet membership tables. This will miss modified records, which have been updated just in these tables (without updating LastModifiedOnDate in users table)"""

summary: "Export_GetAllUsers doesn't provide correct record for servers in some time zones"
description: """the formula used to calculate time from timeutc does not support timezone with fractional hours offset (Iran Standard Time, Afghanistan Time, Chatham Island Standard Time, Cocos Island Time, Newfoundland Standard Time, ...).    Lines should be:   DECLARE @ToDate   DateTime = DATEADD (n, DATEDIFF(n, GETUTCDATE(), GETDATE()), @ToDateUtc  )   DECLARE @FromDate DateTime = DATEADD (n, DATEDIFF(n, GETUTCDATE(), GETDATE()), @FromDateUtc)"""
comments: "better:   DECLARE @ToDate   DateTime = DATEADD (n, DATEDIFF(n, GETUTCDATE(), GETDATE()), IsNull(@ToDateUtc,   0))   DECLARE @FromDate DateTime = DATEADD (n, DATEDIFF(n, GETUTCDATE(), GETDATE()), ISNull(@FromDateUtc, 0))  "

summary: "404 Errors not working properly."
description: """I am currently running DNN version 08.00.03 in a production environment.    I recently noticed that my 404 page not found errors no longer work the way they should. If the page requested is a child page, then instead of throwing the 404 error DNN just bubbles the navigation to the parent page.    I don't know if this is related or not but I noticed it started acting like this after I changed my config file to handle the """"advanced"""" url re-write.    I have a clean unmolested install of dnndev.me (version 9.1.1) on my local machine and the same process is occurring. Any 404 error on child pages get sent to the parent page instead of showing the 404 page. Only the main menu items (parent pages) will actually throw a 404 error."""
comments: "So here is an example.     If I type in ""http://www.dnndev.me/Test-Page/Test-404ss"" as a bad url   I get re-directed to ""http://www.dnndev.me/Test-Page"" but the url remains ""http://www.dnndev.me/Test-Page/Test-404ss""  " [SEP] "Even the current DNN website has this same issue.  Go to or any other page.  http://www.dnnsoftware.com/community/participate/community-showcase  Add some bogus stuff at the end of the page like this:  http://www.dnnsoftware.com/community/participate/community-showcaseblah  and the website ends up taking you here:  http://www.dnnsoftware.com/community/participate  instead of giving you a 404 Error. Plus the url is left as:  http://www.dnnsoftware.com/community/participate/community-showcaseblah      " [SEP] "This is a known issue, and the intended functionality of AUM. More information here:    http://www.ifinity.com.au/Products/KB/Url_Master?topic=Why+don%27t+non-existent+child+pages+return+404"

summary: "CAN'T SELECT PAGES IN PARENT PAGE DROP DOWN SELECTION"
description: """Dear All,    There seems to be a bug with the Parent Page drop down selection where you can't select a page unless it has the """"VIEW"""" permission enabled for """"all users"""" (single page creation only, page can be selected fine in multiple page creation).  We have found some people are reporting on the same issue in the DNN website as below:    http://www.dnnsoftware.com/answers/cant-select-pages-in-parent-page-drop-down-selection      On single page creation, the roles parameter values passed in as  {1,-1} no mater what the user roles are. Therefore, the FilterTabsByRoles method in the TabsController [Dnn.PersonaBar.Library.Controllers] will not return the tabs that does not match these roles that been called by GetPortalTabs method.   https://local.mantrahotels.com/API/PersonaBar/Tabs/GetPortalTabs?portalId=-2&cultureCode=&isMultiLanguage=false&excludeAdminTabs=false&disabledNotSelectable=false&roles=1%3B-1&sortOrder=0&selectedTabId=-1    On the other hand, the multiple page creation does not pass the roles parameter values and hence it will returns all the tabs except  disabledNotSelectable.   https://local.mantrahotels.com/API/PersonaBar/Tabs/GetPortalTabs?portalId=-2&cultureCode=&isMultiLanguage=false&excludeAdminTabs=false&disabledNotSelectable=false&roles=&sortOrder=0&selectedTabId=-1"""
comments: "Marked this one as duplicate since DNN-10620 was logged as SI issue." [SEP] "Closing this as duplicate"

summary: "Pormpt needs to be allow hosts to script across portals"
description: """Currently Prompt will run all commands that are portal specific (e.g. roles) at the current portal. Given that we want to support remote scripting and given that hosts can transcend portals, this should be opened up so that commands can be run across different portals.    The provided solution adds portal ID to the Prompt WebAPI path. This way we avoid adding another parameter to the commands and it will only be used by those that implement it (the Personabar extension doesn't need it and will be left unchanged in behavior).    https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.AdminExperience.Extensions/pull/371"""
comments: "I reviewed and tested that the new changes can be applied to many portals, then merged it.    For QA testers: you can test by sending API requests to different portal (using Fiddler or Postman) by appending the portal ID to the end of the normal Prompt API call as there is no UI to test this with. For example:  {color}POST /API/PersonaBar/Command/Cmd/1{color}  where the *1* is the portal ID to target that is different than the currently active portal." [SEP] "Hi [~accountid:<USER>, this introduced a breaking change ( DNN-10583 ),  we need to reintroduce ""List"" method." [SEP] "I'm following up on this." [SEP] "https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.AdminExperience.Extensions/pull/383" [SEP] "I validated a few of the PROMPT commands and found that some don't work as expected across portals. After further investigation, I figured out that it is a bug in the implementation of these commands themselves (how they call the methods down the stream or the libraries the commands use) and not a bug in the implementation

summary: "SI: Advanced Editor Modal Can Break Edit Interface"
description: """If you click outside the advanced editor modal, it can hide the persona bar and edit bar for the rest of your session.    Steps to reproduce:    1. Create a new page  2. add an HTML module to the page  3. add some content and publish  4. switch back into edit mode  5. Click the HTML content to edit it, then click Advanced Editor  6. Click OUTSIDE the html editor modal, in the dark grey mask area  7. Click the X to close the popup    Your edit bar and persona bar will be gone until you open the site in a new browser tab.    Note this does not occur if the page is in the middle of a workflow."""

summary: "Update Knockout version from 3.3.2 to 3.4.2"
description: """DNN's current version of knockout in Resources/shared/scripts is version 3.3.2.    The deferred loading option is unavailable until 3.4(ko.options.deferUpdates = true;).  Latest version is 3.4.2.    Updating DNN's version to the latest may allow for possible performance benefits from it's implementation without the use of any plugins.    Many other fixes as well. Per the release docs there appears to be no breaking changes from current version.  3.4 documents possible compatability issues.    I am currently testing 2 installs with the updated versions of knockout. I've noticed no issues yet that would suggest compatability problems.  It would be great to know if someone is already aware of any.    http://knockoutjs.com/documentation/deferred-updates.html    https://github.com/knockout/knockout/releases"""

summary: "PB - Users: Incorrect data display on value selection from ""Show"" dropdown"
description: """+*Issue1: Select """"All"""" filter - Steps to Reproduce:*+  1) Go to PB  2) Manage -> Users -> Select """"All"""" value from """"Show"""" dropdown  3) Superusers are not getting displayed   *Expected Result:* Superusers should also be displayed under """"All"""" filter  *Actual Result:* Superusers are not displayed under """"All"""" filter  +*Issue 2: Authorize or UnAuthorize users under """"All"""" filter  - Steps to Reproduce:*+  1) Go to PB  2) Manage -> Users -> Select """"All"""" value from """"Show"""" dropdown  3) Make unauthorized user as Authorized by clicking """"Authorize User"""" or authorized user as Unauthorized by clicking """"Un-Authorize User""""  *Expected Result:* The respective authorized or unauthorized user should be displayed immediately on conversion  *Actual Result:* Only on Page refresh, the respective authorized or unauthorized user is displayed*  +*Issue 3: Make Superuser under """"Authorized"""" or """"Unauthorized"""" filter - Steps to Reproduce:*+  1) Go to PB  2) Manage -> Users -> Select """"Authorized"""" or """"Unauthorized"""" value from """"Show"""" dropdown  3) Click on """"Make Superuser"""" for any of the displayed user  *Expected Result:* 1) Authorized user who is made as Superuser should be displayed under """"Authorized"""", """"Superusers"""" & """"All"""" filters                              2) Unauthorized user who is made as Superuser should be displayed under """"Unauthorized"""", """"Superusers"""" & """"All"""" filters  *Actual Result:*    1) Authorized user who is made as Superuser is displayed under """"Superusers"""" & """"All"""" filters but NOT under """"Authorized"""" filter                             2) Unauthorized user who is made as Superuser is displayed under """"Superusers"""" & """"All"""" filters but NOT under """"Unauthorized"""" filter  +*Issue 4: Delete user under """"Superusers"""" filter  - Steps to Reproduce:*+  1) Go to PB  2) Manage -> Users -> Select """"Superusers"""" value from """"Show"""" dropdown  3) Click on """"Delete User"""" for any of the displayed user  *Expected Result:* Deleted superuser should be displayed under """"Deleted"""" & """"All"""" filters  *Actual Result:* Deleted superuser is NOT displayed under """"Deleted"""" & """"All"""" filters"""
comments: "Superusers are not site users and all superusers should only been displayed when selecting ""superusers"" from the DDL.  They cannot be unauthorized (but deleted).  You could include superusers in authorized (which doesn't make sense), deleted and all users, but  * there is a performance penalty, due to the data structure  * superusers are only displayed to viewers, who are superusers themselves (site admins are NOT allowed to manage superusers), i.e. different users will get displayed different lists." [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> Yeah I agree. Supersusers are not site users and they can be only deleted.    But what I have meant is the authorized user or the unauthorized user who is been made as superusers should be listed under Authorized/ Unauthorized/Superusers/All filters. Hope this makes sense.    For clarity, i have updated my testcases accordingly."

summary: "Installing extension replaces more recent dll with an older version"
description: """Installing an extension which in its manifest includes an older dll will replace version already existing in the application, even if that existing dll has a more recent version.    For example, see the attached libraries  - Evoq.Microservices.Library contains *Evoq.Microservices.Framework.dll* version *10.0.9*  - Evoq.StructuredContent.Library also bundles *Evoq.Microservices.Framework.dll*; version *10.0.6*    These versions are correct in each extension's manifest.    The installation order is enforced by the manifests:  # Evoq.Microservices.Library  # Evoq.StructuredContent.Library    *Expected behavior*    Installing Evoq.StructuredContent.Library should not replace the *Evoq.Microservices.Framework.dll*    *Actual behavior*    When the second extension is installed it overwrites version 1.0.9 with the older 10.0.6.    Here are the results of running the query         !image-2017-12-04-15-54-38-478.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Journal Comment not updated properly in case of more than 2000 characters"
description: """Journal comment data is stored in """"Journal_Comment"""" table. Comments are usually stored in """"comment"""" column in text form, but in case of comments larger than 2000 characters, it's stored in """"commentXml"""" column in an XML form.    The Sql Stored Procedure """"Journal_Comment_Save"""" is used to save the comments/replies to the database.   In case when a comment larger than 2000 characters was modified to a one which is less than 2000 characters, it gets stored in """"comment"""" column but the *older version in """"commentXml"""" column was not deleted*."""

summary: "slow performance of GetUserByPasswordResetToken"
description: """On sites with many users (> 100k), the procedure tends to time out, as it scans all records due to a missing index.  The best solution would be a dedicated table PasswordResetTokens.  As an easier fix, I suggest adding a filtered index on PasswordResetToken  of users table (where passwordResetToken is not Null) and clearing all outdated and used tokens when possible, which keeps the index very small and fast.  Limitation: the system might no longer be able to identify and display, whether a token is just outdated or (not issues or already been used) - but IMO this is not necessary.    I volunteer to provide necessary SQL code, if accepted (should not require API changes)"""

I volunteer to provide necessary SQL (and API) code, if this improvement gets accepted.  """

summary: "Normalize userportal for superusers"
description: """While working on DNN-10425, we noticed performance issues due to missing normalization of Superusers. While each other user has at least one userportal record, superusers sometimes don't have userportal records or not for all portals. This requires a left join from UserPortal table to Users table, which is not useful for performance and a number of other isses, like missing UP properties.    The best solution would be creating userportal records for each superuser in any portal, which need to be completed a) when a superuser is created or b) a new portal is added. the superusers might also be added to RegisteredUsers Role, if they should appear in role member list.  If a user is turned into a superuser or back, no records will need to be adjusted anymore.    I am offering to provide the necessary SQL code, if this ticket gets scheduled for a release.  """

summary: "JavaScript error - Sys.InvalidOperationException: Type Telerik.Web.UI.Orientation has already been registered."
description: """When I load the Admin/File-Management/ page the below java script error will be displayed in the Chrome developer tools console """" Uncaught Error: Sys.InvalidOperationException: Type Telerik.Web.UI.Orientation has already been registered. The type may be defined multiple times or the script file that defines it may have already been loaded """" .    From the investigation, we found that in the  DotNetNuke.Modules.DigitalAssets/View.ascx.cs is code to register the Telerik.Web.UI.Common.Core.js manually via ClientDependency, which then will register Telerik.Web.UI.Orientation for the second time. This should not be required as the script is already included via loading the Telerik controls such as the RadGrid.    You'll need to set  compilation debug=""""true"""" in the web.config to reproduce the error. The JS error is not raised if the website config is set to compile in release mode.     The FileManager seems to function correctly in both debug & release mode when the code to add the reference to Telerik.Web.UI.Common.Core.js, within View.ascx.cs of the DigitalAssets module is removed/commented out.    Suggested resolution is to remove the 2 lines of code from within view.ascx.cs  var telerikCoreJs = Page.ClientScript.GetWebResourceUrl(typeof(RadGrid), """"Telerik.Web.UI.Common.Core.js"""");                  ClientResourceManager.RegisterScript(Page, telerikCoreJs, FileOrder.Js.jQuery + 3);"""

summary: "Webform postback in Edit mode loosing viewstate of the GridView"
description: """When a webform module is submitted in Edit mode, the Gridview used in the module is disappeared. This exactly webform module works fine in DNN7.4.2 in both view and edit mode. Please see the attached simple sample module that we use to test in DNN9.1.1 Vanilla version.   Steps to reproduce,  1. Load the page that has the sample module TestForm in Edit mode  2.The page is loaded and the GridView is displayed with the data  3.Click 'Get Rows' button  4.The page is refreshed and the GridView is gone. No. of Rows is shown as '0' which means that the viewstate of the gridview is lost of not able to retrieve.     If you do those steps and in View mode, it works perfectly. Please see the attached Steps to reproduce.docx.    From the investigation, we found that CreateChildControls() in Dnn.EditBar.UI\Controllers\ContentEditorManager.cs is wrapping all the panes in a new UpdatePanel and this is causing the pane id of the controls are changed. So on the postback event, the viewstate of the web controls associated with the pane is not able to retrieve.     By commenting out the function CreateChildControls(), the webforms postback are working fine. Could you please shed the light what is the purpose behind this?     We can't find any impact from our testing to remove the function CreateChildControls() in ContentEditorManager.     """
comments: "Upon further investigation and stepping into System.Web.dll, we have found the root cause of this issue    * The function GetUpdatePanelsInPane(pane) using in ContentEditorManager.cs is causing the issue.  * The line of codes that cause  the issue is when the all the child controls for each pane is retrieving recursively for each control by  'parent.Controls'. This is basically going through every single controls in the page   * When 'Controls' property is called, the CompositeDataBoundControl like GridView is causing to call EnsureChildControls() and in turns CreateChildControls() will be called. Please find below a piece of code.  * At this stage of Initialization (pls note this is happening in ContentEditorManger.OnInit) , LoadViewSate is not happening yet. So this.ViewState[""_!ItemCount""] is null at that point  namespace System.Web.UI.WebControls  {   /// <summary>Represents the base class for a tabular data-bound control that is composed of other server controls.</summary>    public abstract class Com

summary: "PB General: Unexpected Postbacks occurring when toggling Edit mode."
description: """When a user is in view mode & clicks the Edit button on the Persona bar to enter edit mode the script within the handleEdit function within the file main.js in the Persona Bar scripts folder calls window.parent.location.reload();    This causes the browser to reload the last request that was made. If the last request happens to be a POST request then that POST request will be made again, rather than the page being refreshed using a a GET request, which I believe was the intention of calling reload.     Re-issuing a POST request can have unintended consequences if the POST caused server state to be updated. For example, if the page has a module on with a grid in it and the user clicks delete to delete an item from the grid this could be done via a postback. If the user then subsequently clicked the edit button to enter edit mode the postback would be issued again as though the item was being deleted again, however the item has already been deleted so the next item in the grid would be deleted.    To verify the issue load a module that has controls which call do_postback & reload the same page, a gridview for example. Click on a control to perform the postback, then click the edit button to enter edit mode. If you use Fiddler to inspect the request issued after the edit button was clicked it will be a post with the event target & argument of the control that was clicked before the edit button.    The simple fix is to change calls to location.reload() so that instead the href is updated. E.g replace   window.parent.location.reload();  with  window.top.document.location.href = window.top.document.location.href;    It seems this has already been done in other places within the EditBar scripts so reload is not used."""
comments: "It also happens on 9.2:   !DNN-10522.png|thumbnail! " [SEP] ".reload() replaced by "".replace()"".    PR here: https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.AdminExperience.Library/pull/112    Change will affect 9.2 only, once merged." [SEP] "Looks good. Code merged" [SEP] "Verified on Platform 9.2.0.256 as per steps given in QA test plan. working as expected. "

summary: "Possible conflicts in React version in Common Components"
description: """In Dnn.React.Common, we have something to investigate in *TreeControllerInteractor*.    As you can see, it uses React """"prop-types"""" npm component. It means, this component is made to work with a version of React >= 15.5, as we can read in the note here: [https://reactjs.org/docs/typechecking-with-proptypes.html|http://example.com]    *Problems are:*  * We use React 15.3.x almost everywhere in our projects, included Common Components.  * When we bundle a project referencing this component, we could have problems related to multiple React versions as, for instance, here:  !multi-react-components.png|thumbnail!   * We could have bundles bigger than needed, because of multiple React versions  * Component could not work and generate """"hard-to-understand"""" runtime errors because of wrong React version.    All these points should be verified and investigated and *TreeControllerInteractor* should be fixed.   *Actually we should choose between a downgrade of React in the component or a general upgrade across the projects*.     !tree-control-deps.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Performance issue with PasswordResetToken"
description: """Password reset may time out on sites with many users.  This is caused by GetUserByPasswordResetToken sproc, which has to perform a full clustered index scan.   I suggest adding a dedicated filtered index on PasswordResetToken (Not Null) and set PasswordResetToken = Null (and PasswordResetExpiration = Null) whenever it is timed out or used, to keep the filtered index small."""

summary: "MVC Module - Unable to Redirect from ActionFilter"
description: """This very well may be a feature request, so feel free to change it if it is  --    The MVC `ActionFilterAttribute` does *not* support page redirects by setting the `filterContext.Result` to a new `ActionResult`. This is expected behavior in ASP.NET MVC and since there is no special DNN `ActionFilterAttribute` AFAIK I would expect this behavior to work just like it would in ASP.NET MVC.     Suppose you want to create an Exception Filter that redirects the user to a special error page.      In the case above whenever there is an exception the action should automatically redirect to an ErrorPage() which is a method on the controller that returns a type of `ActionResult`.    This is something I can help solve but I may need someone that I could message that has a little bit more domain knowledge of the MVC Architecture."""
comments: "After further investigation into how our `ModuleApplication` and `ResultCapturingActionInvoker` all work together in returning the `ActionResult` from the Controller I have found a solution that is currently redirecting. This currently works with an `ActionFilter` that implements `IExceptionFilter`.    I am going to continue to work through this and try to get the other `ActionFilters` to properly redirect and then I'll submit a Pull Request" [SEP] "I believe this should be changed to Feature Request instead of bug since this is a feature that appears to have not been implemented. summary: "SI: Oauth Cookies should be deleted / expired when session expires"
description: """Currently if the expiration timeout for a DNN session occurs before an oauth token such as the FacebookUserToken, the user is auto logged in as soon as the login page is loaded since the oauth_token is still alive; nevertheless, the user should be prompted with the login page again as the session should be expired, and one should have the option to select a different login methods.    To reproduce:    1. Enable FB Login.  2. Login with FB  3. Wait for .DOTNETNUKE timeout to expire, do not enable persistentcookie  4. Go to login page.    Actual Result.  User is redirected to home page and auto-logged in with facebook    Expected Result.  The facebookusertoken cookie should be removed once the dotnetnuke cookie / persistent cookie expires."""

summary: "SI: Partial Rendering & Grids Module"
description: """Modules which use partialrendering (Example EasyDNN News) encounter an error when they are added to a page with the DNN Grids module.      SetPartialRenderingMode can only be called once.   InnerStackTrace: at System.Web.UI.UpdatePanel.SetAsyncPostBackMode(Boolean asyncPostBackMode)   at System.Web.UI.PageRequestManager.ProcessUpdatePanels()   at System.Web.UI.PageRequestManager.RenderPageCallback(HtmlTextWriter writer, Control pageControl)   at System.Web.UI.Control.RenderChildrenInternal(HtmlTextWriter writer, ICollection children)   at DotNetNuke.Framework.PageBase.Render(HtmlTextWriter writer)   at DotNetNuke.Framework.DefaultPage.Render(HtmlTextWriter writer)   at System.Web.UI.Control.RenderControlInternal(HtmlTextWriter writer, ControlAdapter adapter)   at System.Web.UI.Page.ProcessRequestMain(Boolean includeStagesBeforeAsyncPoint, Boolean includeStagesAfterAsyncPoint)       Note from the developer of EasyDNN News:     The problem can be fixed only by DNN. We can't make it working in both cases, with DNN content module and Drag&Drop option when adding a module. We are able to make a version that works in one case, like you've seen when we sent you two versions of the module. For now we've chosen not working of drag&drop option. The module can be moved afterwards after adding on a page and we consider this less important that not working in the DNN content layout module.    In order to fix the issue:   1) DNN must fix the content layout module   2) Or return functioning of the update panel as it was until 9.1"""

summary: "Site languages displays twice"
description: """Build=>EvoqEng 9.2.0.113  1.Navigate to settings=>Site settings=>Languages  2.Click press languages.  3.Language has been shown twice (refer the attachment)  |!Screen Shot 2017-11-10 at 8.19.13 PM.png|thumbnail! |    Issue2  You will see the enabled language flags right of the language panel.(refer the attachment)  | !Screen Shot 2017-11-10 at 8.19.10 PM.png|thumbnail! |    """
comments: "Issue #1 not reproducible.  Issue #2, PR submitted, https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.AdminExperience.Extensions/pull/375" [SEP] "Issue#2 fixed.    Issue#1 can reproducible in evoq engage 9.2.0-119 with chrome browser.    Refer the attachment=> languagetwice.gif" [SEP] "There might be an issue with the images in your site, but I don't see the languages twice anywhere" [SEP] "We call .net method ""CultureInfo.GetCultures"" to get the list of cultures and based on MSDN doc, the culture names and identifiers represent only a subset of cultures that can be found on a particular computer. Windows versions or service packs can change the available cultures. The culture list on Windows 10 contains  more cultures than the one on Windows 7 and for some of the cultures on Windows 10, we don't have corresponding flags in the ""/Iimages/Flags/"" folder. We may add missing flags later but won't fix it for now. "
