summary: "SMTP Server Settings per portal inoperable"
description: """We are unable to get the SMTP Server Settings per portal feature (which came in DNN 7.x.x) to work properly (Admin, Site Settings, Advanced Settings, SMTP Server Settings).  We have 10s of customers in one DNN instance, some of which need to utilize their own specific SMTP settings for registration notifications, etc.      All of the respective fields are in the UI and can be populated properly/saved to the PortalSettings table.  We have verified that the SMTP Server Settings area in Host Settings works as it always has, however those global settings will not work for our customer base.    We have performed this additional test with no success:    - Created a specific account in each customer's portal and made that user a member of the Administrators role.  This account also has the same email address that we are supplying to the SMTP Server Settings area in Admin, Site Settings, Advanced Settings, SMTP Server Settings.  - Set that account as the administrator in Admin, Site Settings, Advanced Settings, Security Settings.    Unfortunately the SMTP Server Settings section of Advanced Settings does not appear to be available to individual portal administrator level accounts vs. being logged into the respective portal as host level account (to try the Test SMTP Settings button while logged in as the actual portal's admin user).  However, a quick new user registration test with all of these settings in place still fails while of course not logged in as any existing user.    I suspect that both the test and live smtp calls are continuing to use the host users email address from the original host level feature's code base, which is causing an authentication/relay scenario that gets properly rejected by each customer's email/smtp server.    We ideally need a fix for this immediately if at all possible.  I believe this may be an issue that's lingered since the introduction of the new feature.  Thanks!  Todd    """

Todd" [SEP] "Todd, with ""Advanced Settings"", do you mean the ""Advanced Settings"" module (which has been retired for DNN 8)?" summary: "Move modules still doesn't work correctly after DNN-7099 fix"
description: """Move modules still doesn't work correctly after DNN-7099 fix, as it checks for all deleted modules on the page while calculating corrected module order value, resulting moving up or down inside a pane with huge steps (expecting just one step).     I think that only deleted modules on same (target) pane should be used to calculate corrected module order value. Pull request is coming."""
comments: "https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1512" [SEP] "To reproduce issue:    1. create new page with a skin having 2 or more panes;  2. create 4 modules on content pane;  3. create at least one module on any other pane (e.g. side pane);  4. delete module from side pane;  5. try to move first module on content pane down.    Expected result on step 5: module move to second place on the pane.    Actual result on step 5: module move to third place on the pane.    Workaround (same for DNN-7099): empty deleted modules from recycle bin for the page."

summary: "Config Merge ALWAYS comments Config files"
description: """Within a module manifest it is possible to set a component type of """"Config"""" to make changes to the web.config, or other configuration files.  As part of these items however, it is not possible to set a version limitation on the processing.    This results in the configuration changes being executed repeatedly.  In the case of a continuous build environment, or other situation with regular updates the web.config is riddled with commented out code making it very hard to read/manage.    Even with a collision option of """"overwrite"""" the config is still commented, even though the current behavior isn't expected unless """"Save"""" is used.      Desired behavior is either for an overwrite to be a true overwrite, or ideally support for """"version"""" in the config section items."""
comments: "[~accountid:<USER> - have you tried the option {{action=""update""}}   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail! " [SEP] "Ash - Yes, we are using action=""update"" and collision=""overwrite"" and seeing the above behavior"

summary: "SI: 404 page not catching all 404 pages"
description: """DNN allows you to select a custom 404 page instead of using the IIS 404 page.  The problem is that DNN does not catch every 404 error such as 404.7, 404.8 and 404.0.    The screen cast below will outline the following steps:  1. Select the default 404 page as the 404 page in Admin > Site Settings > Advanced Settings > Page Management and click update at the bottom of the page.  2. Navigate to website.dnndev.me/gibberish to get the 404 error page you have selected and to confirm that is is working  3. Navigate to a folder structure of your choice that is not protected in any way, such as config or portals and you will get the 404 page.  4. Navigate to a protected folder such as bin or app_data and you will receive the IIS 404.8 page instead of the selected 404 page.    Other 404 error page breaking links are navigating to any of the files in the root directory that are protected by the application which deliver a 404.7:  DotNetNuke.config, Global.asax, ect..    To get a 404.0 page that breaks the 404 page simply navigate to website.dnndev.me/anything.anything and it will display the IIS 404.0 error page.    Expected results for the above URL's is to display the selected 404 page.  Actual results are different IIS 404 pages."""
comments: "I fear, some of the 404 are generated by IIS, not the DNN. You would need to catch all file access through the DNN pipeline." [SEP] "For one of our clients: 'sitename/anything.anything' or 'sitename/anything' takes the site to custom 404 page but 'sitename/anything.' takes the site to HTTP 404. A 'period' after any word is taking the site to HTTP 404. It does not seem to happen with other symbols, or if there is a word after the 'period'. "

(> denotes sub-level)    Home  >Sub Page 1  Contact  >Sub Contact 1  >Sub Contact 2    * With the above tree structure created, login as host  * Navigate to """"Host"""" -> """"Site Management""""  * Select """"Export Site Template"""":  * Provide the template name and description  * Expand the """"Advanced Configuration""""  * Attempt to unselect """"Sub Page"""" 1.  Notice that Home is also unselected.    * Attempt to unselect """"Sub Contact 1"""". This will work, as Sub Contact 2 is still selected  * Attempt to unselect """"Sub Contact 2"""" as well.  Now, notice that contact is unselected    Expected behavior would be that the """"root"""" pages should be selectable, even if their child are not. summary: "RGR - Reset password link is not expiring"
description: """Preconditions:    A user is created by admin/host in the system  SMTP is setup with Papercut to receive emails on port 25    Steps:    1. As host go to Host Settings - Host Settings - Advanced Settings - Membership Management  2. Change """"Reset link timeout"""" and set it to 1 and update the page  3. In a different browser browse to the site and click on login and then click on reset password  4. Enter the username and click on Send reset link  5. On Papercut intercept the email and extract the reset link  6. Wait for more than a min (i.e wait for 5 min)  7. Browse to the link from the email  8. summary: "User who has UpdatePassword set but has not logged in before the reset token expires cannnot do anything any more"
description: """When an admin selects """"User must change password"""", the user is marked as UpdatePassword and a reset token is generated (which is not sent to the user or anyone). With the reset token comes a timeout. Under normal circumstances the user will log in and be prompted to change the password and all will go well. But if the user waits too long the reset will not happen because the """"update password"""" logic is tied to the """"reset password"""" logic. The only way the user can still get access is to regenerate a token but this is not explained nor logical.    What should happen is that (like is done elsewhere for different purposes) the reset token is regenerated right before the redirect to the update password screen. Not at the """"update password"""" button click (although that code does no harm)."""
comments: "I reviewed and merged this PR after testing it. This will appear as of build #9.2.0.135-301    For QA - test steps:  - As a super-user select ""Force Password Change"" for another user in the site (PB > Users > any user under the gears section).  - Go directly to the database and change the ""PasswordResetExpiration"" value in the ""Users"" table to a date in the past for the user whom was forced password-rest.  - As a super-user select ""clear cache"" (under PB > Servers)  - Logout (or from another browser or an incognito window) and try to log-in as the users whose password was reset.    Expected:  - Before the fix: you will receive a message a message saying ""The password reset link has expired and is no longer valid""  - After the fix: you will see the reset-password screen where you can enter new credentials and confirm.    Note: this fix doesn't cover the case where if you have received a link to reset the password that has an expired token. In this case, when you browse to the link, you will still re

Re-edit that same system role (e.g. Administrators)    h2. Expected   1. The role should remain a system role.  2. No disabled control in step 4 above should become enabled.  3. The IsSystemRole flag in the database has been updated to false (0)."""

summary: "block user profile page by default since it potentially discloses sensitive information about users?"
description: """A security owasp test suggested to block user profile page by default since it potentially discloses sensitive information about users?    Increased attack surface because of non- essential functionality (LPLIS-1608- A06)    {quote}  Description  Offering more functionality than strictly needed for correct operation of the appli-cation increases the  likelihood of additional bugs and misconfigurations. This provides an attacker with a larger attack  surface, increasing the likelihood of suc-cessful exploitation of the application.  The following non-complete list of seemingly non-essential pages/functions are found:  /Activity-Feed/userId/1  /Search-Results?search=~&  /Install/UpgradeWizard.aspx  /Resources/Shared/components/ckeditor/samples /index.html  Impact: low  Seemingly non-essential functionality is available on the web server which forms an increased attack  surface to the application.  Probability: high  The non-essential functionalities as listed above can easily be discovered, simply by looking through  the page source and making use of Google search.  Recommendation  Only deploy or install services, middleware and programs which are essen-tial to the correct working  of the application. If partial installations are not possible, non-essential services and functionality  should be removed or disabled.  Reflect the above in the security baseline of the host in order to prevent the same finding in future  assessments. The security baseline must be applied every time a host is taken into production.  {quote}"""
comments: "We can evaluate it for 8.2, but we may not change this.  More investigation is needed.  Thanks!" [SEP] "* we have had several clients perform 3rd party OWASP security scans and all of them marked this as a serious security/privacy issue.  * in the Netherlands we have new EU privacy laws that impose very high fines on these kind of privacy leaks  * these laws will become in to effect in the rest of europe within the next 12 months  * from the ""security of least privilege""  principle making the user profile page containing potentially private information visible by default seems a very bad practice to me?  (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Principle_of_least_privilege)  * so I guess an Enterprise Level CMS like DNN would want to adhere to privacy laws and security best practices? ;-)" [SEP] "The module that displays the profile properties is designed to respect visibility (and it does this today). This visibility can be set by the user and the administrator gets to set the default value for all users. I get tha

summary: "Changes in ProfileController poses problems working with external membership"
description: """UpdateUserProfile in ProfileController has changed in DNN 7.4.2. The change poses problems with working with external membership stores.    The new code (in ProfileController.cs)    public static void UpdateUserProfile(UserInfo user)  {   if (!user.Profile.IsDirty)   {    return;   }     var portalId = GetEffectivePortalId(user.PortalID);   user.PortalID = portalId;     var oldUser = new UserInfo { UserID = user.UserID, PortalID = user.PortalID};   _profileProvider.GetUserProfile(ref oldUser);      _profileProvider.UpdateUserProfile(user);     //Remove the UserInfo from the Cache, as it has been modified   DataCache.ClearUserCache(user.PortalID, user.Username);     if (ProfileUpdated != null)   {    ProfileUpdated(null, new ProfileEventArgs { User = user, OldProfile = oldUser.Profile});   }  }          oldUser contains only UserID and PortalID. This is a problem working with external membership/profile when the username needs to be used as key to the profile store.    In our case we had to rewrite our profile provider to re-lookup the username from the DNN database, an unnessecary call.    ==================================================  I suggest the oldUser is redefined to:    var oldUser = new UserInfo { UserID = user.UserID, Username = user.Username, PortalID = user.PortalID}    ==================================================  It should allow for greater flexibility.    Best regards    Pedro  """

summary: "Quicksettings css styles move <ul> elements to upper right"
description: """When putting a <ul> element in a quick settings control, the css styles used will cause the element to appear in the top right of the control.  This can be overridden by using custom classes on the <ul> element.  In my case, I had to repeat the class name several times in order to overcome the specificity requirements.  Sample below:    {code:css}  ul.ui-fancytree.ui-fancytree.ui-fancytree.ui-fancytree {      position: relative;      width: inherit;      top: 0;      display: block;  }    li.fancytree-lastsib.fancytree-lastsib.fancytree-lastsib > ul {      position: relative;      width: inherit;      -webkit-box-shadow: none;      -moz-box-shadow: none;      box-shadow: none;      border: none;      top: 0;  }    ul.fancytree-container.fancytree-container.fancytree-container ul {      padding: .3em 0 0 1em;      width: inherit;      margin: 0;      display: block;      position: relative;      top: 0;      border: none;      -webkit-box-shadow: none;      -moz-box-shadow: none;      box-shadow: none;  }  {code}  """

summary: "Event Viewer : Allow for simplified Purge procedure"
description: """+Background+    The PurgeEventLog stored procedure can perform badly when the eventLog gets over a certain size.  This is often encountered when an exception is being created frequently by the application.  In these cases the purge event log is unable to complete.    In addition to the slow performance of the query, the structure of the PurgeEventLog query means that it selectively deletes event log types based on the number of rows to keep as per the EventLogConfig table.   This leads to confusing diagnosis in many cases as supporting statements are deleted, leaving an inaccurate timeline when looking back through time.    This change is to modify the PurgeEventLog stored procedure so that it can be changed to a rolling-date age based purge.  This should keep the log size relatively static over time, depending on the log age chosen and whether there has been an increase or decrease in log activity.    With options appearing for off-site log storage (Such as Application Insights and other solutions) the need to keep an extensive log history is redundant, and the Log table is frequently the source of slow database performance, database space failures and other issues.      Current PurgeEventLogCode    +Requirements+  * Add a new Host Setting which specifies the maximum age for EventLogs to be kept  ** This will be called 'EventLogKeepForDays'  ** For new installations and upgrades this value will be created and set to -1 (to keep existing behaviour)  * In the PurgeEventLog stored procedure, the logic will change to:    * Note that this logic will bypass the current logic which checks the number of events to keep for each event type in favour of just keeping a set number of days of logs - if the EventLogKeepForDays value is set, the logic will switch to a simple date based delete.      The purpose of this requirement is to allow people who have set up more comprehensive log preservation to keep the on-site logs small. """
comments: "Related change which stops running the PurgeEventLog when you load the EventViewer page." [SEP] "Hi [~accountid:<USER>, have a question that whether need change in UI for this host setting, or just add it in database/codebase, no need change it in host settings page? thanks,    question #2:  for example, if i set EvengLogKeepForDays to 1:  1. should we remove logs which comes from yesterday or older, only keep todays log;  2. should we remove logs comes from the day before yesyerday or older, only keep yesterday and today logs;  3. should we remove logs which is before 24 hours, and only keep logs in recent 24 hours.  which one is the correct result?" [SEP] "No need for UI - this will be something people can change in the database if they want to change the behaviour.    Answers for question 2:  if KeepDays =1, then the purge means that only items are kept are for the last 24 hours  if KeepDays = 2, then the purge means that only items are kept are for the last 48 hours" [SEP] "if you override the number of

summary: "Event Viewer : Don't process Purge before showing Event Viewer Page"
description: """+Background+  Currently whenever the Event Viewer page is loaded, the code runs the 'PurgeEventLog' stored procedure before showing the UI.  The PurgeEventLog SP is also run on a scheduled task.    While this works OK, if you have a situation where the Event Log is over-full and the Purge Event Log cannot complete, running the PurgeEventLog procedure will cause the Event Viewer page to time out waiting for the Synchronous return of the SP.    This change will be to stop running the PurgeEventLog when the Admin->Event Viewer is loaded.    +Requirements+  * PurgeEventLog stored procedure should not be run when the Admin->Event Viewer page is loaded  * PurgeEventLog stored procedure will continue to be run as a scheduled task  """

summary: "XmlMerge adds extra empty element when targetpath and path are both inside location element"
description: """XmlMerge automatically translates XPath queries to handle [{{<location>}}|https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/b6x6shw7(v=vs.100).aspx] elements in the {{web.config}}.  However, there's an issue where the {{path}} attribute can get translated without also properly translating the {{targetpath}} attribute.    There's [a test where the {{path}} is outside the {{<location>}} element and the {{targetpath}} is inside the {{<location>}} element|https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/blob/development/DNN%20Platform/Tests/DotNetNuke.Tests.Integration/Services/Installer/MergeFiles/UpdateWithTargetPathMerge.xml#L3], which works.  However, if they're both inside the {{<location>}} element, then an extra, empty copy of the top-level element is created.    For example, to add {{<staticContent>}}:      If {{<system.webServer>}} is inside a {{<location>}} element, this will produce an empty {{<staticContent/>}} element, alongside the correct {{<staticContent>}} element, which breaks the site."""
comments: "I've pushed a failing test case to the [bug/DNN-8651 branch|https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/compare/bug/DNN-8651?expand=1&w=1]"

summary: "SI: The contents of the emails should be filtered to prevent XSS and HTML links from being sent."
description: """From client: On the Log Viewer page there is a feature to allow a user to “Send Exceptions”. This feature allows the user to attach site exceptions to any email address. The user can also include an email message, and include malicious code/links within the email. If an attacker were to gain access to the application and utilize this functionality, an unsuspecting victim may see an email from an @fisglobal.com email address and trust the contents. The contents of the emails should be filtered to prevent XSS and HTML links from being sent. Steps for Verification: 1. Log into application 2. On the Log Viewer page, click an exception to attach and enter an email address and subject for the email 3. In the Message section add an HTML link (click) 4. Send the email 5. The recipient will see the email with the link and will possibly trust the source, which could then contain malicious links."""
comments: "viewing and sending log entries is accessible for site admininstrators only.   If a hacker gets access to a site administrator account, he has a number of options - this would be one of the smaller ones.  I suggest closing this issue." [SEP] "Thank you for report this issue.  We will look into addressing it in future releases.  " [SEP] "As Sebastian said, if they get to this point you have bigger concerns (security wise). Also, the functionality fills (sending error reports of XML data) is going to contain HTML link (and should). "

summary: "When renaming a top level folder, the folder provider is never called"
description: """Please see the attached video.    Renaming a subfolder works fine (the file manager calls the provider to do its thing, as expected (and verified in the S3 console).    However, nothing happens when renaming a root folder. The provider is not called at all to do anything. Therefore, the renamed folder does not exist in S3 (nothing happened in S3, because DNN didn’t called the provider to rename the folder), and gets deleted when performing a sync operation.  """
comments: "This is a huge problem for us at SI Play. Any update on a fix?" [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> This is as per design actually. See the video here: http://screencast.com/t/heGDU7l5T    The root folder in Evoq's S3 provider and other ones is actually a virtual folder in DNN's side. It's not a physical folder on S3. " [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER>  Can you discuss this in a bit more detail? Could you give me a call?" [SEP] "Hi Ash,  Did you get my email (Sent: Monday, May 9)?    I found where the problem is.  In \DNN Platform\Library\Services\FileSystem\FolderManager.cs,  RenameFolder() is performing the rename based on the parent folder provider, not the actual folder provider (of the one being renamed). This is why the provider (in this case, s3) is never called.     Tks,  H" [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> Yes, I have details from you. Thanks for that. Our Engineers are digging further. The changes recommended by you is creating further challenges. We will keep you posted here." [SEP] "changes looks good and merged in 8.1.

summary: "Use DateTime.UtcNow instead of DateTime.Now"
description: """Throughout the DNN code, there's a lot of incorrect uses of DateTime.Now to store values into the database. DateTime.Now is the representation of the configured timezone on the server (EST, CST, etc.). We should always be storing DateTime.UtcNow into the database so that values can be converted back to any local time, as needed. It makes DNN more portable when switching hosting providers in different geographical data centers because UTC datetime is always the same regardless of timezone or daylight saving. To be truly enterprise ready and secure, DNN needs a consistent way to store date and time.    There is also a performance benefit to switch, because DateTime.UtcNow is many times faster than DateTime.Now. The .NET implementation of DateTime.Now is actually taking DateTime.UtcNow and converting it to local time of the server so it's doing a lot of unnecessary arithmetic.    [https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/kirillosenkov/2012/01/10/datetime-utcnow-is-generally-preferable-to-datetime-now/]  [https://dzone.com/articles/darkness-behind-datetimenow]  """
comments: "Hi Stephen, this sounds like a good addition.  However, we are not able to get any additional items into the release unless it has a major impact on usability.  I'd like to address this in future releases.  "

summary: "remove ckeditor samples documentation from install version"
description: """A security owasp test suggested to block access to the files below:    /Resources/Shared/components/ckeditor/samples    seems to me that these files are not necessary for live/install version?    also I would suggest to block user profile page be default since it potentially discloses sensitive information about users?    Increased attack surface because of non- essential functionality (LPLIS-1608- A06)  {quote}  Description  Offering more functionality than strictly needed for correct operation of the appli-cation increases the  likelihood of additional bugs and misconfigurations. This provides an attacker with a larger attack  surface, increasing the likelihood of suc-cessful exploitation of the application.  The following non-complete list of seemingly non-essential pages/functions are found:    * /Activity-Feed/userId/1  * /Search-Results?search=~&  * /Install/UpgradeWizard.aspx  * /Resources/Shared/components/ckeditor/samples /index.html    Impact: low  Seemingly non-essential functionality is available on the web server which forms an increased attack  surface to the application.  Probability: high  The non-essential functionalities as listed above can easily be discovered, simply by looking through  the page source and making use of Google search.  Recommendation  Only deploy or install services, middleware and programs which are essen-tial to the correct working  of the application. If partial installations are not possible, non-essential services and functionality  should be removed or disabled.  Reflect the above in the security baseline of the host in order to prevent the same finding in future  assessments. The security baseline must be applied every time a host is taken into production.  {quote} """

summary: "Config component in package manifest shouldn't touch web.config if it doesn't change anything"
description: """It is common for packages to include a [config component|http://www.dnnsoftware.com/wiki/manifest-config-component] in their manifest if they require any adjustments to the {{web.config}} file.  On initial install, this change will be made correctly.  However, this component will also run on upgrades (ensuring that the config is correctly in place), and the vast majority of the time, won't do anything, since it was done on install.  The problem is that the config component still updates the {{web.config}} file, even if no changes are being made, which causes the website to be restarted unnecessarily.    I've attached a modified version of the Xcillion theme package which adds support for the {{woff}} and {{woff2}} font file formats.  When installing this a second time, it should not require restarting the site, but currently DNN will restart the site anyway."""

summary: "Adding Query strings after root url converts params in ""/"" and adds seotitle of homepage"
description: """Keeping Query strings in URL's is possible with the fix on http://www.dnnsoftware.com/wiki/keeping-querystring-items-out-of-the-url-path-in-dnn.    However at the homepage this doesn't work. The query strings are still replaced by slashes in the address bar.    Steps to reproduce:    # Go to the homepage of a DNN website  # Append ?utm=123123123 in the adress bar (for example http://dnn8.dev?utm=123123123123312)  # Hit enter to go to the page    *Result:*    URL redirects to http://dnn8.dev/Home/utm/123123123123312  (The Seo title of the homepage is added and query strings are converted)    *Expected result*    The URL with query strings stays intact and the browser shows the homepage. """
comments: "The changes in DNN-6826 are for the google parameters, which generate URLs in this format:   example.com/?utm_source=src&utm_medium=med&utm_term=term&utm_content=cont&utm_campaign=name    Your example (utm/123123) is not an example of the Google parameters, which are all utm_something.  The regex pattern matches utm* values, not utm.  If you re-do this test with ?utm_medium=123123 you will see that it works as expected.    If you wanted to change your specific setting to match the use case here (utm/123123) then you would need to change the regex pattern in your specific install to:        I will close as won't fix because this is not a bug and if you have specific Url needs you will need to change the settings to suit.  " [SEP] "Issue can be worked around by modifying the regex parameters for a specific installation." [SEP] "Thanks @bruce chapman.    It indeed works!    However I find it strange that this is not added or adjusted on the page about this topic: http://www.dnnsoftware.com/wiki/keeping-querystr

summary: "Conditional loading of unneeded resources such as jQueryUI"
description: """There are a few resources that are automatically loaded by DNN even if they are not required. These include jqueryui and modelpopup.js  In the interest of speeding up load times these resources should only be loaded when they are needed. jQueryUI for instance is 450k (unminified) and my site does not use it at all. Not everyone is on high speed broadband and load speed can affect the way Google considers a site.  I understand that some DNN facilities may require these resources but that would only be the case when those facilities are used. """
comments: "Which DNN version are you referring to?  Any component in DNN Client Resource Management is loaded only upon request (by the framework, a module or the skin applied to the page)" [SEP] "I am referring to version 8.001.  My test home page does not load any modules at all. It is completely blank and my skin does not load jqueryui  Another resource that is being loaded automatically is a 146k javascript file mainly containing Microsoft Ajax. This is being loaded via the url /Telerik.Web.UI.WebResource.axd. I do not have 'Enable Microsoft Ajax' turned on  These resources may be needed when using the DNN CMS but when the viewer is not logged in then why load them at all    " [SEP] "Resources are loaded conditionally, and we're not shipping with Telerik."

summary: "Multilanguage AUM pass incorrect urlParms to TransformFriendlyUrlToQueryString"
description: """By default, running advanced URL format, the resulting URL looks like this:    http://site.com/pagename/param1    The TransformFriendlyUrlToQueryString method that needs to be implemented by providers will see the correct urlParms parameter containing 1 array entry:     urlParms {string[1]} string[]    [0] """"param1"""" string    As soon as you enable multiple languages on a site under Admin > Languages, it automatically creates site aliases with entries like *site.com/en-us* and *site.com/en-ca*    http://site.com/en-us/pagename/param1  http://site.com/en-ca/pagename/param1    Notice the urlParms parameter is incorrectly populated breaking the parser in the URL extension provider. The URL extension provider is unable to correctly guess and separate between what is actual query parameter and page:    urlParms {string[3]} string[]    [0] """"en-us"""" string    [1] """"pagename"""" string    [2] """"param1"""" string    To confirm the inconsistency, the article here [http://www.dnnsoftware.com/wiki/extension-url-providers] states the urlParms parameter should exclude the page path (and therefore language too).    *urlParms : a string array of the URL segments (/segment1/segment2) in the URL """"after"""" the site alias & page path have been matched and removed from the entire URL. In this example, it would be an array of 1 position with 'My-Item' in it.*    Please fix this ASAP as it breaks the advanced URL provider badly."""
comments: "I like to add, I tested it also breaks in DNN 8.0.1" [SEP] "Hi,    Can we please expedite the fix on this? Many international sites operate in more than one language and it's breaking the advanced URL provider. It's often not an option to switch back to humanfriendly because it breaks the SEO efforts already invested with advanced URL (all public URLs become inaccessible)." [SEP] "Hi,    Is someone working on this issue?    We are using DNN7 which supports url rewriting using iFInity Url Master Module which has been acquired by DNN Corp in 2012.  http://www.ifinity.com.au/Products/Url_Master_DNN_SEO_Urls    It is hard to believe that 5 years later, URL rewriting is still not working properly in a multi language mode.    We would like to migrate to DNN9 but we need an URL rewriter without bugs.    Hoping to get some feedback soon.  Best regards.            " [SEP] "This problem appears only when the provider returns *false* in the *AlwaysUsesDnnPagePath* method. Is that the intended behavior?"

summary: "Performing Hard Tab Deletion, messes up TabOrder of other portals"
description: """Currently, when performing a hard delete of a root level tab in a multi-portal environment, the DeleteTab script does not check against the PortalId while updating TabOrder. This means that other portals that are not affected will result in an incorrect TabOrder.    Steps to Reproduce:  1. On a new DNN Site, browse to Host > Site Management.  2. Create a new Portal.   3. On portal 1, browse to Admin > Page Management.  4. Add a new page.  5. Move this new page to the top of the root level.  6. Delete this page.  7. Browse to Admin Recycle Bin and click Empty Recycle Bin.  8. Check the TabOrder of the portal 2 and see it is incorrect/ has duplicates."""
comments: "I was of the impression, this had been fixed in DNN 8.0.0.  I am using a different (more stable) approach to adjust TabOrder upon insert, move and delete of pages in my TurboDNN.  " [SEP] "Yeah, I think this one just went under the radar. The MoveTabAfter, MoveTabBefore, & MoveTabToParent were updated in 7.3.3, but I am still am to reproduce this issue on a fresh 8.0.1 site." [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER>,  you are correct, the issue is in statement       which ignores the portalid. Turbo Scripts use a call to BuildTabLevelAndPath procedure, which rebuilds all tabPaths, levels and tabOrder values. See http://dnnscript.codeplex.com/downloads/get/1465341 for details." [SEP] "PR #1453 submitted" [SEP] "Create new PR #1893 based on #1422, to made changes in 9.2.0 sqldataprovider file. should available in platform 9.2.0.174-391 build." [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER>,  please see my comment for your PR." [SEP] "Verified and Issue has been fixed in Platform 9.2.0 #221 472 (/)"

summary: "User With ""Add Page"" permissions cannot use Bulk Add Feature"
description: """Currently when a user has the permission to """"Add Pages"""" to a particular page, but is not an administrator they are given access to the """"Bulk Add"""" feature.  (""""Admin"""" -> """"Page Management"""", then right click on the node & select """"Add Pages"""")    However, once on this page if you add a page, or pages, and click the button the page immediately returns.  This action method on this control handles the permissions DIFFERENTLY than the permissions to show the build addition.     The security check should be the same for both.  (I will submit a pull request for this)    Additionally: Users can use the """"Add Page"""" feature to add individual pages without any issue."""
comments: "Changes good. PR Merged. Available in 8.1.0.39" [SEP] "Previous existing issue : reproduce in screencast:     http://screencast.com/t/N50pcFUL    Verified in Platform 8.1 Build 46 , fresh install and upgrade   The Regular User who has permission of add pages is now able to add the bulk pages creation    Please refer to the screencast   http://screencast.com/t/LV5WbM4DxWBL"

summary: "Check for Null Redirect Reference on tab when Exporting template."
description: """If you delete a tab that other pages are still redirecting to, when you export the site template, DNN throws a null reference exception.    1. Login to a site as a superuser.   2. Browse to Admin > Pages.   3. Right click the root node and select Add Pages.  4. Add 2 new pages. IE """"Test Page 1"""" & """"Test Page 2""""  5. Set """"Test Page 1"""" to redirect to """"Test Page 2"""" and Update.  6. Delete """"Test Page 2"""".  7. Notice that """"Test Page 1"""" still has a Link setting for Page (A Page on Your Site), but the dropdown says """"Select a Page"""". If you query the database for this, it still contains the TabId for the old page.  8. Browse to Host > Site Management.  9. Select """"Export Site Template"""".  10. Select the current site, enter a name and description and check all the boxes.  11. Notice the critical exception.(this part fixed in DNN-8563, so its unable to see this error if use 8.1 latest build, in this case can continure below steps.)  12. create a new site based on the new template, then visit """"Test Page 1"""", you will see YSOD, also if you visit this page in original portal, it doesn't work as well."""

summary: "Si: EVOQCONTENT - Users not receiving notification when registration has been approved"
description: """Brief Description of Issue:    User does not receive an email notification when their registration has been approved.    Clear Steps to Reproduce:    Set the site's registration to Private (in site settings)    Register as a new user    As host/admin/cm/moderator, Approve this user.    The user is added to the site.    However, the user is NOT sent an email informing him that his request for registration on the website has been approved.      """
comments: "Update for the client that submitted this request:    Thank you for submitting this feature enhancement request. However, this will require significant code modification, and would require a lot of testing to ensure it hadn't negatively effected other pieces of the product.  Because we already working on, and have planned out our next release, we wont have time to address this issue.  We are keeping it in our backlog, and hope to address it in future releases.  " [SEP] "Another customer has opened a ticket for this.    Ticket # 1403" [SEP] "Hi [~accountid:<USER>, can you provide what message title and body need send to user when user get approved? thanks," [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> I've created a default message below.  However, this message ought to be stored in the same place as the initial welcome message ""Thank you for registgering, etc"" It needs to be editable and have the same behavior.      Subject:  Registration has been approved for {URL}    Body:    Dear {Customer Name},    Thank you for register

summary: "Account Login Module Redirect Issue"
description: """I have a custom module that heavily uses the ReturnUrl querystring parameter for better user flow. The site also has the DNN Account Login module placed on each page of the website for users to login. When a user is logged in, the user is immediately 302 redirected to the URL specified in the ReturnUrl parameter. This behavior breaks any module that utilizes the ReturnUrl feature of DNN.    In versions prior to DNN v8 (e.g. v7.4.2), authenticated users were not redirected when the Account login module is on a page and the URL included a ReturnUrl parameter.    I am aware that the LOGIN skin object can create the login link to open the popup window or take the user to the login page, but the Account Login module should be able to be placed on a page without affecting the flow. The RedirectUrl parameter should only be followed during an actual successful authentication, not because the page it is on just happens to have a RedirectUrl parameter.    QA need to pick up build less than 9.2.0.301 though it is present since 8.0.1, and issue is about having Account Login Module in a page for logged in user, if on such page returnurl is present then application was redirecting it to that page, which is not desirable.    +Steps to reproduce:+    1. After login as host, add two pages one with all (PageAllWithAccountModule) and other is registered users permission (restricted)  2. In edit mode, Add Account Login Module on PageAllWithAccountModule, then exit from EDIT MODE.  3. You should be on that page then your URL will be  http://yousitename/PageAllWithAccountModule  4. Now add returnurl in the above URL and enter, like this http://yousitename/PageWithAccountModule?returnurl=restricted    Expected:  It should be on same page with URL, should not follow returnurl   http://yousitename/PageWithAccountModule?returnurl=restricted     Result:  It is redirecting to restricted page   http://yousitename/restricted     However if user is not logged in and he tries to access http://{yousitename}/restricted, then he would see redirected url like this  http://yousitename/Login?returnurl=%2frestricted  And upon logging in he should be redirected restricted page with URL  http://yousitename/restricted    So, in brief returnurl should only work if user is not logged in, if logged-in then not"""
comments: "Can you provide or point us to a sample module that will allow us to reproduce this error? We haven't seen this in our own properties. " [SEP] "To reproduce the issue, all you have to do is create a new page in any DNN v8.0.1 page, allow the page to be viewed by all users and add the Account Login module to a page. When a user is not authenticated, they will see the login form whether the ReturnUrl parameter is in the URL or not. But an authenticated user will be automatically redirected to the URL in the parameter which is undesirable.    Here is a template to quickly create the new page as specified above:     [^DNN8552.page.template]     You may need to change the hostname, but on my server here are the values I tested:    http://dnndev.me/DNN-8552    http://dnndev.me/DNN-8552?returnurl=%2fDefault.aspx" [SEP] "Is this going to be assigned to a release?" [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> - Thank you for your patience.  We had other items that had to take priority - but I have assigned to 9.0 for release. " [SEP] "

summary: "Newtonsoft.Json assembly conflict"
description: """Starting from DNN 8.0.1 referencing the assembly DotNetNuke.Web.Mvc with cause this warning when building:    No way to resolve conflict between """"Newtonsoft.Json, Version=7.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=30ad4fe6b2a6aeed"""" and """"Newtonsoft.Json, Version=6.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=30ad4fe6b2a6aeed"""". Choosing """"Newtonsoft.Json, Version=7.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=30ad4fe6b2a6aeed"""" arbitrarily.  Consider app.config remapping of assembly """"Newtonsoft.Json, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=30ad4fe6b2a6aeed"""" from Version """"6.0.0.0"""" [C:\inetpub\DNN8\801\bin\Newtonsoft.Json.dll] to Version """"7.0.0.0"""" [] to solve conflict and get rid of warning.    Sometimes when staring debug VS replaces the Newtonsoft.Json.dll (ver. 6.0.8) in the bin folder which will display an error: {color:#f6c342}[FileLoadException: Could not load file or assembly 'Newtonsoft.Json' or one of its dependencies. The located assembly's manifest definition does not match the assembly reference. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80131040)]{color}    I believe this is because some .Net assemblies like: *System.Web.Http.dll* & *System.Net.Http.Formatting.dll* uses the Newtonsoft.Json.dll ver. 6"""
comments: "Hi [~accountid:<USER>, It happens due to some of the dependency to old version of Newtonsoft. To solve your issue you can add app.config with dependent assembly referring to version 7.0.0.0 of Newtonsoft.    " [SEP] "Hi [~accountid:<USER>, that did the trick. Thanks" [SEP] "Please be noted, there are some 3rd party modules still bring in older version Newtonsoft.Json.dll and modify the <dependentAssembly> in web.config file. And if it happened, the site will be down. "

summary: "SI: Add option to enable rel=""canonical"" tag for Primary Alias "
description: """A client has asked that they be able to apply the rel=""""canonical"""" tag available for the Primary Alias. At this time, if a url typo is made, they would like the tag to be present on the page that is displayed.    Example:  http://example.com/community/communitypageasdf (typo)    In the case of the example, http://example.com/community/communitypageasdf would remain in the URL, but the parent page - http://example.com/community/ - would be displayed."""
comments: "any typo should result in a 404 IMO. Custom 404 might contain a link to the home page." [SEP] "The issue here is the same as returning a redirect or 404.  The dynamic Url capability of Dnn, which allows any third party module to show content based on a URL format the module defines, means that the Dnn platform cannot differentiate the difference between a valid third-party module Url (i.e., blog/some-blog-post and an incorrect url like /blog/oops-this-is-not-a-page).      The only way to do this is to either implement a property of a page which prevents *any* third party module from running on the page and defining a url scheme (thus, the universe of known good urls for that page are defined) or to modify the core so that any third party module running on the page must define the list of known good urls to the Dnn platform.  Neither are particularly workable solutions.    Setting the canonical link property at the site alias level will show a canonical link, but that will merely reflect back the Url requeste

summary: "Deleting a tab should delete all redirect references to that tab."
description: """Currently if you create a page, and set that page to redirect to a page on your site, if you delete that page that it is redirecting to, the page will still try to redirect to the non-existing page. I think it would be better to update tabs that were redirecting to the page to remove the redirect.    This was also causing a problem with exporting a site when it had a tab that was redirecting to a tab that didn't exist. The export would fail with a critical exception.    The proposed fix will add a message to warn users that deleting the page will remove the redirect messages, remove redirect references when a page is deleted, and handle exporting for sites that already have invalid records in the database.    Steps to reproduce.    1. Login to a site as a superuser.   2. Browse to Admin > Pages.   3. Right click the root node and select Add Pages.  4. Add 2 new pages. IE """"Test Page 1"""" & """"Test Page 2""""  5. Set """"Test Page 1"""" to redirect to """"Test Page 2"""" and Update.  6. Delete """"Test Page 2"""".  7. Notice that """"Test Page 1"""" still has a Link setting for Page (A Page on Your Site), but the dropdown says """"Select a Page"""". If you query the database for this, it still contains the TabId for the old page.  8. Browse to Host > Site Management.  9. Select """"Export Site Template"""".  10. Select the current site, enter a name and description and check all the boxes.  11. Notice the critical exception."""
comments: "this cannot be implemented in the database schema, unless using a trigger. Are you saying that the implementation for the pull request I submitted won't work because I am missing something, or just that we could do it in the sproc?    Pull request can be found here: [https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1409]    Thanks!" [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER>,  Your pull request is not optimal, as it causes a number of calls to the database (in RemoveRedirectReferences), while just a small enhancement to a stored procedure is required.     However, there is a general downside of this approach: Note that we are talking about softdelete, which means that the tab might be undeleted afterwards, if it was deleted by accident. In this case, the previous situation should be restored - which doesn't work

summary: "WEB API performance investigation"
description: """During profiling Platform performance, I found that DNN's WEB API requests take 1 to 2 milli-seconds more than the time taken by those requests from a default WEB API application created by VS. We need to investigate the cause of this extra time which could be coming from authentication/authorization, extra filters, AUM, etc.) and investigate whether there is a possibility to reduce/remove these extra milli-seconds.    Note: the test was done against a """"do-nothing"""" DNN WEB API method against a default WEB API application method created by VS 2015 project which returns a single string value. The average time taken by DNN app was about 3 to 4 ms while the default app was 2 to 3 ms.    I attached a JMeter image of performance comparison between the two calls ( !WebApiPerf.png|thumbnail! )"""
comments: "I’ve investigated the WEB API performance in DNN and found few sources for the performance hit we incur. Here are the major identified areas:  - The rewrite and process request methods of the {{AdvancedUrlRewriter}} are other areas. This is the most costly area in performance for WEB API calls.      - Injected {{DnnContextMessageHandler}} class in the API processing pipeline. This one is called in each API request to setup the proper context for the call. The major time is consumed in creating the portal setting object in this call:    In this call the  is called which is the one responsible about consuming most of the time.    Measuring these with JMeter against a plain vanilla WEB API service created as a Visual Studio C# project, showed that these are adding more than 1 millisecond on local machine when there are no Database access and all values are obtained from the cache.    To improve this and reduce some of the extra overhead would require a huge amount of work to reduce the re-writer logic and cre

summary: "VW_MODULES CASE statement causing pagebase error"
description: """The VW_MODULES view has the below CASE statement causing an error:    CASE WHEN tm.IconFile LIKE 'fileid=%' THEN                            (SELECT     IsNull(Folder, '') + [FileName]                              FROM          dbo.[vw_Files]                              WHERE     fileid = CAST(SUBSTRING(tm.IconFile, 8, 10) AS int)) ELSE COALESCE (tm.IconFile, '') END AS IconFile    When this view is joined to another table as in the stored procedure 'getModulebyDefinition', the subquery in the above codes is fired for some unknown reason.  The substring returns a partial string of a file path for iconfile.  It cannot be cast to integer and, therefore, causes the error stated below.    My proposed solution is to change the code to the following:    CASE WHEN tm.IconFile LIKE 'fileid=%' THEN                            (SELECT     IsNull(Folder, '') + [FileName]                              FROM          dbo.[vw_Files]                              WHERE      CAST(fileid AS nchar(10)) = SUBSTRING(tm.IconFile, 8, 10)) ELSE COALESCE (tm.IconFile, '') END AS IconFile    The result is the same but no cast accidental casting of strings to integers will happen.  I am currently working on a code change and will issue a pull request when fixed.    Error:  [FATAL] DotNetNuke.Framework.PageBase - An error has occurred while loading page.    System.Data.SqlClient.SqlException (0x80131904): Conversion failed when converting the nvarchar value '/Sigma/Use' to data type int.       at System.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection. summary: "GetModulesByDefinition method should use caching for faster performance"
description: """The ModuleController.GetModulesByDefinition() method should perform a cache lookup to avoid querying the database like its counterpart GetModuleByDefinition() method is already doing. The GetModuleByDefinition is limited to returning the first arbitrary module only, which is less useful than the GetModulesByDefinition that returns the full information.    Any relatively complex module with more than one module definition that requires figuring out if other supporting modules are added to the site will call this method, often for as many module definitions it has leading to multiple database queries per page. For example, a shopping cart module has many cross-functional links on the shopping page that needs to figure out if the category, product list, product detail, cart modules are present will query the database repeatedly leading to poor performance.    Unfortunately, this method call cannot be cached by the calling application because it has no way to properly invalidate the cache (when page is deleted, module removed from page, etc.) that only the core library can know.    The cache implementation should be quite easy to implement simply by following/reusing the similar cache code in the GetModuleByDefinition method.    Thanks,"""
comments: "there is a difficulty in cache dependency, as any update to a moduledefinition and module needs to clear this cache as well"

summary: "Cannot add module when packages with same friendly name conflict"
description: """The Add Module loads forever and never completes if there is a package with the same friendly name as another package (see attached image). Friendly names should not need to be unique, only package names should be unique. This is troublesome for popular modules like """"Wiki"""", """"HTML"""", """"Chat"""" where the advertised product name is short and has a high chances of collision.    To replicate, simply go to Host > Extensions. Choose a package like """"Module Creator"""" and rename it to """"Razor Host"""" and Update Extension. Then go to any page and try adding a module and notice the Add Module panel is frozen.    Other users noticed and discussed this issue in the forum about duplicate friendlyName in the DesktopModule table causes problem:    [http://www.dnnsoftware.com/forums/threadid/509795/scope/posts/modules-not-loading-on-add-new-module]      """
comments: "we might need a strategy, how to display two modules with same friendly name - one idea would be prefixing it with vendor name." I think prefixing is bad idea because a lot of companies are using DNN as CMS and adding their own custom modules to re-sell as an added value service and even white branded to their own. Many companies would not like to have a prefix as it makes their modules look like second class lesser modules. summary: "Do not use dot in folder names"
description: """There are a number of users and Hosters, who use urlscan tool on their server.  By default, this tool blocks access to paths with dots (.) in its name.  DNN 8 introduces versioning for default.css, moving it to   * /Resources/Shared/stylesheets/dnndefault/7.0.0/ and  * /Resources/Shared/stylesheets/dnndefault/8.0.0/ folder.  As a result. the default.css file is not loaded and all site administration is messed up.  These folders should be renamed not to contain a dot, e,g. by removing the dots or replacing them with another character, e,g. a dash (-)."""

summary: "Also import PDB-files in addition to dll files in the installer-assembly area"
description: """When users install our modules and run into an error, they have trouble reporting what exactly the error is, because the stack in the events-module is not really helpful.     BUT there is a solution: if we deploy the built PDB files from visual studio together with the .DLL files, then the error messages include exact line numbers. This is very helpful in fixing issues.     PDB files have no security implication, they are like JS-map files and only help the error-system inform the user about the code-line where something happened.     Because of this, it's important that we can also include the PDB files in the installation. Unfortunately as of now, the installer blocks this extension - telling the user it's unsafe.     We should add pdb-files to the list of allowed files in the assembly installer to aid in better user error-feedback.       The fix is to replace this            /// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------          /// <summary>          /// Gets a list of allowable file extensions (in addition to the Host's List)          /// </summary>          /// <value>A String</value>          public override string AllowableFiles          {              get              {                  return """"dll"""";              }          }      with this            /// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------          /// <summary>          /// Gets a list of allowable file extensions (in addition to the Host's List)          /// </summary>          /// <value>A String</value>          public override string AllowableFiles          {              get              {                  return """"dll, pdb"""";              }          }    on lines 104 to 115 in the AssemblyInstaller.cs - linked here: https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/blob/115ae75da6b152f77ad36312eb76327cdc55edd7/DNN%20Platform/Library/Services/Installer/Installers/AssemblyInstaller.cs#L104-L115"""

Change is available in platform build# 9.1.1.108." [SEP] "Verified in Platform v. 09.01.01 (115) that PDB files been copied fine now and sample package attached for further testing."

summary: "Allow Override of prefixing Service to Username for OAuth providers."
description: """The fix for DNN-4133 was to add a prefix for the service to the beginning of the username. This broke our custom OAuth provider and I believe that we should have the ability to override whether we want to prefix the service to the Username in OAuth providers.  In addition to allowing that overriding, this also implements the option to automatically associate logins to existing users, based on email.    Testing of this API change will require a custom OAuth provider implementation which sets the new {{PrefixServiceToUserName}} and {{AutoMatchExistingUsers}} properties in that provider's implementation of {{OAuthClientBase}} to {{false}} and {{true}}, respectively.  That custom provider may be an existing provider (e.g. Facebook), tweaked to override those two properties.    When configured as mentioned above, the username of users who log in via that provider should be the user's email (as provided by the OAuth provider).  Additionally, if a user with that email address as their username already exists, that OAuth login would be associated with the existing account, rather than trying to create a new account."""

summary: "Search Results without Url"
description: """*Notice*:    Urls of all Search results are empty for one of my portals. Other portals in the same installation are still working correctly.   Reindex doesn't change anything.  There is only one [thread in forum| http://www.dnnsoftware.com/forums/threadid/513398/scope/posts/threadpage/1] which looks similar..    *Diagnose*    Debugging source pointed out the fact that there was no _default portal alias_ declared for that portal.    It is quite simple to get your portal into such a state:    * In portal settings, add an alias and set it to the default portal. This will disable default for the current alias.  * Edit the new portal alias and unset default  * This would be enough, but you might even delete that alias.    Now there you are with one single non editable non default portal alias. And you would never think that this will result in broken search results.  """

summary: "Conditionally include SPA script/css token support for settings"
description: """In a DNN 7 version of my module, I was able to expose a setting to my users to allow them to choose whether or not they would use debug, CDN, or local versions of script files.  With the current SPA implementation, we're unable to do any of that.  Our users have no choice in the matter.      We need a way through extensibility to tell the DNN rendering engine when/if to include a CSS/JS file and what version to include.      An example use case would be my module requiring Bootstrap.  However, if the site theme already has it, then I don't need to include it.  The site administrator should be able to make a decision here.  Maybe on the page with my module, they include a skin without the bootstrap dependencies and prefer I load them instead, or vice versa.  With the current implementation, there's nothing we can do either way.  It's currently a very technical decision instead."""

summary: "Conditionally include SPA script/css token support for browsers"
description: """A critical thing to support when building any website is how to support Internet Explorer and sometimes other browsers too.  Unfortunately, there's currently no way for us to indicate to DNN when to include certain tokens, and when not to.  We are forced to always include all scripts/CSS regardless to the use case.      At a minimum, we should be able to indicate which web browser(s)/version(s) that a script needs to be included for.  For example IE shims.  Ideally, we should re-purpose a common standard when doing this, like with how we do it elsewhere with conditional HTML.    http://www.quirksmode.org/css/condcom.html"""

summary: "Telerik: Control Replacement"
description: """Telerik is used in multiple areas throughout the Platform. With Platform version 8, we started making changes to completely move away from Telerik throughout the products. This story is about the areas worth converting over (some areas, it doesn't make sense because it would basically be a complete rewrite; like the old admin/host modules). We will have drop downs move to normal asp.net dropdown controls (html select inputs preferred), calendars can use pikaday (or some alternative as long as this is client-side focused, localizable for multiple cultures and it is not a server side .NET control) and grids should go to the standard asp.net grid (HTML/JavaScript alternatives preferred).     Non-module UI to Convert:  - URL Control (used in page settings, module settings, file upload from URL).   -- 4 drop downs  - Permissions Grids (used in page settings, module settings, extension deploy permissions)  -- 2 drop downs, 1 grid  - File Picker (used for profile photo)  -- 1 drop down  - Page Settings  -- 4 drop downs, 2 Date/Time Pickers, 1 grid  - Module Settings  -- 5 drop downs, 2 Date/Time Pickers, 1 grid    Areas to *NOT* Touch:  - Digital Asset Manager Module  - Document Viewer Module (Evoq only)  - Ribbon Bar: Control Panel (Ensure removed completely from product packaging)  - Any Admin/Host module removed in 9.0"""

summary: "Payment Processor Credentials Auto-Populated and Saved in Plain Text"
description: """*Brief Description of Issue:*  If an Administrator or Host user elects to save their login credentials through their browser, their credentials are auto-populated in the host and admin Payment Processor UserId and Password fields if they were initially blank. If the user is not carefully observing this being set automatically, they can inadvertently save their login credentials to these fields on Update.    The values from these fields are saved in the Portals and HostSettings tables in plain text. These un-encrypted username and password cause a significant security risk.    *Clear Steps to Reproduce:*  Save login credentials in browser.    Admin >> Site Settings >> Advanced Settings >> Payment Settings  Host >> Host Settings >> Basic Settings >> Payment Settings    Processor UserId and Processor Password are populated with the browser's saved login credentials.    Save/Update settings.    *Actual Result:*  Processor UserId and Password auto-populated with browser saved login credentials if fields are initially blank. Field values are saved as plain text in database.    *Expected Result:*  Fields not auto-populated by browser if blank. Password encrypted when saved in database."""

summary: "dnnModal shows incorrect height in mobile view"
description: """The dnnModal is used to display content for Login, Register and including Web pages from external sites. In particular, hosted payment pages is used to collect credit card information in an IFRAME uses the dnnModal for popup. It appears the recent dnnModal JS checks for mobile and started ignoring the height specified in the API call causing the pop up to display very narrowly in height on mobile devices. It may be ignoring the width too, but I didn't test it. Below is an example call to the API that supposedly allows you to pass the width and height but is now being ignored in mobile view:    dnnModal.show(""""http://somewhere.com"""", false, """"300px"""", """"200px"""", false);    Notice, the first attachment (BeforeCoffee.png) shows the incorrect height making it very difficult for customers to enter their credit card information for payment leading to lost sales. The 2nd attachment (AfterCoffee.png) shows what it should look like if it was properly sized.    In my opinion, I think the width and height should be left to the caller and not hardcoded by the API. Since it's javascript, the caller has many means to perform its own size detection (even if mobile) and knows to call the correct size to display for the type of content it needs.    I was able to workaround it by hardcoding the following rules in the portal CSS:    .dnnFormPopup.dnnFormPopupMobileView  {      height: 80% !important;  }    iframe#iPopUp  {      height: 100% !important;  }    """
comments: "[~accountid:<USER>, I've tried this on the latest platform build, and it seems to be working fine. I believe the problem here is that you are trying to call the method using strings to pass in the width and height. See attached photos.    You should be using dnnModal.show(""http://somewhere.com"", false, 300, 200, false);" [SEP] "hi Miguel    We're calling the function and passing numeric values and not as string. You can see the issue if you visit http://coffeerocket.com on a mobile device (or use the F12 to turn on mobile view -- i tried IPhone settings) and put any product to cart. Proceed to the checkout page, fill in a valid address, name, etc. Then under the Payment method section, select VISA/MC/AMEX click the *Add new* button and the popup will appear and you'll see the popup is too small.    If you load up the JS file here [http://coffeerocket.com/js/debug/dnn.modalpopup.js] responsible for the popup, you'll see there's a section of code that checks for if (!isMobile) and ignores the passed in dimen

summary: "Error when Page Setting is loaded up"

However, we would like to request to fix the root cause of the problem which is storing the data Enddate '31/12/9999 11:59:59 PM' in Database. Enddate column in Tabs and Modules table are now updated with '31/12/9999 11:59:59 PM'  due to the changes in DNN 7.4.2 when it's trying to create content item for the old DNN versions. We suppose it doesn't make sense to keep the maximum datetime value in Database when it's equivalent to NULL.   !Root Cause.png|thumbnail! Thanks and regards,  Nang Nang      """

summary: "[ENTER] key takes Admin or Host user to home page or in some cases another portal"
description: """When a module on a page has for instance a search box and an admin or host user presses [ENTER], he sometimes get's redirected to the homepage or in some cases even to another portal.  It took me a while to find what's going on, but I found it.  The go button in the control panel has  type """"submit"""" and on [ENTER], the first submit button get's activated.    This can simply be avoided by changing the type to """"button"""", although the styling does change a bit when you do that.  I did not notice it yet, but the language selector has the same issue it seems    I'll create a pull request for this and the styling.    On existing installations you can avoid this behavior by adding this JS to your skin:    jQuery(document).ready(function(){      $(""""#controlBar_SwitchSiteButton,#controlBar_SwitchLanguageButton"""").attr(""""type"""", """"button"""");     })    """

summary: "Support host-level OAuth authentication provider configuration"
description: """DNN installations with many sites sometimes want to share the API secret & key between those sites, instead of having to redefine them for each site."""
comments: "[~accountid:<USER> Can you please explain steps for our QA team to be able to test your changes. " [SEP] "Brian, Could you please provide sufficient details and steps to verify the fix so this issue can tested in QA?    Thanks!" [SEP] "After reviewing the changes a little more closely, I'm not sure that this change covers all of the scenarios that it should.  We've used it for a client to create a custom OAuth authentication provider which always stores its config at the host level, but it doesn't look like it probably supports an auth provider to be configurable about where it stores its config.  I'm waiting to see if our client is willing to put more time into testing and extending this solution.     [~accountid:557058:c87b0188-6649-4168-a5b7-ea5074701c39], do you have any thoughts on what it would look like to support, for example, the Facebook auth provider with optional host-level config?" summary: "Support empty ""meta"" JS library packages"
description: """DNN should support JS library packages without their own script, but which just pull in dependencies.  This mostly works already, but causes a {{script}} tag to be included for the empty library, causing a 404 request.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Download all packages from https://github.com/EngageSoftware/DNN-JavaScript-Libraries/releases/tag/jQuery.cycle_2.1.5 which have a numeric prefix  # Copy those packages to the {{\Install\JavaScriptLibrary}} folder of your DNN website  # Hit the {{/Install/Install.aspx?mode=installresources}} URL of your DNN website  # Ensure script combination is turned off  # Install attached module which requests the {{jQuery.cycle2}} JS library    h3. Expected Results  * All dependent libraries included on the page  * No script requests that result in a 404    h3. Actual Results  * Request for the jQuery.cycle2 library folder (e.g. {{/Resources/libraries/jQuery.cycle2/02_01_05/?cdv=91}}), which results in a 404"""

summary: "Non US-English Installations fail"
description: """At 18% the installation stops with this error: The conversion of a varchar data type to a datetime data type resulted in an out-of-range value. This happens on non US-English SQL Servers.    Reason: At the end of the script _08.00.00.29.SqlServerProvider_, there is this statement:    IF OBJECT_ID(N'{databaseOwner}[{objectQualifier}Tabs]', N'U') IS NOT NULL  UPDATE {databaseOwner}[{objectQualifier}Tabs]  SET EndDate = NULL  WHERE EndDate >= '9999-12-31'  GO    It can be fixed by either adding a line (SET LANGUAGE US_ENGLISH) before this statement, or better by changing it to    IF OBJECT_ID(N'{databaseOwner}[{objectQualifier}Tabs]', N'U') IS NOT NULL  UPDATE {databaseOwner}[{objectQualifier}Tabs]  SET EndDate = NULL  WHERE EndDate >= '9999-12-31T00:00:00'  GO    """
comments: "Pull request #1275 submitted to fix it." [SEP] "Thank you, Sebastian :-)" [SEP] "This is merged. Note for QA - You need non-English SQL Server to test this." [SEP] "Verified in Platform 801 Build 119 in the VM machine having non English SQL server its working fine    The Issue occur in 8.0.0  is :   !Errore occur in 8.0.png|thumbnail!     And issue resolved in 8.0.1 Please refer to screenshot    !resolved issue screenshot.png|thumbnail! "

summary: "SI: 404 error page - redirect loop (status/404)"
description: """Default 404 error page handling throws redirect loop when page is unpublished and have no content.    +*Steps to reproduce:*+    1. Add new page '404Error' and select page view permission to all users (don't add any content and leave it unpublished.  2. Go to Site Settings -> Advanced Settings -> Page Management  3. Specify a page as the '404Error' Page and click update button  4. Navigate to a URL which does not exist, i.e. www.yourtestsite.com/asdfasdfasdfasdfasfdadsf  5. You will see the redirect page.    The error occurs in PageBase and the system will add """"status=404"""" in URL which causes a redirect loop.     !2016-01-21_13-44-37.jpg|thumbnail!   """
comments: "This is the expected behavior, until there is a published version of any page you will get these results. The reason it goes into a loop is the page they can't view is the unpublished one. We won't fix this, its an education issue. "

summary: "Add ability to lock a site"
description: """We've made some customizations for a client which we think would be a good fit for inclusion in the core.  There's a """"Lock Site"""" action added to the control bar, which allows a super-user to turn off modifications to the site (probably for entering some sort of maintenance mode).  For our client, the """"good enough"""" way this is implemented is to disable the module actions whenever the site is locked (in addition, custom modules are able to access the locked status via a property on {{PortalSettings}} and do any additional changes, if desired).    Testing Notes:  - Test exposed on/off button in the control panel  - When on, verify that it disables module actions (for any user)"""

summary: "Invalid class in Core Messaging for action buttons"
description: """If you go to the messaging /notification section of a current DNN site. You'll see that the links for """"Compose New Message"""" and """"Dismiss All Messages"""" are wrapped in a DIV with a class of """"dnnFormExpandContent"""" which is intended to be used for the tops of Panels. Default.css specifies, .dnnFormExpandContent > a, to have color of #333 (very dark grey) so that it doesn't look like a clickable link in this context.    It seems like """"Compose New Message"""" and """"Dismiss All Messages"""" should very clearly be links, perhaps even with a class of dnnPrimaryAction, so that it's clear they are actions. Instead, in most skins, it will appear as plain, almost black, text and it's not at all clear that it is an action.    The corresponding code can be found in /DesktopModules/CoreMessaging/View.ascx. The dnnFormExpandContent class should be removed from the DIV that wraps the a tag with class of ComposeMessage (line 27) and the a tag for DismissAllNotifications (line 218)"""

summary: "Site Export Fails When Files Have Special Characters"
description: """If you export a site and include files as part of it, if the files contain special characters this will result in an error and the .resources file (the zip file that contains the portals directory) will result in a 0 KB file. This most likely is some sort of encoding issue for the template XML itself (the .template file). Characters to look/test for:  - ' (single quote/apostrophe) - This is the one we know broke it; see comments below  - """" (double quote)  - + (plus)  - = (equal)  - ( or ) (parentheses)  """
comments: "I have done multiple site imports and exports in 7.4.2 (multiple DNN/Evoq products and installs) and I haven't been able to reproduce the problem. Also, there was much testing around this in 8.0 and we didn't see a problem. The .resources files is simply the content of the portals directory that is registered in the file manager (that isn't associated w/ a Users folder, for example) so it is possible it is a successful export and that file size is 0. " [SEP] "Here's a video of my attempt. http://www.screencast.com/t/JdKY8ZAoMTL    Here is my error message: Error: Add New Site is currently unavailable. summary: "Review usages of lists of image file type extensions"
description: """We have the Globals.glbImageFileTypes property, which can be used to retrieve the list of defined extensions for image file types.    In several places in the core, there are different hard coded lists of image extensions. These usages must be reviewed, and if possible, be replaced to use the global property.    Places where this has been detected:  - DotNetNuke.Web.InternalServices.FileUploadController  - DotNetNuke.Services.GeneratedImage.StartTransform.SecureFileTransform  - DotNetNuke.Services.GeneratedImage.StartTransform.UserProfilePicTransform  - DotNetNuke.Services.UserProfile.UserProfilePicHandler  - DotNetNuke.Modules.Journal.FileUploadController  - DotNetNuke.Modules.Journal.ServicesController  - DotNetNuke.Providers.RadEditorProvider.ImageTester    Also it can be useful to add helper functions to check this list of images. For example the {{IsImageExtension}} method has been copy+pasted in several places.    Note: in some places the ICO extension is hard coded. This should be removed (ref DNN-7809)"""

summary: "Add SVG as default image extensions"
description: """Currently, we have the Globals.glbImageFileTypes property, which is returning a comma separated list of image extensions. By default, this list is: jpg,jpeg,jpe,gif,bmp,png    In Host > Lists > ImageTypes, a user can edit this list    The type SVG should be added as images by default.    Items to work on:  - Change the hard coded default list in Globals.cs.glbImageFileTypes  - Add the types to the {{Lists}} SQL table in the upgrade script    """
comments: "Should also add TIFF ? - This extension has been used in other places in Platform" [SEP] "I would like us to identify anywhere TIFF and ICO are used in Platform code (see DNN-8364). That said, I want only SVG to be the one we support and the others aren't supported by default (in addition to the other image defaults such as jpeg, gif, etc.). " [SEP] "Duplicated by SI (DNN-9963)"

summary: "Improve Journal_Save to support updates"
description: """Journal_save does not support updating a Journal entry, which might be useful, e.g. if a Forum post is updated. AF works around by deleting and re-adding the entry, but this doesn't seem to be reliable (https://github.com/ActiveForums/ActiveForums/issues/352), maybe due to a race condition. It would be better, if Journal_save would support updating items (and maybe Journal even gets an """"edit"""" button, allowing users to fix spelling issues...)"""

summary: "using two different modules on a single page causes one of them to fail"
description: """This was reported in dnn forums: http://www.dnnsoftware.com/forums/forumid/203/threadid/526207/scope/posts  Create two new modules from the mvc templates.  install them on a website and drop on same page.  You will see that one of them can not load and throws exception as show in image MVC.png"""
comments: "The issue was happening due to MVC cache key and rendering step for the mvc module.  Since MVC is designed to work on single site architecture and does not expect multiple modules and views folders, the cache key has been modified to include the namespace in it so that each module is treated differently. Also the call render module has been moved from onload to oninit because in onload case the if the page has different modules and then the view locations are overwritten to the last module at the time of onload. So all the other modules are not found and throws exception.    PR Created.    [~accountid:<USER> please review the PR." [SEP] "Code review done, it looks good.    Available in build #8.0.1.97." [SEP] "Verified ability to build both module in VS 2015 Community  Verified both modules can be installed in any 7.4.1 or later version of DNN products  Verified you may have more than 1 version of SPA or MVC on any page or mix n' match more than 1 version of both SPA/MVC on a page  Platform 8.0.1 build 108;
