summary: "DnnImageHandlers > Enhance IPCount mechanism to avoid file locking"
description: """The dnnImageHandler IPCount mechanism to prevent DoS attack write files under AppData. In 8.0.0 regression we have found some locking issues and this locking can be more aggressive in web farm scenario (see related issue DNN-8086).    The goal of this improvement is change the mechanism to use memory (i.e.: cache) or database to provide a more roboust solution.    *Important*  Caching should be the first option as we want have a very high performance mechanism. Database would imply a considerable number of read/writes that could hit performance. In that case we should ensure that the solution does not impact the site overall performance.  If we use a system based on cache we should consider web farm scenario.   """

summary: "File permissions check should be performed before locking the installation."
description: """Currently when we perform new install a lock file in created (installblocker.lock) in the root of the website folder. This file is used to check the install in progress.     Currently this file is being created before the file permission check. This will create issue in scenario when file permissions are failed. summary: "Profanity filter definition issues"
description: """The following problems have been detected trying to define profanity filters.    By default, this filter is defined in a clean install:  Text=FindThisText, Value=ReplaceWithNothing    Note that Text can have duplicates, but Value can't.    In the case we would need to define filters so several prohibited words change to """"censored"""", we would need to define:  - Text=ProhibitedWord1, Value=censored  - Text=ProhibitedWord2, Value=censored  This is not possible, as value can't have duplicates    In the other hand, it is possible to create other filters like:  - Text=SomeProhibitedWord, Value=aaaa  - Text=SomeProhibitedWord, Value=bbbb  In this case, it is not well defined to which value, the appareances of 'SomeProhibitedWord' will be replaced to.    Summarizing, for profanity, the duplicate check would need to be done for Text, not for Value."""

[SEP] "Submitted PR https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1235.    Changed code done in DNN-6919 to ensure that incremental cleanup and config are executed in the right step. The install process is done in 4 steps: Script, Application, Cleanup, Config Updates. The incrementals Cleanup and Config were executed inside the Scripts step. So 7.x config undo config done in 8.0.0.x configs as the order of execution was incorrect. description: """steps:    1. Create a role called Tester and add RU and assign RU to this role  2. Create a page and add permission for Tester role to Allow and Edit page  3. Actual result:    Page export causes the following exception in the log and on the screen shows that an error has occurred:    """
comments: "After debugging the problem is in the SP 'GetFoldersByPermissions', which has a known issue, DNN-6567. It returns wrong folders, so the popup for exporting the page shows folders that are not accessible by the user. So when user tries to export, he gets a permission error in logs related to the access to the 'Templates' folder for saving the page.     Reviewing the code, this function is only used to get the 'Templates' folder and check if is accesible. If the folder is accesible it will be used as default folder. To avoid the issue, we can directly retrieve the 'Templates' folder and then check the permissions of the user. This way we avoid the problem and it will also have a better performance.    [~accountid:<USER>, could you please review my changes?  https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1234    *Note for QA:*   After this fix the 'Templates' folder won't be visible by the user at least the 'WRITE' permission has been added to the user:  # Go to Admin -> File Management.  # Assign 'WRITE' per

summary: "Job 'Search: Site Crawler' is leaving open sql connections when an unhandled exception is thrown and crashing web site"
description: """In several of our sites, there was a scheduled job within DNN called 'Search: Site Crawler' that was enabled and running every minute. Under certain condition the job had an unhandled exception that led to the error below (taken from the log file on DNN site):        Site were published in Azure Site using SQL Azure Databases.    It appears that when the exception was thrown the database connections was left open and therefore exhausting very quickly connection to the database.    On one single day the log file grew up to 835 MB with the exact same error written thousands of times.     !image-2016-01-07-09-33-29-863.png|thumbnail!     The job was disabled for now. summary: "Upgrade should not be paused/failed if some site pages missing. "
description: """  This is a legecy problem and this is an improvement request based on CONTENT-5666     Upon developer's investigation, during upgrading, somehow it will try to check out admin page. However, if certain page(s) access is failed or page(s) lost, even in the child site(s), the upgrading will be failed.     And if this happened, Support will be the cost center for sure to get calls from customer.     Improvements:   The upgrading process should be stronger and more error tolerable.     Investigation might be needed:   - Why check for admin page necessary?  - How many other dependencies to cause the upgrading paused because of missing pages. """

summary: "User Profile > Deleted Empty Entries upon Upgrade"
description: """When a user goes and updates their profile setting, we used to create a lot of empty entries in the UserProfile table. These entries were created even when user did not fill in the value in UI.     We made a change in 7.4.2 (I can't seem to locate the Jira), where we no longer create empty entries. However, we do not remove them during upgrade.    We need to remove those entries during upgrade. Older sites with large users have millions of such records. This creates problems in backup and restore of the database."""
comments: "Batch update of issues that won't be addressed due to change in functionality from version 9.0"

summary: "Add New Scheduled Task to Purge Event Viewer"
description: """As of now the Admin > Event Viewer purges old record when module is being loaded. When the table EventLog grows big, the deletion starts to time out. Over a period of time, table grows even bigger as deletion no longer happens.    We need to create a scheduled task that deletes content of this table (based on the settings that admin has specified in the event viewer module). The schedule task must do deletion in batches (say 10,000 records), to prevent time outs.    One run of scheduled task should do 10 loops of 10,000 records in each loop - so a maximum of 100,000 records in one run.    Ideally the loop count and batch size should be read from hidden schedule-item setting that admin can manually override using SQL. This is a very standard practice in Evoq (the settings)."""
comments: "We actually have this capability in the Purge Event Log. This is a task today that is disabled by default. You can also choose the amount of history per log entry type to keep (via Admin -> Event Viewer). "

summary: """Add New Module"" needs more info"
description: """When a user is adding a module to a page there are a few more things he/she is likely to desire:  # A 1-2 sentence description of the module, something more than just the module name. (I, for one, can't recall the functionality from the name.)  # Some idea of the lineage/heritage of the module: is it associated with the Blog Extension, 2Sxcy, Document Exchange, etc. (Many of these extensions have a myriad modules, but there is no way to know their parent). Maybe this can be put into the Admin or Host's Extension page (which many page designers can't access), but what's the URL to learn more about it: the distributor/creator/documentation/version number.    Soooo, in addition to the useful """"Drag this onto the page"""" tooltip, can we also have a bit more info added below that with read-only info about the source of the module and where to learn more?"""

summary: "Remove cookies and unwanted headers from WEB API responses"
description: """The WEB API responses are revealing too much by sending back the cookies with each response. The cookies (and other unnecessary headers; e.g., {{Server}}) are unusable, expose sensitive information, are security risk, and increase the response size. Therefore, these headers should be removed from all WEB API responses."""

summary: "FileSystemPermissionVerifier exception in platform after installation"
description: """It happened after installation, and this exception only in platform, didn't see it in the build 2 days ago, and didn't see it in Content and Engage.       """
comments: "PR => https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1554    This is hard to reproduce issue, but the source was apparent as a race condition between two threads during checking file/folder permission checks where both create a file/folder and both delete at the same time and one of them succeeds while the other fails. I changed this to be done on a single thread through locking to ensure no other threads interfere during the checking process.    There is nothing QA can do to check this, but it will be validated by the cloud team after this being merged during their test installation. Also, our automation tests will validate this as the issue appears about 20% of the times in automated installations." [SEP] "Changes looks good and merged in 9.0.0.92 build." [SEP] "Couldn't see this exception in recent builds anymore, close as it is. "

summary: "Modify IConnector interface in order to get more info regarding settings, like Secure settings"
description: """Modify IConnector interface in order to get more info regarding settings, like Secure settings  After fixing issue SOCIAL-4532, we have introduce a way to setup Secure settings and allow UI to draw passowrd-type input.  However, this approach requires that developers must know that the UI is using a default setting since current implementation only is able to get the value of each setting    We should modify the IConnector interface to expose a method that gets more info regarding each setting such as Secure. and simplify the creation and maintenance of Connectors."""
comments: "IMO we doesn't need modify the IConnector interface in general, the UI are controlled by each connector them-self.    one solution on this requirement is in GetConfig, we can set the value to be: ""{value:***, type: ***}"" in GetConfig method. and parse the JSON data in the connector implemention, also bind the control as needed.    send to [~accountid:<USER> for confirm." [SEP] "Agreed, no work needed at the moment. We may revisit this later. We need to close it as won't fix for now." [SEP] "Based on above comments , this JIRA will be closed, no changes implemented"

comments: "This was from an email from [~accountid:<USER>:  ""I was reviewing the issue. The issue is related to the HTML module settings. I was able to reproduce it and the problematic setting is ‘HtmlText_UseDecorate’. The value for this setting in 7.3.4 is ""1"" but this value isn’t casted properly in 8.0.0, so we get these errors. In 8.0.0 this boolean settings are stored as “True"" or “False"". As workaround the user can set this setting to “True” in the modules that they are having problems. """ [SEP] "The problem was related to how the new module settings repository works. In Platform 7.3.4 some boolean module settings were stored in database as ""1"" or ""0"". With the new implementation, it only allows to parse string as boolean if their value is ""True"" or ""False"", otherwise, it is not able to convert the value into a boolean.     In this scenario, the HTML module had a boolean setting ""HtmlText_UseDecorate"" that in some scenarios had set. Since all the settings for a module is retrieved at

summary: "RGR - BLOCKED - Workflow: Lost Publish button again. "
description: """Noticed when testing for CK editor and I believe the workflow is missing here. I remember it was fixed back to couple weeks ago... Based on the feedback from Behzad, looks like it only happens on Cloud, not with local install.     http://dnn8y6hqy.qa.evoqondemand.com    - Log in as Host,   - Go create a new page or activate an editor editing mode and add some words.     Result:   There is no Publish button showing up, only """"Close"""" button.     Expected:  As default workflow, it should display """"Publish"""" button in front of """"Close"""" button.     """
comments: "The problem is that the Portal 0 (the default one) has not enabled the settings for workflow and versioning, so the Publish and Cancel buttons are not shown:     !No_setting_workflow_versions.png|thumbnail!     This is caused by a known bug that is currently being fixed on DNN-7570. The code that enables the versioning and the workflow in the existing portals is executed before portal 0 is created. Thus, it is not set correctly. (For the rest of the portals, the settings are set just in the moment of creation and they have the settings).     Once finished issue DNN-7570 this issue should be fixed as well." [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> you mentioned in today's scrum that it might be fixed. Can you please have a look." [SEP] "Hi Pedro, I believe this one already fixed, and I tried on Content 8.3.0.303, the publish button is back.   I saw there is a dependency issue attached with it, so I will leave it to you for the decision, if we can close it or have to wait for DNN-7570 and to validate again.   Thanks. " [SEP]

Steps to reproduce: Open any page in Edit mode --> Edit any HTML Pro Module --> Switch to Advanced Editor --> Rick Text Editor --> Switch to HTML mode --> Paste this sample HTML
--> Switch to Design/Preview and then switch back to the HTML mode --> Now the HTML will be changed to(See the two single quotes around the URL) :
--> Now again switch to Design/Preview and then switch back to the HTML mode --> The HTML will change to -->
Work Around: Use Basic Text Box, but the disadvantage is that user can't see the preview/design simultaneously while making the changes in HTML."""
comments: "We have changed the editor used as part of Platform 8 (and Evoq 8.3). We tried to reproduce the problem in the new editor and everything worked as expected. The customer will need to change their editor provider to CK Editor from the Telerik RadEditor provider to avoid this issue going forward. "

summary: "SSL Enable and Enforced in Site Settings does not work"
description: """steps:    1. Setup a site with legitimate SSL certificate in IIS with https and SSL cert  2. Install a 8.0 site and as host login   3. Go to Admin > Site Settings and check both SSL Enabled and SSL Enforced  4. Create a page that is accessible by anonymous users  5. "

summary: "Memory Cacheing does NOT work woth multiple langauges"
description: """The Memory caching provider does not cache websites that run multiple languages.    I've created this problem with a fresh install of DNN7.4.2, using a single language en-US the caching provider works OK.    If you have a website with  fr-FR   en-US  en-GB (fallback to system default)    The caching stops working.    The function  DotNetNuke.Common.Utilities.DataCache.SetCache(cacheKey, objObject, absoluteExpiration);    Works and the data store with the cachekey can be retrieved directly after the setting of the cache, however on page refresh the GetCache() function returns null.    If en-GB and en-US are disactivated, then the caching again works correctly.    Re-activating en-GB or en-US will stop the caching from working again.    If File caching option is selected in Host Settings all caching works as expected.    A change in DNN 7.2.2 sets the default to memory (DNN-26708) , a quick fix is to revert back to the old file caching default should be done, until a good solution can be implemented."""

summary: "Pages > Link-URL: Quit adding http:// if URL starts with ""#"""
description: """When building """"One Page Design"""" sites with DNN (quite popular in times of tables and mobile devices), it's a common task to point your page-URLs (=menu items) to text anchors (ex. """"#section1"""", """"#section2"""",..) inside the current page.    The URL-picker component in the Page > Link-URL dialog keeps adding a """"http://"""" to the beginning of the """"URL"""". This behavior forces the user to pick an absolute path to make the link work in the generated menu (""""http://www.myurl.com/#section1""""). If you only enter a relative """"#section1"""" as """"URL"""", the component will render the link as """"http://#section1"""", which will lead to a broken link.    In scenarios where there are multiple site-aliases, this turns out to be a little annoying, since it forces the user to switch to the """"primary"""" domain (or the one defined in the menu), as soon as he clicks on one of the menu items (=text anchors in this scenario).    Solution: Make the URL-dialog of the Link-Component a little less strict: Let the user add text-anchors (""""#section1"""") without 'automatically' adding a 'http://' in front of the URL, at least, if the """"URL"""" starts with """"#"""".    What do you think?"""
comments: "Batch update of issues that won't be addressed due to change in functionality from version 9.0"

summary: "Implement Moniker API for Service Framework "
description: """A moniker is a simple name provided for an objects that simplifies identifying specific object instances.  This concept was first used in Microsoft Object Linking and Embedding (OLE), and the Component Object Model (COM).      The DNN Services Framework was first introduced in DNN 6.2.  When creating web services with the Service Framework, it is common to require callers to provide the ModuleId and TabId as part of the web request header.  This requirement complicates the programming model since service endpoints become tied to their specific location in a website.  Modules are able to easily retrieve the ModuleId and TabId from the service framework as part of the initial module rendering.  However, this mechanism is not available for remote callers, where the UI rendering is not directly controlled by DNN.    In order to encapsulate this context data and to simplify the programming model for web services, we should introduce the concept of a moniker.  The DNN service moniker will provide a mechanism for mapping a simple name to a specific module instance.  This moniker can then be passed to a web service in place of the ModuleId, TabId or any other context data specific to a module.    The moniker should be implemented as an additional system TabModule setting. By default, the moniker will be composed of the page name, the module definition friendly name and the TabModuleId separated by /.  When composing the default moniker, all spaces should be replaced with dashes.  For example, if the Social Groups module was on the “Community” page with TabModuleId of 100, the default moniker would be “Community/Social-Groups/100”.      The default moniker implementation provides a human friendly method to identify the page and module being addressed and also a unique element to differentiate between multiple modules on the same page.  The core platform should expose a service endpoint that allows an application to query for monikers related to a specific module type that the current user has permissions to view.    When calling service endpoints that need a specific module context, the moniker will be included as part of the service call.  The service can then use the moniker to easily lookup any context for the module instance associated with the moniker.    *Developer Notes:*  The purpose of this step is to help the back-end to create the DNN context for the web API request. Therefore, it is not tied to any authentication mechanism (like JWT, Basic, Forms, etc.)    Injecting the moniker in the query part of the API request requires modifying all existing API’s that need to use this moniker  but is not feasible and requires making changes to existing core (which we want to completely avoid). What we need is a non-invasive method. One way in which this can be implemented is by adding a new vendor specific header in the request as an alternate way to using the {{TabID}} and {{ModuleID}} that are in use today. Assume we are going to use {{X-DNN-MONIKER}} for this purpose. An example of a full header would be:  {{X-DNN-MONIKER: John’s Blog}}    Before being able to use this feature, there is a need for an API that returns the various monikers associated with a specific module. Let’s say in an EVOQ Engage site if we need to find all the monikers that use the discussions module, say discussions (i.e., all tabs/pages that have the discussions module in them), we will send an API request that returns all of these monikers associated with discussion modules placed on various pages. This call may return zero, one, or more monikers.    There must be a consideration for user rights in every data returned from the API; no private data should be revealed to an un-authorized user."""
comments: "there is no need for tabID or moduledefinitionid, both are identified by TabModuleID.  The moniker should be able to identify the module to direct the request to the appropriate module." [SEP] "This feature is ready for testing after merging the PR." [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> built a sample mobile app to use this feature. The source code of the mobile app is here: https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform.Samples.Mobile. There is not much for QA to verify any further. This issue now can be closed." [SEP] "Verified in:  * Platform 8.0.0.798  * Content Upgrade from 8.0 to 8.3.0.376  * Engage 8.3.0.376 (clean install and upgrade)    For Engage, API for create comment was tested in Answers module. For Platform, and Content (Upgrade), DAM module's GetFolderContent API was tested."

summary: "SMTP: Adjust default setting for SMTP client for idletime and connectionlimit "
description: """The default DNN Maxidletime is stopping emails from working.  The default on a fresh install should be the same as the a.Net default.  100000.      https://dnntracker.atlassian.net/browse/DNN-21235"""
comments: "We ran into a complicate issue since DNN 7.3.0 with SmtpClient that reuses a closed connection/socked randomly. summary: "Update Newtonsoft to the latest version"
description: """Updating to the latest version will allow the use of SignalR 2 for developers without jumping through many hoops. It also makes sense to stay up to date with 3rd party libraries."""
comments: "These are the major changes from the version 4.5.1 to 7.0.1 (extracted from http://james.newtonking.com/ and https://allmychanges.com/p/c%23/Newtonsoft.Json/):  * 7.0:  ** DiscriminatedUnionConverter performance improvements.  * 6.0:  ** Annotations.  ** Support to ASP.NET CoreCLR.  ** Memory Usage Optimizations.  ** Dependency Injection.  ** JSONPath  * 5.0:  ** Immutable Collections.  ** Round-trip Extension Data.  ** DefaultSettings  ** Performance    No breaking changes have been detected.    Local installation scenarios tested:  * platform 8.0.0.  * platform 7.4.2 + upgrade to 8.0.0." [SEP] "Merged into development. Conflicts were due to some modules that were deleted (Dynamic Content Manager and Viewer). Some additional references had to be updated. Available in build #8.0.0.713.    Installation scenarios with TC packages:  * platform 8.0.0 installation.  * platform 7.4.2 + upgrade to 8.0.0.    *Note for QA*  The changes are related to upgrade Newtonsoft library, a JSON framework for .NET, to the lates

summary: "SI: Random exceptions of RegEx engine in log file during load testing"
description: """Noticed this in Engage 8.3.0.218 during load testing.     Per discussion with George's, this should be a bug. It refers to when RegEx engine trying to load or parsing a page, but took too long and timed-out.     The error might not be easily reproducible, and the error might happened during the automated activity trying LogIn --> Visit """"Activity-Feed"""", which also triggered User Friendly URL functions.     It will be great if any developer can drill down to this issue and if it could be fixed or handled smoothly.       """
comments: "This is not easy to reproduce but may related to the default timeout of regex:  public static Regex GetCachedRegex(string pattern, RegexOptions options = RegexOptions.None, int {color:red}timeoutSeconds = 2{color})    if in some edge case the server is very busy/slow, it could possible to process more than 2 seconds and cause timeout exception, IMO we may can add an app setting in web.config to override the timeout value, then if customer really meet this problem, they can add/update the setting and set a large value to avoid the problem. assign to [~accountid:<USER> to take deep look, thx." [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> I recall you had made the Regex definitions (or constructors) static so they didn't have to be created all the time. Will that help here? What are your thoughts about the web.config setting proposed by [~accountid:<USER>?" [SEP] "The reason behind making them static was for performance reasons (no need to create each time when used). The timeout 2 seconds is huge in terms of compute resources ev

summary: "Page Management - Some areas in the UI are left justified"
description: """*Screencast.com*   http://screencast.com/t/PC9GIhNvA    Install or upgrade to latest Platform 8 build (I used 634)  Log in as Host or Administrator  Go Admin > Page Management  Open each area and view the UI    *Actual Result*  Some areas of the UI are left justified  Some areas of the UI are not properly algined - Small & large icon areas of Page Management    Appearance    *Expected Result*  UI should match Platform 7.4.2"""
comments: "[~accountid:<USER>, I chose to include this fix in default.css and put it under the dnnLabel selector as I noticed many alignment issues with dnnLabels. This JIRA is just another example of one of them. See attached image for another example in Adding users.    With the new changes to default.css, items that are more dynamic in size (width: 30%, padding: 50px), no longer align themselves properly and are pushing the items next to them down or right. Adding box-sizing border box changes the way padding is calculated, and instead of expanding outwards, it expands inwards, which fixes these alignment issues.    Let me know if you think this fix is OK. Thanks!     !case1.PNG|thumbnail! " [SEP] "Reviewed and merged build 8.0.0.647" [SEP] "verified in Platform  8.0.0 build 662 and its align properly    Please refer to screencast : http://screencast.com/t/7kf5xrwCg1I    we are closing the issue"

summary: "Remove DotNetNuke.Services.UserProfile.UserProfilePicHandler"
description: """With the new dnnImageHandlers in place we decided to remove from the platform the code of the old user profile picture handler: *DotNetNuke.Services.UserProfile.UserProfilePicHandler*"""
comments: "Hi [~accountid:<USER>/[~accountid:<USER>, I am no longer sure we want or when remove the old UserProfilePicHandler code.     This task was created to remove all the code from platform related to this component, while the http handler was already been removed from the web.config during the upgrade to Platform 8.0.0 as part of the integration of the new dnnImageHandler (see DNN-6506).  During 8.0.0 regression we found that Catalyst was hardcoding the old User Profile Url Picture in some modules (not using the Platform API to obtain the new one) so we finally revert this change (see DNN-8323).    IMO we should remove it as we cannot leave dead code forever in the Platform, but we need to figure out when do that and what how communicate that to the community/customer ecosystem to minimize the effect of this breaking change. Thoughts?" [SEP] "IMO the old pic handler should be treated like the Telerik library and moved to a deprecated library to be removed in a later version. DNN core should stop using it."

summary: "UI - Host > Extensions - Module Definition collapses when trying to creating a new module definition"
description: """Steps:    1. Login as Host  2. Go to Host > Extensions and click on Edit icon for any of the extensions  3. Scroll to Module Definitions section and expand it  4. Click to Add New Definition    Expected result:    Module definition remains expanded with fields for user to fill in.    Actual result:    Please watch: http://screencast.com/t/n2MscCGzXD    The module definition collapses and user is confused where to add the new definitions."""
comments: "[~accountid:<USER>, the issue with this is that a postback is being done. Unlike how knockout handles these things, when the add definition button is clicked a postback is sent and the entire page is re-rendered; hence the closed module definition dropdown. After speaking with Amarjit, it's not easy to fix this issue and our suggestion is to mark this as Won't Fix. What do you think?" [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> The dnnPanels jQuery plugin should utilize cookies to remember what was expanded/collapsed even in post back situations.    http://www.dnnsoftware.com/wiki/dnnpanels-jquery-plugin " [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER>, thanks for the link, that helped identify the problem. I've added a fix that adds an ID to the h2 tag so that the cookie can be saved when the dnnPanels function is called." [SEP] "Verified and fixed in Platform v8.0 build 689, Content v8.3 build 208 and Engage v8.3 build 244."

summary: "Host pages have invalid EndDate when first accessed"
description: """After creating a new site, when you access pages under the Host menu (e.g. {{//Host}} or {{//Host//SQL}}), some incorrect information gets saved for the page.  Specifically, the End Date is set to _12/31/9999 23:59:59_ (i.e. {{DateTime.MaxValue}}), which causes an error to show up when trying to access the page's settings:    {quote}{quote}    I believe that the {{EndDate}} property is being set as part of [setting up the {{ActiveTab}} property of {{PortalSettings}}|https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/blob/a27a2927aac16ca169258494005398ca4fb1c5fb/DNN%20Platform/Library/Entities/Portals/PortalSettingsController.cs#L94].  In addition to {{EndDate}}, other columns that are set include {{SkinSrc}}, {{ContainerSrc}}, {{IconFileLarge}}, and {{ContentItemID}} (not to mention the audit/version columns that always update).    I believe that the lack of an associated Content Item is probably what is triggering the saving of the {{TabInfo}} with bad data, but I haven't been able to track down exactly where that part is happening.    h2. Steps to Reproduce   # Install a new DNN site   # Go to a host page (e.g. Host or SQL)   # Go to Page Settings for that page    h3. Expected Results   * Settings show in pop-up    h3. Actual Results   * Error shows in pop-up"""
comments: "This issue is fixed in Platform v8.0 build 734.    The problem was that page was crashing and this does not happen. However accessing Page Setting for a Host page such as Host Settings opens the pop-up but the End-date is still set to 12/31/9999 11:59 PM. Which after talking to Amarjit is fine and is not a problem.    Closing this issue.  "

summary: "Add Dnn.LabelFor extension which will show labelText and helpText from property metadata"
description: """Currently the Dnn.LabelFor has 3 overloads as follows:  LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression, string labelText, string helpText)    LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression, string labelText, string helpText, object htmlAttributes)    LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression, string labelText, string helpText, IDictionary<string, object> htmlAttributes)    New:  LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression)  LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression, object htmlAttributes)  LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression, IDictionary<string, object> htmlAttributes)  LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression, string helpText)  LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression, string helpText, object htmlAttributes)  LabelFor(Expression<Func<TModel, TValue>> expression, string helpText, IDictionary<string, object> htmlAttributes)    These overloaded methods should take the labelText and helpText from the property metadata values specified in the Model."""
comments: "DNN-8075 - Added the overloaded methods.  They will pick the labelText and helpText from Model properties metadata." [SEP] "Verified both code & behavior in Platform 8 build 633 clean installation & upgrade  "

Add Edit permissions to Registered User on that page  # Add a HTML Module  # As Registered User, go to page created in the above step  # Edit page  # Go to Edit Content for the module created above  # In the Editor, click on Image icon    _Actual_  Beside the URL field, user should see the Browse Server button since he has been allowed, but the button does not appear. summary: "CKEditor should set as default Html Provider when installed as package from the DNN Connect Repo"
description: """In Platform 8.0.0 CKEditor will be shipped as default html provider in new install. However in upgrades we won't change the current html provider of the upgraded site (see DNN-8047).    Said that, we need to mantain this behavior and in the other hand ensure that when the CKEditor provider installed from the DNNConnect repository, this will be set automatically as default html provider.    *Step to Reproduce*  * Install Platform 7.4.2  * Install CKEditor from the DNNConnect Repository (see https://ci.appveyor.com/project/OliverHine/ckeditorprovider/branch/development/artifacts to get the zip package)    *Actual Result*  The provider is installed but not set as default html provider    *Expected Result*  The provider is installed and set as default html provider    *Note for DEV and QA*: as part of this issue we should regress DNN-8047 to ensure that the platform 8.0.0 behavior is mantained for new installs and upgrades."""
comments: "Talked with Chris that move this issue into next release to find out a better solution."

summary: "Implement Trace Statements in Dnn Platform Code"
description: """+Background+    It can be difficult to do performance troubleshooting on a live running Dnn site, if some specific module is not performing well.    This change is to add in trace output statements to the codebase, so that anyone can hook up any trace listener (such as Dnn Glimpse) and determine which components on a page are running slowly.    As the Log4Net component provides a trace listener, it can be leveraged for this purpose and have the output directed via the log4net configuration.    +Requirements+  * Implement the trace statements in code as provided  * Add the required log4Net configuration change to this JIRA as an example  * Test by checking with a trace listener such as Glimpse"""
comments: "In order to use Log4Net to trace these particular messages in {{System.Diagnostics.Trace}}, this is what we have to set up in the file {{DotNetNuke.log4net.config}}:  * First, there will be another appender for this type of traces:  {code:xml}  <appender name=""TraceAppender"" type=""log4net.Appender.TraceAppender"">      <layout type=""log4net.Layout.PatternLayout"">          <conversionPattern value=""%date - %message%newline"" />      </layout>  </appender>  {code}  * Second, we have to add a Logger, to log those particular traces:  {code:xml}  <logger name=""DNN.Trace"" additivity=""false"">      <level value=""DEBUG"" />      <appender-ref ref=""TraceAppender"" />  </logger>  {code}    With these changes, if we want to send traces to the {{System.Diagnostics.Trace}} environment by using log4net, this is what we have to do:  {code:java}  DnnLogger.GetLogger(""DNN.Trace"").Debug(""This is a test"");  {code}    The primary purpose of these trace statements is to collect data on performance when loading pag

summary: "Commented CDF code in page behind"
description: """Take a look at the attached screenshot, I see a lot of commented code in the code-behind. I use the standard .zip install package (not dev compiled)."""
comments: "DNN-8050 The comments will be injected when debug is enabled either from web.config or from host settings.  There is no setting available for disabling the AddTag=false directly.    Main change done is this only:    if (AddTag && (this.Context.IsDebuggingEnabled || dnnConfig.IsDebugMode()))  {          writer.Write(""<!--CDF({0}|{1})-->"", DependencyType, FilePath);  }" [SEP] "The comment is actually used/required for correct operation.  It's used to trigger a request of the resource when pulling content from cache (i.e. a module that requests a script when it loads will miss that script when loaded from cache, unless this comment is there)" [SEP] "Code reverted and issue closed as Won't fix because it will impact modules which register scripts via CDF and are cached. For these modules the scripts are re-registered from cached version of the module by finding the CDF version from cached content." [SEP] "The CDF part is required only for the files which are included by the cached modules and still the core fi

summary: "GlobalResources.resx contains hardcoded ""http"" prefix - even if site is SSL-only"
description: """The default App_GlobalResources\GlobalResources.resx  localization file contains hardcoded http prefix on certain URLS. - expecially password resets/reminders,     eg EMAIL_PASSWORD_REMINDER_BODY.Text :     Link to reset password: *http*://[Portal:URL]/default.aspx?ctl=PasswordReset.. etc etc    This issue has existed since DNN 4.x at least, but with HTTPS becoming much more prevalent, including a push for https on everything, this is becoming more and more a problem.  Every DNN site I've built in the last year has needed https and one had http disabled (until a redirection was set up to get around this issue, among others!).    Workaround:  edit resource file for every new installation, by hand (or create a custom site template).  Lots of work and not fixing this in DNN source means potential upgrade issues.    One suggestion:  Add a new Portal token  say  [Portal:FullURL]  which includes the http(s) prefix,  equivalent to     From a quick look seems like adding to PortalSettings.cs, GetProperty() method something like this:    {{    case """"fullurl"""":          propertyNotFound = false;          result =  PropertyAccess.FormatString(                  (SSLEnforced ? """"https://"""" : """"http://"""" ) + PortalAlias.HTTPAlias                 , format );          break;  }}       .. but I can see a bit of complexity to navigate around SSL offloading - obviously even if SSL is offloaded, the email should still contain https."""
comments: "Just a thought that maybe FullURL might not be the best choice for a token , out of risk for confusion with [Tab:FullURL]  .  However that pointed me to  TestableGlobals.Instance.AddHTTP(PortalSettings.Current.PortalAlias.HTTPAlias)  - which sounds like it might be better for getting the https bit - although I suspect SSL offloading still an issue.    Other code change besides PortalSettings  -  App_GlobalResources\GlobalResources.resx  contains three references to  http://[Portal:URL],  change to [Portal:PrefixedURL] or whatever..." [SEP] "there should be an exposed protocol property of portalinfo, providing either ""http:"" or ""https:""  Resources need to be adopted to use it." [SEP] "Fixings for [DNN-10289|https://dnntracker.atlassian.net/browse/DNN-10289] are quite enough as redirections are also working fine for SSLbased site. Though need the thorough QA for whole site if SSL enabled.   This ticket can be closed after QA verification" [SEP] "Moved to QA"

summary: "SI: Persistent session exists as session identifiers were not invalidated upon logout"
description: """DNN session cookies value exists persistently as session identifiers were not invalidated upon logout.  Session identifier should have been destroyed immediately upon logout, however, the value is able to load privileged page after restoring the previous session cookie.  We implemented as belowing link in our staging environment by following this recommendation.  http://www.codeproject.com/Articles/26106/Handle-Session-Timeouts-on-DotNetNuke-Through-an-H  After implementation, We tested in same browser is working fine by using Cookies Manager. However, session has not logged out if tested on different browsers.  """

Session identifier should have been destroyed immediately upon logout, however, the value is able to load privileged page after restoring the previous session cookie.  We would like to know  1. If DDN would plan to fix this issue and,  2. What is the reason why there is no official release to this issue. Is there any technical concerns where no fixes to it so far. And,  3. We need to confirm that if there any official solution(the area of customization) done by DNN? So far all of the reference you sent to us were actually from from external resources, which were not supported and proved by DNN." summary: "Review the incremental cleanup, config and dataprovider processes"
description: """Platform has a mechanism that allows adding incremental modifications associated to:  - cleanup of resources (DNN Platform\Website\Install\Cleanup)  - configuration (DNN Platform\Website\Install\Config)  - sql upgrades (Website\Providers\DataProviders\SqlDataProvider)    These processes use a name convention based on the number of the release + an incremental sequence. Ex: 08.00.00.01, 08.00.00.02, etc.    However, if there is a jump in this last sequence, the process will be stopped and will continue with the next release files. So if there are more files after this missing sequence number, they won't be executed. Currently this kind of problems is happening on the cleanup and configuration folders.    This behaviour implies that a new issue has to add a new incremental configuration, it will have to used the first missing sequence number in order to be executed. This can have a potential impact since following files, which were NOT executed, will start to execute what can imply undesired side effects.  """
comments: "Finally, the source of the problem is not related to jumps in the sequence of incremental upgrades. The problem is that Platform generates the names of the files that will be managed by using the one in the SqlDataProvider folder. And then it uses these names in the rest of the steps of the upgrade process.     It seems that this behaviour comes from the ""old"" upgrade process where no incremental sequences were used. In this scenario, only one file was added by release, so this name was looked for in the SqlDataProvider folder and then used safely in the remaining steps. However, once the incremental upgrades have been added, these names can be not valid, for example:  - the number of incremental files in the cleanup step can be bigger than the ones in the SqlDataProvider folder.  - if there is a missing file in SqlDataProvider folder, eg. '08.00.00.02', the file with that name steps will be ignore in the remaining.    So the whole upgrade process needs to be refactor to take into account properly this new

summary: "Web links that can break a page with Journal stream (/w solution)"
description:
"""Greetings all, today I would like to highlight n issue we came across not long ago relating to posting web links into Journal stream.    As we all know DNN has been great at scraping of web page contents anytime a link is posted. But there exist the possibility of that link breaking your page.    Long story short one of our clients included a link to rte.ie article which to my surprise included a 4000+ character description which would obviously overflow ItemData column in Journal table.    To fix this problem I've included a crude yet simple loop to truncate a description to fit 2000 character limit just before ItemData is set during save/update procedures.    Now it is important to note that ItemData is essentially JSON encoded string with the following properties:      * Url      * Title      * Description      * ImageUrl      * Cacheability    Five properties that supposed to fit into 2000 characters is a tricky task in it self as there is no set limit to how long a Url, Title, Description or ImageUrl can be. But in my case it was clear that Description was just out of proportions resulting in truncated ItemData value which was impossible to convert into back into object.    Example of good ItemData after description has been truncated to fit:  {""""ImageUrl"""":""""http://img.rasset.ie/00076520-500.jpg"""",""""Title"""":""""Winning Stories & Shortlist 2014 - Francis MacManus 
Short Story Competition - RTÉ Radio 1"""",""""Description"""":""""RTÉ Radio 1 is delighted to announce that the winner of this year’s Francis Mac Manus Short Story Competition 2015 is Eleanor O’Reilly for her story ‘Stoppin’ the Silence’. 

Dolores Walshe has been awarded  2nd prize for her story ‘Small Yellow Sun’ and Kathryn Burke’s story ‘Irish Gothic: Waiting For Samhain’  has won 3rd prize.

Winner of the Francis Mac Manus Short Story Competition 2015 

Eleanor O’Reilly teaches English and Classical Studies at The Community School in Gorey, Co. Wexford.  She began writing one morning, three years ago, over a bowl of porridge, while on maternity leave. Since then she has won a number of awards and commendations, including winning the Trevor/Bowen International Short Story Award 2013, the Writing4Autism 2014 and being shortlisted for the James Plunkett Literary Award 2013 and the Fish Short Story Award 2014.   She is currently studying for a Master’s in Creative Writing and is half way through her first novel, as part of the Master’s programme.

She lives in Arklow, Co. Wicklow with her husband and daughter and their two dogs.

The judges described ‘Stoppin’ the Silence’ as  a ‘stand-out’ story, which managed to be ‘very funny, very sad and very clever’.  They praised the author’s skill in using a child’s voice to deal with very tricky subject matter.

‘Stoppin’ the Silence’ by Eleanor O’Reilly will be broadcast on RTÉ Radio 1 at 11.10pm on Monday, October 5th.  It will be read by Genevieve Hulme-Beaman.

2nd Prize 

The winner of the 2nd prize is Dolores Walshe for her story ‘Small Yellow Sun’.

The judges described this story as ‘deeply affecting’, told in a voice that really drew the reader"""",""""Url"""":""""http://www.rte.ie/radio1/francis-macmanus-short-story/generic/2014/0709/629559-winning-stories-and-shortlist-2014/"""",""""Cacheability"""":10}    And if you wish to find out what the bad ItemData will be just copy and paste the Url part into your Journal and post a message. Be warned that this will break your page and you will need to manually fix ItemData column by completing JSON syntax.      *On to the fix*  There are two function (which can be found in Library/Services/Journal/JournalControllerImpl.cs) that can cause this problem namely:        public void SaveJournalItem(JournalItem journalItem, int tabId, int moduleId) { }      public void UpdateJournalItem(JournalItem journalItem, int tabId, int moduleId) { }    Each one has a line of code that sets what will be stored in ItemData column:        string journalData = journalItem.ItemData.ToJson();    And to work around the problem I just added the following loop to reduce the length of Description property until the whole thing fits 2000 character limit:        while (journalItem.ItemData.ToJson().Length > 2000)      {          journalItem.ItemData.Description = journalItem.ItemData.Description.Substring(0, journalItem.ItemData.Description.Length - 5);      }      string journalData = journalItem.ItemData.ToJson();    Each time while loops over ItemData property it removes 5 characters from description until the condition is no longer valid at which point it should be safe to set the JSON string.    Again just to reiterate on one important point. Because there is no hard length limit on ItemData properties in theory it can happen where say Url property may take up the whole 2000 character limit. But in practice no one uses Urls that long. However, there are other properties that may be quite lengthy so just be aware that Description is not the only problem property."""

Actually, before that I also encountered one issue in """"Edit Div"""", but difficult to repro.     """

summary: "Journal Get More button not displaying when deleted records are present"
description: """This is a trivial issue that we have had to deal with for a long time now. Basically Journals Get More button in certain circumstances was not being displayed when we knew for a fact there were more records in the systems. At first we just though is something to do with time. As in id the system is abandoned for a while the posts go stale. But today we have traced this issue right down to its root which was hidden in stored procedures and it turned out that its not the records that were becoming stale but deleted records preventing correct number of good records being returned.     IF EXISTS(SELECT * from dbo.[Journal_TypeFilters] WHERE ModuleId = @ModuleId)   INSERT INTO @j     SELECT j.journalid, jt.datecreated from (     SELECT DISTINCT js.JournalId from dbo.[Journal] as j      INNER JOIN dbo.[Journal_Security] as js ON js.JournalId = j.JournalId      INNER JOIN dbo.[Journal_User_Permissions](@PortalId,@CurrentUserId ,1) as t        ON t.seckey = js.SecurityKey AND (js.SecurityKey = 'R' + CAST(@GroupId as nvarchar(100)) OR js.SecurityKey = 'E')       --WHERE j.PortalId = @PortalId       WHERE ((@IncludeAllItems = 0) AND (j.PortalId = @PortalId AND j.IsDeleted = @IsDeleted))          OR         ((@IncludeAllItems = 1) AND (j.PortalId = @PortalId))     ) as j INNER JOIN dbo.[Journal] jt ON jt.JournalId = j.JournalId AND jt.PortalId = @PortalId AND jt.GroupId = @GroupId     INNER JOIN dbo.[Journal_TypeFilters] as jf ON jf.JournalTypeId = jt.JournalTypeId AND jf.ModuleId = @ModuleId     ORDER BY jt.DateCreated DESC, jt.JournalId DESC;   ELSE   INSERT INTO @j     SELECT j.journalid, jt.datecreated from (     SELECT DISTINCT js.JournalId from dbo.[Journal] as j      INNER JOIN dbo.[Journal_Security] as js ON js.JournalId = j.JournalId      INNER JOIN dbo.[Journal_User_Permissions](@PortalId,@CurrentUserId ,1) as t        ON t.seckey = js.SecurityKey AND (js.SecurityKey = 'R' + CAST(@GroupId as nvarchar(100)) OR js.SecurityKey = 'E')       --WHERE j.PortalId = @PortalId       WHERE ((@IncludeAllItems = 0) AND (j.PortalId = @PortalId AND j.IsDeleted = @IsDeleted))          OR         ((@IncludeAllItems = 1) AND (j.PortalId = @PortalId))     ) as j INNER JOIN dbo.[Journal] jt ON jt.JournalId = j.JournalId AND jt.PortalId = @PortalId AND jt.GroupId = @GroupId     ORDER BY jt.DateCreated DESC, jt.JournalId DESC;      I've attached a screenshot of the same code just so I can point out where the issue was occurring.    Lines 8 and 22 show where I commented out original WHERE clause.  Lines 9-11 and 23-25 show what I replaced them with.    I had to do this in order to get around the issue created by deleted records in the final SELECT statement's WHERE clause. I am not sure why the procedures are setup the way they are as it would make more sense to check for IsDeleted at the very start and not have deleted records trickle down all the way to the final select only to run into a problem where deleted records get filtered resulting in fewer records being returned then can/should be returned.    Stored procedures that this affects:  [Journal_ListForGroup]  [Journal_ListForGroupSinceDate]  [Journal_ListForProfile]  [Journal_ListForSummary]    I am not entirely sure how long this has been around but looking as it hasn't been a fix in 7.4.2 no one has reported this as a problem."""

summary: "DAM module on Host File Management page shows error"
description: """(i) This bug occurs in all products: Platform, Basic, Content, and Engage    Steps:  # Installed Platform with dummy serialization caching provider and object qualifier.  # Login as host.  # Go to Admin File Management. DAM module works correclty.  # Go to Host File Management. DAM module shows error. _Nothing logged in the logfile_.    !dam.png!"""
comments: "This bug is a regression from DNN-7857. The change done in that issue does not handle properly web api request made from Host pages.   Note: Is not related to object qualifier or the dummy caching provider." [SEP] "The fix in the DNN Platform\DotNetNuke.Web\Api\Internal\DnnContextMessageHandler.cs TabIsInPortal method did in DNN-7857 does not handling properly host pages. Unfortunately there is no way in the DnnContextMessageHandler to know if a web method request is done in the context of an host tab. The portal id is based on the alias so this will always set a valid portal Id.    I have fixed extended the logic added in DNN-7857 returning true in the method if the tab is an Host tab. The private method has been renamed from *TabIsInPortal *to *TabIsInPortalOrHost*.    Submitted PR: https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1009" [SEP] "Reviewed and merged build 8.0.0.525" [SEP] "Verified fixed in:  * Platform 8.0.0.527  * Basic and Content 8.3.0.110  * Engage 8.3.0.150"

summary: "Security practices recommendation in DNN source code - ValidateAntiForgeryToken"
description: """Hi Developers,    I’ve noticed in few controllers methods that this pattern is used:  [HttpGet]  [ValidateAntiForgeryToken]   needs to be removed    The anti-forgery token is needed only for POST (and PUT) requests and is useless for GET ones (just an extra unnecessary overhead that hurts the performance). So, remember to use it as follows only:  [HttpPost]  [ValidateAntiForgeryToken]   must be added  """
comments: "DNN-7929: remove ValidateAntiForgeryToken attribute on get methods." [SEP] "Hi [~accountid:<USER>, good job :). I have seen that as part of your commit you added some ValidateAntiForgeryToken to some HttpPost web methods that did not have that. Did you test that those web methods are requested from the client side code sending the ForgeryToken, so that are still working fine? Can you please check and add a comment with the scenario tested so QA is aware of the impact of the change?  Thks" [SEP] "Hi [~accountid:<USER>， im not add this attribute new, the attribute was add as class attribute before, which also contains Get methods, so i move this to each post method in it." [SEP] "My bad [~accountid:<USER>, thank you for the clarification :)" [SEP] "Merged build 8.0.0.558" [SEP] "This is a dev task, Closed based on Francesco's review." [SEP] "Verified with Miguel for latest build in platform and quick review from QA side and its closed  "

summary: "DNN's default css should be split up and only optionally loaded"
description: """Default.css contains a css reset and numerous styles to provide a basis for DNN. Since there is no option to not load this stylesheet, developers have to jump through all kinds of hoops to get the effects they desire.    A way forward would be to  1. Get rid of a default loading stylesheet altogether  2. Allow both skins and modules to signal dependencies like we do with JS libraries  3. Split the old default and deliver a reset css and dnn css file as """"core libraries"""" skins and modules can reference          """
comments: "Timo has come up with an interesting suggestion: create a v8 skin package type. For older skins the default.css will load as before. For v8 skins it is up to the skin (skins will let DNN know what to load). This may very well offer us a way forward with minimal breaking changes." [SEP] "This is ready for review.  " [SEP] "Code review done.    Summary of the changes done:  * previous default.css moved to new path    * new default.css for 8.0.0, the new default one:    ** 8.0.0 'default.css' cleaned up in DNN-7994.  * client dependency has now new options:  ** Framework.  ** Version.  ** ForceVersion, meant for skin designers that wish to override choices made by module developers or the framework.  * added new controls that manage the new options:  ** RegisterScript (DnnJsInclude/Exclude).  ** RegisterStyleSheet (DnnCssInclude/Exclude).    *Note for QA*  Although this issue was pending to be reviewed, it was merged long time ago. So new features related to the default.css file for 8.0.0 are been already teste

summary: "Recycle App Domain & Clear Cache now create 400 (Bad Request) in jQuery code"
description: """Install or upgrade to latest Platform 8 build (I used 508)  Log in as Host  Go * Admin > Page Managemet*  Right click on the site name in left nav  Select *Create Pages*  Bulk create at least 10 pages  Go to each new page and edit HTML module add text, images, etc  Once you've edited every new page hover over *Tools*  Click *Clear Cache*  Click *Recycle App Domain*    Check Dev Tools/Firebug in the browser    Actual Result:  POST http://platform800508.dnntest.ca/DesktopModules/internalservices/API/controlbar/ClearHostCache 400 (Bad Request)send @ jquery.js?cdv=41:8526jQuery.extend.ajax @ jquery.js?cdv=41:7978dnn.controlBar.clearHostCache @ dnn.controlBar.js?cdv=41:477(anonymous function) @ dnn.controlBar.js?cdv=41:1462jQuery.event.dispatch @ jquery.js?cdv=41:3074elemData.handle @ jquery.js?cdv=41:2750jquery.js?cdv=41:8526 POST http://platform800508.dnntest.ca/DesktopModules/internalservices/API/controlbar/RecycleApplicationPool 400 (Bad Request)send @ jquery.js?cdv=41:8526jQuery.extend.ajax @ jquery.js?cdv=41:7978dnn.controlBar.recycleAppPool @ dnn.controlBar.js?cdv=41:529(anonymous function) @ dnn.controlBar.js?cdv=41:1467jQuery.event.dispatch @ jquery.js?cdv=41:3074elemData.handle @ jquery.js?cdv=41:2750      *Expected Result:*  No 404 errors and no .js errors """

summary: "Performance Issue : Install of platform in Cloud take 11:37 Min to complete"
description: """Install platform 8.0.0 Build 506 in cloud environment take 11:37 min to complete the installation process    please refer to screen cast http://screencast.com/t/oYJoFa9j """
comments: "Hi Amrit, we need more information to describe this ""slow"". " [SEP] "The installation in cloud is now taking 9 to 10 min which is as usual time.    The Installation time is similar with the previous build of the platform 7.4.2  and we will keep a note on the installation performance of the Platform.    We will keep checking the performance time to time for the build.    We are closing the issue , but we will track the record of the installation time.    "

summary: "Platform 8 - MVC HTML module exists in Available Extensions"
description: """*Screencast.com*    http://screencast.com/t/sHLMi3Vxa8    Found while regressing DNN-7289    Do a clean install of or upgrade a site to Platform 8 latest build  Log in as *Host*  Go *Host >Extensions > Available Extensions > Modules*    *Actual Result:*  *HTML Module* exists as an available installable module    *Expected Result:*  There is no v8.0.0 HTML module in *Host >Extensions > Available Extensions > Modules*        """
comments: "This issue should be simple enough, remove it from packaging. " [SEP] "This is the expected behavior:  The installed module by default is not the MVC HTML, but the old aspx HTML.  The module under Available Extension, is a different module    This can be confusing, as both modules have the same name, and when trying to install the MVC module, it warns that the module is already installed (although the installed module is the ASPX module). This behavior is similar to the HTML module in Evoq.    Our recommendation is to close this as won't fix        " [SEP] "I am saying we need to remove the MVC HTML module from being available at all; thus we need to take it out of packaging. " [SEP] "Chris is correct.  The MVC HTML module was started as a replacement for the HTML module and it was never intended that we would ship both modules.  When we started the DCC module, we halted development of the MVC HTML module and it should just be deleted completely." [SEP] "Removed the MVC Html module. Please [~accountid:<USER>

summary: "DCC - Add a very long string into Description field for new Content Type error pop up appears"
description: """Instal or upgrade to latest version of Platform 8  Log in as Host or Admin  Go *Admin > Dynamic Content Type Viewer*  Click *Add Content Type* button  Enter a name  Enter a description > 255 characters - I input 1,150 characters in the *Description* text box  Click the blue *Save* button    Actual Result:  A pop up renders:    *Alert*  An error has occurred    In Dev Tools/Firebug:    POST http://platform800502.dnntest.ca/DesktopModules/Dnn/DynamicContentManager/API/ContentType/SaveContentType 500 (Internal Server Error)    jquery.js?cdv=42 : 8526  send @ jquery.js?cdv=42:8526  jQuery.extend.ajax @ jquery.js?cdv=42:7978  call @ dnn.utility.js?cdv=42:263  post @ dnn.utility.js?cdv=42:339  self.saveContentType @ contentTypes.js?cdv=42:263  (anonymous function) @ knockout.js?cdv=42:83  jQuery.event.dispatch @ jquery.js?cdv=42:3074  elemData.handle @ jquery.js?cdv=42:2750    *Expected Result:*  Pop up that says *Text limit is 255 characters please try again*"""
comments: "Batch update of issues that won't be addressed due to change in functionality from version 9.0. If you feel these issues are still relevant, please feel free to re-open."

summary: "Changing the ModuleTitle will change the module title in cache"
description: """When in custom module you change the ModuleTitle it stays cached. It affects only logged out user. The bug is from version 7.4.0 and up  Example:  this.ModuleConfiguration.ModuleTitle = """"New changed module title"""";    I have created simple module to demonstrate this bug.  """

summary: "Enable CL popup won't close after process complete"
description: """Reproduce Steps:  1. install 8.0.0 latest version, login as host;  2. go to Host > Extensions, install a LP from Available Extensions list;  3. enable CL from host  settings;  4. goto Admin > Languages, click """"Enable Content Localization"""" button;  5. tick """"Make all pages Translatable"""" checkbox, then click """"Enable Localized Content"""" button.    Expected Result:  after process complete, the popup closed and page refresh.    Actually Result:  popup not close, and error occur in console:  !20151113090046.png|width=800!  """

summary: "HMTL Editor Manager - Cannot change the Toolbar Setting to anything other than Basic"
description: """*Screencast.com:*  http://screencast.com/t/LdwBBsGR6    Install latest Platform 8  Uninstall CK Editor Provider   Install CK Editor Provider downloaded from build servers   Go *Host > Advanced Settings > HTML Editor Manager  Click on the *Custom Toolbars* tab  In the *Custom Toolbars List* select *Full*  Scroll down and on the bottom left click *Save*  As instructed F5 (refresh) the page    Actual Result:  The change in the *Custom Toolbars List* does not persist  *Basic* is set in the *Custom Toolbars List* after the page refreshes  There is a broken image at the top of the page in the green *Success* message after you've changed the *Custom Toolbar List*    Expected Result:  Any changes in the *Custom Toolbars List* persists after change has been saved and the page refreshed  The *Success* message has an image after you've saved the *Custom Toolbar List*"""
comments: "The missed image has been fixed.  Regarding Custom Toolbars, there is no work for that since that is the current behaviour. You can see how it works in the following screencast: http://www.screencast.com/t/WEg32TrMpcC    Please [~accountid:<USER>, could you review my changes and merge the Pull Request?  https://github.com/DNN-Connect/CKEditorProvider/pull/21    Thanks." [SEP] "Assigned to [~accountid:<USER> due to I do not have permissions to do the merge on that repository." [SEP] "Code review done. Merged PR to add missing images." [SEP] "Failed testing in Platform v8.0 build 535. Only partially fixed, the broken image is fixed however when refreshing the page the selection is set back to ""Basic""    I have tested this on IE9." [SEP] "Behzad, actually that field is not a provider setting, so it isnot persistent. Please, look at my screencast above in order to know how it works and what is its aim" [SEP] "I have verified the missing images are now all appearing   I have verified the new workflow to change

summary: "Remove the revision part from built assemblies"
description: """In DNN products every new build has a new assembly version with the 'revision' part set to the build number.    I suggest removing the 'revision' part from the {{AssemblyVersion}}, but keep it in the {{AssemblyFileVersion}}. This makes testing with various DLL's much easier during the development time.    For example, I can drop {{DotNetNuke.dll}} file from a newer build into my local installation without having to replace all the DLL's that depend on that specific previous build of this file. Therefore, we avoid the YSOD errors of ASP.NET in such cases during testing a small change in the Platform main library without the need to create a complete build of the whole site.      The same can be applied to all built DLL files."""

comments: "I am having the exact same issue, I troubleshooted a bit and found out that the language code in the rewritten url is lower case and so it does not match the database. I was not able to correctly identify why the language code in the rewriten querystring is lowercase but I hope it gives you a clue to investigate further.    As a workaround, I added:    string locale = Locale.Substring(0, 3) + Locale.Substring(3, 2).ToUpper();    Just before line 526 in LanguageEditor.aspx.cs" [SEP] "PR submitted, https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1045" [SEP] "I've reviewed the code, I looks good to fix the problem but it seems to be a workaround to avoid crashes when locale is wrong, but I think that we should avoid this kind of defensive code and figure out who provokes the error. The problem should come from who is generating the URLs that appear in the HTML of the page:      'LanguageEnabler.ascx.cs' seems to be the responsible of populating the languages, this information comes directly from database. Afte

summary: "Password reset emails not being sent"
description: """Portal is configured to use email addresses as usernames. Custom registration form used (via Admin->Site Settings->User Account Settings) and includes the registration field """"Email"""").    When user clicks the """"Reset Password"""" link and supplies a valid email address, the mail is never sent, and the event log contains the Operation Failure """"Password Sent Failure""""."""
comments: "Yes there is an issue here.     From testing the values available in the Custom Registration Form it is that if you include *Username* in the *Registration Fields* text box, in any position, it becomes the dominate *field type* for registration.   So, in a *Custom Registration Form* where you have both *Username* & *Email* defined for *Registration*, when a user clicks *Reset Password* the text field appears tagged as *Username*. This is *not a mistake* if you input *Username* then the* reset password link* will be emailed to the *email address* stored for that *username*.    I have tested this on both on-premise and cloud instances and the behavior is the same.    There are 2 workarounds available to avoid *Username* being the default value for *Password Reset* request  1. Do not use *Username* as a *Registration Field* when using *Custom Registration Form*  2. Invoke *Requires Unique Email* via changing the web.config file - This will populate the *Password Reset* pop up with both the *Username* and *Email

description: """h2. Card  One DNN 7 """"feature"""" that I think needs to be added to items for DNN 8 cleanup is the removal of the Advanced Settings screen.  This page was created to """"simplify"""" settings by aggregating many common settings into one location.  What this ultimately did was complicate things by giving people multiple locations for accomplishing the same task.  It didn't remove options, but instead duplicated options that were already available.    !AdvancedConfigurationSettings.png!    h2. Conversation  Often the way this was implemented was by re-using existing user controls in the new Advanced Settings screen.  This approach is blocking the team's ability to separate out the Skins module since one of the user controls in the skins module is also used in the advanced settings page. See DNN-6637.    h2. Confirmation  The Advanced Configuration Setting page and module should be uninstalled and removed in upgrade and new install of platform 8.0  Note for DEV: please ensure that we remove anything related to that feature and we do not leave any dead code in the source. Keep in mind that probably all the user control used by this module does not belong to him as are from other module. In this case we cannot cleanup these files as part of this story."""

summary: "Reduce the spent time of the InstallWizard main request"
description: """It is known that some environments throws exceptions when a request is slow. So after a deep investigation, we have found that the InstallWizard main request could run into that problem since it is alive during all the install process. description: """*Found while regressing DNN-7350*    *Screen Cast:*   http://screencast.com/t/6UbqTRKDUMn       Hit a site built in DNN QA Azure enviornment I used http://dnn90crfi.qa.evoqondemand.com/    Go *Admin > Page Management*  Right click on Home and select *Add Page*  Add something like    Page 1  > Page 1A  > Page 1B  > Page 1C  > Page 1D  Page 2    This will give you a 23 level page navigation from Home to Page 1 to the 3rd level pages  Hit the site from a mobile device, iPad, iPhone, Windows Phone, Asus EEPad, etc, etc  Press or hover over *Home* the *Page 1* and finally select Page 1A, Page 1B, etc    Actual Result:  Only the 2nd level Test1, Test 2, etc pages are ever visible  The mobile browsers are all trying to display the 3rd level pages at far right outside of the actual browser widith    Even in the cases where you get a hamburger menu with an accordian drop down you'll not see anything but:  Home > Test1  Home > Test2  Home > Test3    Expected Result:  If there are multiple levels of navigation they should be accessible   In this case the Test1, Test2, etc pages should have been displayed to the left of the pop up  In the case of Hamburger menu w/accordian drop down the navigation should just show up  in order"""

summary: "WebAPI Detects the wrong portalID when using a child portal"
description: """When using the WebApi since the request to the controller will also remove the child portal sub folder the code loads the wrong portal instance.   This is a problem since if you have a user called UserA in the child portal www.test.com/child then unless that same username exists in a parent portal called www.test.com then the user will not be able to call the action.    When on a child portal of www.test.com/child the controller will be called on the url  ww.test.com/... hence why the wrong portal instance is then loaded in the DNN WebApi code.    This will only effect child portals but since the move is now away to MVC and SPA modules this really needs to work on child portals.   I always develop in a child portal and hence why I found this bug which stopped by SPA module from working."""
comments: "You _should_ be making web API requests with the child portal folder in the URL.  Are you using the {{getServiceRoot}} method on {{$.ServiceFramework(moduleId)}} to generate the URL on the client?" [SEP] "@Brian, you called. I failed to take into account I needed to use the jQuery ServiceFramework extension. I think this is a non-issue and this pull request (https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/952) can cancelled.  Thanks for the pointer :)"

summary: "WebAPI - TabId Always Returns True"
description: """When use the WebApi the code attempts to validate if the tabId provided in the header belongs to the portalId as determined by the portal alias of the WebApi request.    The issue is that this code will also return.   DNN Platform\DotNetNuke.Web\Api\Internal\DnnContextMessageHandler.cs    private static bool TabIsInPortal(int tabId, int portalId)          {              var tab = TabController.Instance.GetTab(tabId, portalId);                return tab != null;          }    The call to GetTab contains code in the core framework to check if the portal matches the tab request. Otherwise that code will find the correct portal regardless of the portalId parameter based to the GetTab method call.   The var tab will never be null in the method above. (So only as the tabId exists in the database)."""
comments: "This issue was fixed in https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/953    Reviewed and merged build 8.0.0.502" [SEP] "Verifies in the changes in the Source code file path :  DNN Platform/DotNetNuke.Web/Api/Internal/DnnContextMessageHandler.cs  DnnContextMessageHandler.cs have the changes in the File.  We are closing the issue based on review of code with Miguel  " [SEP] "Attached DLL to process to check values being passed in are correct. See attached screenshots." [SEP] "I have to reopen this as it break function of copy modules from site groups." [SEP] "DNN-7857: return true when the tab's portal id and alias' portal id are in same portal group.    PR: https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1345" [SEP] "PR merged build 8.0.1.155" [SEP] "We have verified the function of copy module from site groups    http://screencast.com/t/ZidZkRQK1apA     we can close the issue."

summary: "Member Directory > Show proper info when a friendship request is approved"
description: """Currently, the module IU is prepared to manage only one valid response when a user send a friendship request.    This behavior changes the text from """"Add as friend"""" to """"Friend Request Pending"""". However, following issue DNN-21989, we have found that the request could be approved directly if target user sent previously the same request.     So, we should prepare the module UI in order to expect also this kind of response."""

summary: "DCC - Move cache from repository classes to managers classes"

It should be located inside the manager classes, and the repository classes should be only in charge of data access management.  This change affects mainly to Dynamic Content Types, Field Definitions and Templates."""

summary: "Create new module feature broken for MVC modules"

Only requirement here is to add a .dnn manifest file in this folder  2. Open the Host > Extensions page  3. Click the _Create New Module_ button  4. In the _Create module from_ drop down, select """"Manifest""""    Expected:  1. I didn't create an """"owner"""" folder for my module, so I should not have to set MVC as the owner.  2. If I had created an """"owner"""" folder (desktopmoules/MVC/owner/modulename) then I will not be able to select my module folder  3. What should happen is that the system treats any folders under desktopmodule/mvc as if they were in the desktopmodules folder.    Actual:  1. The system is treating the MVC folder as a standard folder rather than being a special folder in desktopmodules."""
