summary: "http://cdn.dotnetnuke.com/installer/banners/banner_1.jpg resource does not exist"
description: """I was trying a DNN install on a server and I noticed the following error on the InstallWizard.aspx page:   Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (Not Found)  http://cdn.dotnetnuke.com/installer/banners/banner_1.jpg    It appears the URL is bad or the resource doesn't exist."""
comments: "This is actually by design. The installer is setup to loop through a pattern of images until one is not found, when it is not found it will then start the loop over again (this was done so we can follow a pattern at any time to include/exclude banners without having to update the installer). "

summary: "InstallWizard.aspx mixed case causing unavailable resources on some hosting providers"
description: """I noticed these issues when I tried to set up a DNN install on Shaw's servers.  When I tried installing a fresh DNN instance with 07.04.02, the install wizard was unusable.  These script references use a capital """"S"""" for """"Scripts"""", where the actual folder has a lowercase """"scripts"""", so it doesn't see these files:    (Line 13)    <script type=""""text/javascript"""" src=""""../Resources/*Shared*/Scripts/jquery/jquery-ui.min.js""""></script>    (Line 14)    <script type=""""text/javascript"""" src=""""../Resources/Shared/*Scripts*/jquery/jquery.hoverIntent.min.js""""></script>    (Line 16)   <script type=""""text/javascript"""" src=""""../DesktopModules/Admin/Security/*Scripts*/dnn.PasswordComparer.js""""></script>    The other script references use the lowercase """"s"""", so it seems to be a mix of case use.    Actually it seems to be happening in quite a few places.  Another example (the folder """"Skins"""" is actually uppercase):    (Line 10) <link rel=""""stylesheet"""" type=""""text/css"""" href=""""../Portals/_default/*skins*/_default/WebControlSkin/default/combobox.default.css?refresh"""" />    I haven't really looked, but this may be the case throughout the project, not just this page.  Is it possible to make the referenced path cases match the actual path cases?"""
comments: "I am not aware of Windows Servers being case sensitive with file names.  Is there anything special with the server setup?" [SEP] "I've only seen it on one hosting provider (Shaw: http://shawhosting.ca/)  I have no idea what they have for their server setup as their interface is very limited and so is their support.  But they claim that they can run ASP.NET based sites.  I was able to resolve those errors by setting ""CaseInsensitive On"" in the .htaccess file, but are still so many issues that I don't get on any other ASP.NET host that they have to be related to their setup.    I thought it may be a good idea (at least good practice) to have the resource case match the reference in the future.  It would increase the platforms that DNN could run on without issue (like whatever Shaw is running right now)."

summary: "SI: For a file Linkclick URL should show the file name also"
description: """In HTML Pro module, create a link for a document file (PDH, DOC or PNG), to the advanced editor, right click on he link and go to properties and enable tacking for this link.    Save the changes and publish the page.    When this link is clicked and the document opens in the browser, the LinkClick URL must contain the file name also.    So, a URL like the following, which is for a file named """"02-06 Valentine's Day Table Maps.doc"""":  https://portaltest/LinkClick.aspx?fileticket=opQ_Pe53wPg%3d&portalid=0  would result in something like the following:  https://portaltest/Portals/0/fileticket/opQ_Pe53wPg%3d&portalid/02-06 Valentine's Day Table Maps.doc"""
comments: "HTML Pro and Advanced Editor are Evoq components.   please consider moving this issue accordingly." [SEP] "The Advanced editor is not an Evoq exclusive component, it's simply the editor provider (used to be Telerik, now it's CK Editor). Besides that, a Jira item belongs in the project where the fix/change should be made. " [SEP] "The issue is related to the LinkClick.aspx behavior when opening documents directly in IE.     Regardless of how you trigger that behavior, the current behavior results in the document being opened with the name LinkClick.aspx instead of the actual original file name.    The behavior also occurs when you use SECURE FILESYSTEM as the folder type, and, probably also when you use SECURE DATABASE, in additional to click tracking in HTML or Links modules. I've also seen this when the folder or file name has special characters (e.g., ""+"").    Note: _If you download the file first, this is not an issue, and since Chrome makes you download first, you don't see it in Chrome. I also don't s

summary: "DCC API - Snippets.Config file is stored under Folder Provider but managed as physical file"
description: """In the current implementation the snippets for the template editor are stored inside a plain text file stored inside Portals/_default with name """"Config/Snippets.config"""" (All the snippets are common for all the portals),  This space is managed by the DNN Folder Provider system. This means that depending on the configuration of the folder the templates could be locally in the server file system (standard folder provider), in the database (database folder provider) or even in remote location (i.e.: Azure, Amazon, DropBox folder provider, see Cloud configuration that by default use Azure folder provider).  We probably need to discuss more this part. Having the Snippets.config file under the Folder Provider could expose several issues:    *Alternative solution*  We could consider storing the file the file under Portals/0-System.    *Benefits:*        We always ensure that the files are locally stored and accessible via the Server File System    *Drawbacks:*        Admin couldn’t edit / replace these file from DAM  """

summary: "DCC - Sync Dynamic Content Item Json to Content Type Definition"
description: """Dynamic Content Creator wants to be a very flexible and dynamic content creator system. On Content Type already in use (we have content item already created in the system for a specific content type) we do not want restrict the content type definition and the system need to allow add, rename or delete a fields.    Currently the system is managing that (see DNN-7791) but when the Content Type fields change (add a new field, remove it) the content item previusly created with the old definition still keep the json with the old data. This impact also search that still is indexing this old information.    This task is to evaluate and consider some strategy to keep in sync the json stored in the dynamic content item json and the content type definition.    The goal of this task will consist in propose 2 or more strategies and evaluating for each of those pro and cons.                                """

summary: "Language Skin Object corrupts PortalSettings.PortalAlias in a multi-language portal"
description: """Brief Description of Issue.  When the LOGIN skin object is executed as last in the skin file (that is after LOGIN, USER and LOGO skin objects) in a multi-language portal, the PortalAlias of PortalSettings is left set with the PortalAlias of the last processed language, thus causing, for example, situations where PortalSetting.CultureCode is set to a language and PortaAlias is the one of a different one.  Any other control or class in the page that uses PortalAlias returns wrong values.    Steps to reproduce the issue.  1: Install a DNN 7.4.2. blank website in English (United States).  2: Add a second language (ie. it-IT).  3: Enable """"Allow Content Localization"""" in Host > Host settings > Other settings.  4: In Admin > Languages press button """"Enable Localized Content"""". In the opened popup window set """"Make all pages Translatable"""" (English - United States will be set as Current Site Default) and press """"Enable Localized Content"""".  5: In Admin > Languages set """"Enabled"""" check box of the added second language in """"Enabled"""" column.  6: In Admin > Languages click on the icon """"Create pages in this language"""" in """"Pages"""" column.  7: In Admin > Languages click on """"Active"""" check box in """"Active"""" column.  8: Translate and publish the home page of the second language  9: In English - United State language, add an HTML/Text module, enable """"Replace Tokens"""" in Settings > HTML Module Settings. Add the following content: """"Portal URL: [Portal:URL]"""".  10: In second language, copy """"HTML/Text"""" module, translate and publish.  11: Open the default skin file in """"/Portals/_default/skins/gravity/2-col.ascx"""", move the LANGUAGE skin object after the LOGIN, USER and LOGO ones and save.      Actual result:  In English - United State language, the HTML/Text module shows """"Portal URL: www.dnn742.com/it-it"""".  In second language, he HTML/Text module shows """"Portal URL: www.dnn742.com/en-us"""".    Expected result:  In English - United State language, the HTML/Text module should show """"Portal URL: www.dnn742.com/en-us"""".  In second language, he HTML/Text module should show """"Portal URL: www.dnn742.com/it-it"""".    A dirty work around waiting for a fix could be adding the following lines to file """"\admin\Skins\Language.ascx.cs"""" just after line 469 as follows:    469: litTemplate.Text = parseTemplate(strTemplate, locale.Code);  470:  471: if ( locale.Code != PortalSettings.CultureCode )  472: {  473:  string tmpString = parseTemplate(strTemplate, PortalSettings.CultureCode);  474: }  475:}"""

summary: "DotNetNuke.Services.FileSystem.FileManager.MoveFile across portals does not change portal id in files table"
description: """When using DotNetNuke.Services.FileSystem.FileManager.Instance.MoveFile(IFileInfo, IFolderInfo) to move a file to a different folder *on a different portal*:    result:  * file is moved in the file system  * Files table: FolderId is correctly updated  * Files table: PortalId is still the old PortalId    expected result:  * file is moved in the file system  * Files table: FolderId is correctly updated  * Files table: PortalId is the new PortalId    suggested fix:    alter the stored procedure UpdateFile so that the PortalId is updated along with the FolderId:    ALTER PROCEDURE {databaseOwner}[{objectQualifier}UpdateFile]  @FileId Int,  @VersionGuid UniqueIdentifier,   @FileName nVarChar(246),  @Extension nVarChar(100),  @Size Int,  @Width Int,  @Height Int,  @ContentType nVarChar(200),   @FolderID Int,  @LastModifiedByUserID Int,  @Hash VarChar(40),  @LastModificationTime DateTime,  @Title nVarChar(256),  @EnablePublishPeriod Bit,  @StartDate DateTime,  @EndDate DateTime,   @ContentItemID Int  AS  BEGIN  SET NOCOUNT ON;  DECLARE @PortalID int;  SELECT @PortalID = PortalID FROM {databaseOwner}[{objectQualifier}Folders] WHERE FolderID = @FolderID;  UPDATE {databaseOwner}[{objectQualifier}Files]  SET FileName = @FileName,  VersionGuid = @VersionGuid,  Extension = @Extension,  Size = @Size,  Width = @Width,  Height = @Height,  ContentType = @ContentType,  FolderID = @FolderID,  PortalID = @PortalID;  LastModifiedByUserID = @LastModifiedByUserID,  LastModifiedOnDate = GetDate(),  SHA1Hash = @Hash,  LastModificationTime = @LastModificationTime,  Title = @Title,  EnablePublishPeriod = @EnablePublishPeriod,  StartDate = @StartDate,  EndDate = @EndDate,  ContentItemID = @ContentItemID  WHERE FileId = @FileId  SET NOCOUNT OFF;  END"""

summary: "Redirect to Login page fails when accessing a page with permission ""Registered users"" in a multi-language portal when navigating with the second languge"
description: """Brief Description of Issue.  When accessing a page with permission """"Registered users"""" of a multi-language portal using the second language (that is different from the default one), the user is redirected to the home page of the default language instead of the login page of the current language.    Steps to reproduce the issue.  1: Install a DNN 7.4.2. blank website in English (United States).  2: Add a second language (ie. it-IT).  3: Enable """"Allow Content Localization"""" in Host > Host settings > Other settings.  4: In Admin > Languages press button """"Enable Localized Content"""". In the opened popup window set """"Make all pages Translatable"""" (English - United States will be set as Current Site Default) and press """"Enable Localized Content"""".  5: In Admin > Languages set """"Enabled"""" check box of the added second language in """"Enabled"""" column.  6: In Admin > Languages click on the icon """"Create pages in this language"""" in """"Pages"""" column.  7: In Admin > Languages click on """"Active"""" check box in """"Active"""" column.  8: Translate and publish the home page of the second language  9: In English - United State language, add a Login page. Add """"Account login"""" module to this page. Set page permission to """"All users"""" for this page.   10: In second language, copy """"Account login"""" module, translate and publish the Login page.  11: Set the Login page for both languages in Admin > Site settings > Advanced settings.  12: In English - United State language, add a new page (ie. """"Reserved page""""). Set page permission to """"Registerd Users"""".  13: In second language, translate (ie. """"Pagina riservata) and publish the page added in step 12.  14: Switch to the home page of second language and try to access the page added in step 13 (ie. /it-it/Pagina-riservata) as a not logged in user.    Actual result:  Redirection to the home page of the default language (ie. /en-us)    Expected result:  Redirection to the login page of the current laguage (ie. /it-it/login?returnurl=/it-it/Pagina-riservata)    ---- Possible Solution (as far is my understanding of the platform and multi-language)  The offending code is located at line 515 of file DNN_Platform_07.04.02_Source\DNN Platform\Library\UI\Skins\Skin.cs where the DefaultLocale is used in a multilanguage portal instead of the Current Locale.  The outcome is that even if the redirectUrl is correctly defined, the following if will always fail when navigating the site in the second language thus overwriting the redirectUrl with the home page of the default langugage.    if (TabPermissionController.CanViewPage())  {   ...  }  else  {   //If request localized page which haven't complete translate yet, redirect to default language version.   var redirectUrl = Globals.AccessDeniedURL(Localization.GetString(""""TabAccess.Error""""));   Locale defaultLocale = LocaleController.Instance.GetDefaultLocale(PortalSettings.PortalId);   if (PortalSettings.ContentLocalizationEnabled &&    TabController.CurrentPage.CultureCode != defaultLocale.Code)   {    redirectUrl = new LanguageTokenReplace {Language = defaultLocale.Code}.ReplaceEnvironmentTokens(""""[URL]"""");   }     Response.Redirect(redirectUrl, true);  }    Proposed fix should affect lines 515-520 as follow:    //If request localized page which haven't complete translate yet, redirect to default language version.  var redirectUrl = Globals.AccessDeniedURL(Localization.GetString(""""TabAccess.Error""""));  Locale currentLocale = LocaleController.Instance.GetCurrentLocale(PortalSettings.PortalId);  if (PortalSettings.ContentLocalizationEnabled &&   TabController.CurrentPage.CultureCode != currentLocale.Code)  {   redirectUrl = new LanguageTokenReplace {Language = currentLocale.Code}.ReplaceEnvironmentTokens(""""[URL]"""");  }  """

Verified the fix on Evoq_Content_9.2.0.121-879.  *+Prerequisite data:+*  - Site with two locals installed correctly.  - Login page created, published, localized for both of the locals with view permission set for All users.  - Login page is set in the site settings for both of them.  - Restricted page created, published, localized for both of the locals with view permission set for registered users only.  *+Covered Scenarios:+*  # (/) Main site, with two locals, while logged out, trying to access the restricted page by the url of the 2nd locale, y

summary: "Html5 Token Replacement in SPA"
description: """When creating a SPA module some valid angularjs code is removed from the HTML page due to the code in the Html5HostControl.    These are two examples of valid angularJS code.    var sbApp = angular.module('sbApp', []);  ...  $scope.orders = [];    However when the html module is rendered to the browser the empty [] is removed and the code looks like this.    var sbApp = angular.module('sbApp', );  ...  $scope.orders = ;    This creates a JS error and stops the module from working. The issue that DNN have used the square brackets to allow token replacement to add modulecontext information, resource string etc.  However the RegEx statement and code in the DNN Platform\Library\Services\Tokens\BaseTokenReplace.cs does not handle the case when it locates an empty bracket statement like [] and instead wrongly removes it.      """

Add a new page and add the module to the page    Observed that the module showed that it contains an empty array and it does not crash and does not produce exception which was the fix for this issue."

summary: "DCC API - Template Files are stored under Folder Provider but managed as physical files"
description: """In the current implementation any content template file is a *.cshtml* file stored inside Portals/X folder (where X is _default for global templates or the ID of the portal for local templates).   This space is managed by the DNN Folder Provider system. This means that depending on the configuration of the folder the templates could be locally in the server file system (standard folder provider), in the database (database folder provider) or even in remote location (i.e.: Azure, Amazon, DropBox folder provider, see Cloud configuration that by default use Azure folder provider).    *Issue*  The current implementation always assumes that these files are stored locally. See GetTemplateName method in the Dnn.Modules.DynamicContentViewer.Controllers.ViewController class        This latter assumption seems related to how the current DCC Module MVC implementation works that need to be able to reach the template file physically in the file system:      We probably need to discuss more this part. Having the template under the Folder Provider could expose several issues:  * We need to add logic into the API to ensure that template are always stored under standard folder provider or investigate if MVC can support read the template file as a stream  * Admin/Content Manager from DAM or Assets could delete those files causing inconsistency unwished issues in the DCC system    *Alternative solution*  We could consider store the templates files under Portals/X-System.  Benefits:  * Admin and Content Managers cannot remove, move these file from DAM or Assets (we have more control over the files to ensure that the system is consistent)  * We always ensure that the files are locally stored and accessible via the Server File System    *Drawbacks*:  * We need change the database ContentTypes_Templates table as currently the reference to the template is stored using the FileId  * Admin couldn’t edit / replace these file from DAM  """
comments: "After some talk with [~accountid:<USER> we have commented to go with the *aternative solution* proposed above. So change the template storage mechanism and use the Portal System Folder outside the Folder Provider system." [SEP] "Batch update of issues that won't be addressed due to change in functionality from version 9.0. If you feel these issues are still relevant, please feel free to re-open."

summary: "DCC API - Template File Caching"
description: """DCC makes use of .*cshtml* physical files to store the content templates. In the current implementation of the DCC Viewer seems that the file is not cached. Any time a page with DCC viewer is rendered we have a I/O read to retrieve and process the template file.  We need to review the MVC implementation in the DCC Viewer to see if we can cache the templates at the MVC API level.    Note for DEV:   * consider view and edit templates  * we need to consider and discuss a way to invalidate this cache if the template is modified via the DCC API.  * we need to consider and discuss a way to invalidate the cache if the template is modified via DAM (this maybe won't be implemented, but at least we need to document this scenario for QA and support)    *Important*: we may need to wait until DNN-7804 is done before start working on this task."""

summary: "DCC API - SQL Caching"
description: """Ideally SQL queries responsible for read Dynamic Content entities (Content Type, Content Items, Data Type, Templates) should be always cached to obtain great performance.    Cache should be defined at first at the API level, so inside the manager classes that expose the DCC functionality to internal or third party modules.    Caching is currently implemented for all the managers using AOP (see Cacheable Annotation). See example below:      However, the *DynamicContentItemManager* use internally the ContentController that does not cache the method *GetContentItemsByModuleId*.  We must cache this call as currently is used in the part more sensible to performance that is the DCC Viewer (see below):   !DCCViewerSQLCallForAnonymous.png!    *Important*: we likely will change the approach to retrieve the content item associated to a module as commented in the jira so please review this jira once DNN-7796 is done.    """

summary: "DCC API - Dynamic Content Item is tied to Module Id"
description: """In the current architecture the Dynamic Content Item instance is tied to a specific module. The Content Item table stores the ModuleId and the TabId:  !DynamicContentItemTiedToModule.png!    Basically the content item is who says: """"I belong to the module"""" when we should invert the relationship and the module should say: """"I in charge to render the content item"""".  We want the content item decoupled of the viewer so we potencially can share and integrate this content outside the dnn system or the viewer module.    The Viewer makes use of this information to know which Content Item is associated with him:      *Solution proposal*:  We should store the Content Item with no TabId or ModuleId associated and store in the Module Setting instead the Content Item Id of the Dynamic Content.  Currently the Module already use Module Setting to know which content type is used and which Edit and View template is set:  !DynamicViewerModuleSettings.png!    Note: we could eventually replace the Content Type Setting with Content Item Id.  * DCC_ContentItemId      *Important Notes*    * *Note for Dev*: we need to review the scenario where the Module is deleted. Probably for this first version we want also be able to remove the Content Item from the system as well. Need to confirmed with PM.    * *Note for QA*: this task is more API level and does not imply changes in funcionality. Just how the relationship between the content item and the module is defined."""

However we should need to change the way the Viewer load the Content Item as commented in the jira." [SEP] "ModuleId and TabId are not no longer stored in the Content Item. comments: "I have fixed changing the way the json parser works.     Before was building the Content of the Dynamic Content Item from the data stored in the database to the Fields Definition. Now it builds the Dynamic Content Item from the Field Definition to the data.    If a field is not found in the json it uses the default value (i.e.: 0 for Integer fields, string.empty for string fields).    This change allows the system to be more dynamic. So even if we have dynamic content items of a content type the admin user can still add or remove fields of the content type without having a crash or an exception in the viewer.    This change fix as well the issue reported in DNN-7789.    Submitted Pull request https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/939." [SEP] "Code looks good. summary: "CDF - Version is not updated iafter installing extension, updating portal.css or clearing server cache "
description: """*This behavior has been in the application since at least Platform 7.4.0*    Install latest 8.0 build or upgrade to latest 8.0 build  Log in as Host  Go Host > Host Settings > Advanced Settings  Scroll down and expand *Client Resource Management*  Tick the check box for """"Enable Composite Files"""" - this will reload the page  Tick each check box for """"Minify CSS"""" and """"Minify JS""""  Click the blue *Update"""" button  Make note of the *Current Host Version* # - need this for comparison later on    Now go upload a new extension into the system  Return and view the version # in *Client Resource Management*    Now go to the root web folder of the site  Open *Portal.css* (Portals\_default) and edit a line.. just adding a space and removing it then saving should be sufficient  Return and view the version # in *Client Resource Management*    Go to IIS on the server hosting the site  Clear the AppPool running the site  Restart IIS for the site     Return and view the version # in *Client Resource Management*    Actual Result:  The  version # in *Client Resource Management* *never increments* after doing any of the above functions    Expected Result:  The version # should have incremented after any of the above operations completed.    Note:  Manually incrementing the version by clicking the *Increment Version* appears to work.  At least you get a # +1 of what was previously showing in the UI    There are zero errors logged in either the Event/Log Viewer or log file    """
comments: "I don't think install new extension need refresh the CDF version, as it won't impact any exist css/js files and need update cache. assign to Chris for confirm." [SEP] "of course, a module might contain module.css, a skin skin.css,  which might be updated when installing a new versions." [SEP] "pls note that its install new extension but not upgrade exist one." [SEP] "When this should be updated (CDF versioning)...  - Upgrades (the platform itself; this way it is only done once as part of upgrade and NOT for every single installed package)  - When someone clicks the save button for portal.css (under site settings; this should trigger a version bump)  - Upgrade of existing module packages (even on the fence about this one)    When it should *NOT* happen...  - Installation of a new module (that didn't previously exist)  - When server cache is cleared  " [SEP] "Verified in latest version:  1. Upgrades (the platform itself; this way it is only done once as part of upgrade and NOT for every single installed packag

summary: "SI: DDR Menu localization"
description: """Using DDRmenu to output the menu. This works well for en-US, es-MX, and tr-TR, but fails for the remaining languages nl-NL, fr-FR, en-GB, and es-ES. Steps to reproduce:  http://screencast.com/t/dPw6syi6PXH7    *Another client reported same issue:*  I exported my site and then imported it to a mirrored server, and the localization works perfectly...except for the DDR menus. I've gone through all the pages, enabled/translated/published/updated, etc. The page names are translated (the URLs come up with the translated page names), and the appropriate pages have """"include in menu"""" checked.  I even re-uploaded the menu and skin files in case something got corrupted."""

summary: "Path paremters in roles administration should be case insensitive but break paging functionality"
description: """If you change the the path parameter """"UserId"""" to """"userid"""" in URLs like   http://.../Admin/User-Accounts/ctl/Edit/mid/397/UserId/2/filter/All/currentpage/1  breaks the paging feature.    To reproduce the issue:  - Login as site admin  - Assign more than 10 roles to a user  - Open page """"Manage Roles for User: [...]"""" (for example by disabling pop-ups and click the edit roles icon for that user on page /Admin/User-Accounts)  - Replace UserId by userid in the URL  - Try to use the paging links below the roles table"""

summary: "SI: Journal Module content is not searchable"
description: """I have a customer whom is running the Journal Module on a page [http://sackings.com/Community] that is using all Types and has multiple forum type entries as content.  I have selected a comment from charlie rozell as """"good luck this year"""" then performed an Advanced Search on the comment within quotations to get an exact match and it returned 'No Results Found'.  For troubleshooting steps I edited the Journal Module on the server and made certain that the *Allow Indexing?* field was indeed checked.  As far as I can tell nothing else needs to be set to allow indexing for this module on the Community page of the site."""
comments: "I know the code was added for this in early 7.x versions (wasn't in 6.x and it was before 7.3). You can see the actual code for indexing here: https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/blob/development/DNN%20Platform/Modules/Journal/Components/FeatureController.cs#L108.     I tried to reproduce the problem and I cannot. It is important to note that the search indexing is a scheduled process that happens every couple of minutes (by default) but it is possible this was changed by the super user. " [SEP] "Reproduced in Platform 8. " [SEP] "Question regarding indexing of Journal: the Journal stores many activities, which are performed in other modules. IMO a single text should show up in Search index only once (from origin), not the replicated text in Journal. This also makes it easier to evaluate permission, as only Journal permission needs to be evaluated, not the permission of the target item (Forum post, ...)" [SEP] "this issue need separate to 2 parts:  1. in 8.0.1 journal module's content can't be search

summary: "Quick Settings > always close the modal right after call the save or cancel callback"
description: """The ModuleAction Quick Settings always close the modal right after call the save or cancel callback.    See below Line 450 of ModuleActions.js      The current implementation does not allow the callback to stop the closing menu action (i.e.: I want keep the modal open as my form has validation errors).    We cannot just solve the problem having the save callback return true/false as the code inside that callback will be probably async (i.e.: ajax call to persist data).     One possible solution would require that both cancel and save callbacks return a promise. The plugin at this point could properly manage the success and the error scenario."""
comments: "Now the function onSave and onCancel passed as parameters of the quick settings constructor have been changed to return a promise, this way the logic to close the modal is executed only after these operations have finished correctly.    [~accountid:<USER>, could you please review my changes and merge them into development?  https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/969  " [SEP] "Code review done. It looks good. Merged PR in build #8.0.0.477.    *Note for QA*: this is a change that affects mostly developer that wants implemente its own quick setting and is not easily testeable. As part of this change we have adapted this new mechanism to the Quick Setting menu in the DCC Viewer.  Please let us know if you want know more about the technical nature of this change." [SEP] "This is more of a dev task as per details by Francesco. Closing this issue as done. The Quick Setting can be closed and no adverse effect was introduced on functionality level on UI."

summary: "DCC - Error creating new content field with invalid field type."
description: """When a user adds a new data field to a content type, by default the selected field type is the text """"Content types"""", which is not a real field type but a UI separator. It is possible to go ahead and create the data field but with an incorrect field type (there is no validation in the server side). Accessing again to the content type causes Http 500 errors.  Please watch: http://screencast.com/t/4aXXwLyhPqw9    *Steps to reproduce*  - Log in as host  - Edit a content type  - Press the Add field button  - Enter a name, label and description for the data field (leave field type as it is)  - Press save  - Reload the DCC-Manager again  - Edit again the same content type    *Expected result*  - The content type information and its data fields are loaded and shown in the UI    *Actual result*  - The inputs are empty an a HTTP 500 error is logged in the browser console    *NOTE: The UI issue only happens with Firefox and IE*    Not only we should not allowed invalid data fields: a server side validation for the content field data should be implemented and the errors coming it should be shown in the UI nicely (on a modal popup window, as in other parts of the DCC - Admin)"""

summary: "SPA Modules need a method for using custom tokens"
description: """h2. Card  As a developer, I want to be able to define custom token types for my SPA module.  These tokens should be automatically included as part of the TokenReplace process inthe SPA module.    h2. Conversation  The token creation needs to occur in server-side code.  This can be done in the """"BusinessController"""" as that is a defined location for every module.    If we define a new interface, then module developers can return a dictionary<string, IPropertyAccess> that contains the tokens.  We should include the page and modulecontext when calling the interface.      h2. Confirmation  This feature should also include a sample implementation in the Dynamic Content Type Manager so that we can verify the implementation and show users how it can be used in their own modules."""

summary: "Add portability to DCC"
description: """I think the Dynamic Content Viewer module needs to implement {{IPortable}}, so that it can be part of page and site templates, maintaining a copy of its content."""
comments: "I've submitted a pull request at https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/932 that implements {{IPortable}} for the viewer module.  One thing we noticed was that it doesn't totally work when importing into an existing module, because the content type ID setting isn't applied (if the module is already setup for the right content type, it _does_ work).  It does work for page templates and site templates without issue.    In addition to the Viewer, this should probably _also_ include portability for the DCC Manager module, for content types, data types, and templates that aren't global." [SEP] "One other place this could be improved would be to support cross-installation templates, which would require removing ambiguity in the content type IDs and template IDs." [SEP] "As noted by [~accountid:<USER> in the pull request, this item is going to be closed as won't fix for now. We will create a new jira for IPortable once we complete the necessary changes in the schema."

summary: "Cannot edit Dynamic Content item when value has comma"
description: """When saving a content item in the DCC Viewer module, if there is a string data field with a comma in the value, there is an error thrown.  This does not affect Rich Text or Markdown, because they are handled differently.  It would also, theoretically, affect Integer or Float fields if a comma was in the value (e.g. as a decimal or thousands separator).    The culprit is the {{GetValueOrDefault}} call in {{ViewerController.ProcessFields}}, which uses {{ToLookup}}, which splits values on commas (in {{ParseValues}}).    For example, but the DCC Viewer module on the page, select _Employee_ as the content type, and save.  Reload the page, then select _Edit Content_ from the action menu.  Enter content with a comma into one of the text fields (e.g. set the Title to _Vice President, Marketing and Sales_), and click the Save button.  You should see a couple of errors, one that looks like this:    """
comments: "https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/889" [SEP] "Move this issue to QA as the above pull request has been merged 10 days ago by a community member." [SEP] "Verified and fixed in Platform v8.0 build 423.    Entering comma does not throw exception any longer."

summary: "Performance need improve when change page"
description: """Reproduce Steps:  install 8.0 latest version, visit Admin/Dynamic Content Type Manager page.    1. when switch page, the process bar is show after page content done, it should show during the ajax call. and looks like the render content in client need improve performance, if you change page several times, the new page content will render very slowly(use with seconds), and browser is frozen in these seconds. notice that there is a call with pagsize=1000 in console. comments: "Submitted a PR for the performance issue. Removed the line that unnecessarily called the API for the second time.    Note: The list is STILL populated before the progress bar has loaded. This is due to the loading bar function in the dnn.utility.js library, which relies on a variable being set in the AJAX completion of the API call, rather than relying on the array being finished populating. As this library is most likely connected to others, I am not sure what the best way is to fix this, please advise." [SEP] "I changed the code a bit : instead of removing completely the call to getAllContentTypes() it is only done when a Content type is saved. Otherwise, we could create a new content type and it will not be available on the combobox until refreshing the whole page. " [SEP] "Could you

summary: "oAuth Support - We shouldn't allow oAuth to be enabled if oAuth Library not enabled"
description: """In the new oAuth Server implementation if you turn on oAuth in Host > Host Settings > Other Settings we throw a labelled warning in the UI:  *Warning: The OAuth library must be installed for OAuth server support to work*  And then we allow enabling oAuth Authorization even though the Library has not yet been installed.    It would actually be better UI/UX if we *did not allow oAuth Authorization to be enabled* and threw a pop up that said something like:  *The oAuth Library is not installed. Please click here to install it then enable oAuth Authorization*    Install or upgrade to Platform 8 (i used build 373)  Log in as HOST  Go Host > Host Settings > Other Settings  Scroll to the bottom of the page  Tick the box for """"*Enable OAuth Authorization*""""  Click """"Update""""    Actual Result:  You see a text warning label:    *Warning: The OAuth library must be installed of OAuth server support to work*    And the check box is enabled even though all the pieces are not installed in the application    Expected Result:  If you tick the box for """"*Enable OAuth Authorization*"""" a check is done to verify the OAuth Library is installed.    If it is then no messaging and when the blue """"Update"""" button is clicked changes are saved and persist.    If it is not then when the check fails a pop up renders with text like:  *The OAuth Library is not installed. Please go here (linked text to Host > Extensions > Available Extensions) and install the Library now.*    """
comments: "Removed from product (before shipping). "

summary: "JS Libraries requested during PreRender are not included in page"
description: """When a control (module, skin, etc.) requests a JavaScript library, that request is not honored if it is made during the {{PreRender}} event.  This is because the JavaScript Library requests are processed during the page's {{PreRender}} event handler, which occurs before its controls' {{PreRender}} handlers.  Specifically, for DNN-7657 I'm unable to use JS libraries because the DDR Menu is rendered in the control's {{PreRender}} handler."""

summary: "Display on all pages: unselecting this option throws an error"
description: """When checking """"display module on all pages"""", the module is added by default to the following pages :  Activity feed (+3 subpages), Search and 404 (plus existing user pages)  When unchecking, an error is thrown and the module is deleted from the 4 activity feed pages, but not from Search and 404    To produce :  1. add a module and check """"display on all pages""""  2. Observe in the settings which pages have been affected  3. Uncheck """"display on all pages"""" and get an error  4. observe that the module has been deleted from a limited number of pages    Expected behavior    1. A consistent behavior (either the module should be deleted from ALL existing pages, or from NONE)  2. A nice error catch, is any, and the reason  3. Considering the consequences, this option DESERVES A REAL HELP message, particularly on UNCHECKING (which results in DELETIONS)   4. The option """"New pages only"""" should apply for unchecking as well as for checking (when unchecking, do you want just the module not to display any more on future pages, or do you want to delete it from where it is already). It should then be """"Affect new pages only"""" rather than just """"Add to new pages only""""     """
comments: "Additional note: one consequence is that after deleting the module from all visible pages, the admin is left with ghost modules in the search and 404 pages, with probably no easy way for him to delete them"

comments: "Note this is due to the assembly binding settings in web.config which redirect to 5.0.0.0 - rather than 5.1.0.0" [SEP] "Submitted PR https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/861" [SEP] "Merged PR https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/861" [SEP] "Verified in clean install & upgrade that the Assembly Binding schema <runtime>      <assemblyBinding xmlns=""urn:schemas-microsoft-com:asm.v1""> now contains:          <dependentAssembly>          <assemblyIdentity name=""System.Web.Http"" publicKeyToken=""31BF3856AD364E35"" />          <bindingRedirect oldVersion=""0.0.0.0-5.1.0.0"" newVersion=""5.1.0.0"" />        </dependentAssembly>          <dependentAssembly>          <assemblyIdentity name=""System.Net.Http.Formatting"" publicKeyToken=""31bf3856ad364e35"" />          <bindingRedirect oldVersion=""0.0.0.0-5.1.0.0"" newVersion=""5.1.0.0"" />        </dependentAssembly>        <dependentAssembly>          <assemblyIdentity name=""System.Web.Http.WebHost"" publicKeyToken=""31bf3856ad364e35"" />

summary: "Enable OAuth Authorization has no help text"
description: """Install latest build of Platform 8  When logged in as Host go Host > Host Settings > Other Settings  Scroll to the bottom of the page  Hover over the 'i' for Enable OAuth Authorization    Actual Result:  There's no 'i' so no help text pop up    Expected Result;  There is an 'i'   There is a help pop up     Lorraine will need to document the help text """
comments: "We are going to remove this host setting (since we are going to remove oAuth for 8). If/when we introduce it, it shouldn't be a host setting anyway. "

summary: "A page that looks normal in the top level menu can have a null PortalID"
description: """The case happens when logged as superadmin you just want to admin a website and create a page forgetting that you """"start"""" from a host page.     What happens then is you realize the page is in host and you CAN move it to the top level menu (no parent page), where you can see it like any valid page.    But it keeps a null PortalID that has unfortunate consequences later. Amoung others, you cannot delete this page (error message : """"negative PortaID"""")    1. Log as host  2. Create a page.   3. Move it to the top level menu  4. Try to delete  5. Observe that you can still perform many standard actions (including localize it!), without noticing the problem (page has a null PortalID)    Expected behavior    - at a minimum, a warning """"you're host, this page will not appear in any website""""  - check if PortaID is correctly set to null, or if it should be -1 (I'm not sure about this)  - keep some visible UI to help remember that this page is special  - authorize deletion    """
comments: "We moved away from Host and Admin menus as part of 9.0. "

summary: "Update Build scripts so uninstalled assemblies are not in bin"
description: """In the related issue we have done what we can to remove unnecessary assemblies from the bin, but the Team City builds still add assemblies from components that are not being installed into the bin.  In an Install or Upgrade package there should only be the minimum assemblies needed to install/upgrade.  All other asemblies will be deployed by the Installer if necessary.    The following should not be copied to the bin in the package:    * Dnn.Modules.*** - any module with this namespace  * DNNConnect.CKEditorProvider.dll  * DotNetNuke.Modules.*** - any module in this namespace  * DotNetOpenAuth.**  * OAuth.**  * DotNetNuke.Authentication.**  * *.designer.cs  * *.less"""
comments: "I did diff on the install zip of Basic before and after port over to 8.0 and found additional issues. Following must also be removed:    *.designer.cs  *.less" [SEP] "Updated the packaging scripts to exclude the above items from the bin folder of the package." summary: "PortalController.DeletePortalSetting clears cache but does not remove PortalSettingsDictionary from current HttpContext if any, which can lead to inconsistencies"
description: """As the summaries says, not only the cache must be cleared but the dictionary of portalsettings must be removed from current http context if any.    One test case where it fails, is when you clear or update a setting and save again the same setting within the same httprequest. The UpdatePortalSetting method uses the httpcontext dictionary through GetPortalSettingsDictionary to check if the value changed, it is not aware that the setting has been cleared or changed, and as a result, the second save will be cancelled.    The fix should be to remove the dictionary from the httpcontext if any, in all places where only the cache is currently cleared (Update, Delete etc.)    On a related note but of less importance, the PortalSettings.Timezone property setter, which is called when building the portalsettings on each requests, ends up calling unnecessary UpdatePortalSetting on each request, but that does not result on a database call, which would have been detected, but instead is intercepted by the same mechanism involving the httpcontext dictionary through GetPortalSettingsDictionary. It is still probably a bad idea that the setter that is regularly invoked on non transient instances is attached to that update call."""

summary: "Page Hierarchy is NOT preserved when changing languages within a site"
description: """Create a 7.4.2 site with multiple languages (FR, IT, DE, etc)  OR  Upgrade to 7.4.2 with a site that has  multiple languages (FR, IT, DE, etc) installed  *After upgrading re-install applicable Language Packs to get 7.4.2 translations*    *All languages must be enabled, active and published*    Log in as Host or Administrator  Create a couple of top level pages  Create at least 2 child pages for each top level domain  *Make sure all new pages are view-able by all users*    Hit the site in a different browser  Start in English and verify the top level and child page positions  Click through each language and verify the top level and child page positions    Actual Result:  In English the page hierarchy is preserved  In any other language the child pages move to the Top Level    Expected Result:  Page hierarchy is preserved regardless of language selected      *NOTES*  If you manually localize pages under the *""""Content Localization""""* tab after you have enabled, actived and published languages then there are no page hierarchy issues"""
comments: "Submitted PR - https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/837 for 7.4.2    and    https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/838 for 8.0.0" [SEP] "This is verified as fixed in Platform 7.4.2 build 215 clean installation & upgrade.  Please note that with this change you either simply enable a language (Don't enable Content Localization)  OR  Enable Content Localization then enable, make Content Localization active then publish your languages   Go back and manually mark pages in the parent/child hierarchy as ""Translated""   Now you have Content Localization that preserves page hierarchical navigation within your site "

summary: "Cannot find a superuser by email."
description: """The class* DotNetNuke.Entities.Users.UserController* has a method to search user by a given email: *GetUsersByEmail*.  When the email we want to search for is the one of a regular user, it finds the user. However, if we use the email of a superuser, the method does not find him/her.    *Steps to reproduce*   - Programmatically, call the GetUsersByEmail in the class UserController. Use the email of a superuser, the right portalId value, and the parameters: pageIndex = 0, pageSize = 5, includeDeleted = false, superUsersOnly = false    *Expected result*   - The superuser is retrieved by the method call    *Actual result*   - No superuser is found    Note: The problem seems to be related to the stored procedure *GetUsersByEmail* and the view *vw_Users*"""
comments: "if a portalID is passed, superuser records are returned only if there is a userportal record for the user and the current portal. Again I suggest to create userportal records for superusers in all portals." [SEP] "Closing as Wont Fix (Ref. SOCIAL-4257)  * SuperUser should not be searchable by email"

summary: "IUpgradable - Add support for non-version based upgrades"
description: """The core Upgrade.cs has the ability to execute code on all version upgrades in addition to the version specific upgrades.    This enhancement will provide the option for IUpgradable on modules to execute code on a new Install and/or any upgrade, by adding the pseudo-versions """"Install"""" and """"Upgrade"""".    """"Install"""" - will execute on any new Install    """"Upgrade"""" will execute on any/all upgrades regardless of whether the current version and the new version are different.    As the """"Upgrade"""" pseudo-version code will execute on any/all upgrades it must be re-entrant.    *Testing Notes:*  - This impacts any extension installed (not the actual upgrade/install of the platform itself)  -- You would want to try installing a module (one that is new, also try an upgrade scenario) to make sure things install without problems (this is the regression aspect, not the actual issue)  - Typical users won't notice anything, the change was in the manifest file (.dnn) for developers as well as the installer of the platform (to read the manifest)  -- This allows you to specify which business logic to run on install and/or upgrade (for the module)  - Install the DCC admin module"""

summary: "File Manager > StandardFolderProvider GetUrl uses LinkClick for file with parentesis in the name"
description: """In the StandardFolderProvider.GetUrl method there is a check that makes use of LinkClick for file with invalid URL characters in the name:     This is done to use LinkClick when IIS cannot serve the file.    However this does not follow the RFC2396 URL specification and parentesis should not be included in the above regex.    *Step to Reproduce*  * Go to Admin > File Manager  * Upload a file with parentesis in the name, i.e.: my(file).jpg.  * Check file Url    *Actual Result*  * A link click url is returned by the system    *Expected Result*  * A normal url should be returned and IIS should be able to return the image browsing that url    Note: we should investigate why the parentesis was added in this regex. Likely some old version of IIS was not able to serve url with parentesis. I have tested with IIS 8 and is able to serve fine images with parentesis in the name."""
comments: "Fixed. I have replaced the existing regex by one that follows the norm  RFC2396 2.2 -- https://www.w3.org/2002/11/dbooth-names/rfc2396-numbered_clean.htm  Only there is one exception, the ""/"" character should be allowed because the provider checks the full file path, and it contains the ""/"" between folder levels. However, DAM has other mechanism to disallow this character as part of the filename so there is no problem with it.    In addition, following an existing IIS impediment -- see issue DNN-7036 --, I have added the character ""%"" as an invalid one in spite of the fact that it is not included in the above RFC. So, if a file contains the character ""%"" in its name, then the URL will be as a Linkclick one     Please, [~accountid:<USER>, could you review my changes and merge the pull request?  https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1251    TIA." [SEP] "Reviewed and merged build 8.0.1.80" [SEP] "Verified fixed in Platform 8.0.1 build 96 & Content 8.4.0 build 57 clean install & upgrade  In al

summary: "SI: AUM - URL conflicts & doesn't match with Page URL field"
description: """The customer would like developer to review 'dnn.PageSettings.View.js' as looks like a bug in DNN.    /DesktopModules/UrlManagement/API/UrlManagementService/GetSystemGeneratedUrls?sortField=0&sortOrder=true that gets run when the settings dialog loads, which returns a list of generated URLs with response codes for the current page. Viewing the response of this URL (attached) shows that a page url is returned for every Site Alias along with a StatusCode. But towards the end it's returning the url /vehicles/all-vehicles.aspx for the alias www.apollocamper.com/camping-cars/australie, which isn't the alias of the page that I'm currently viewing.     This row is also returning the StatusCode 200. The Javascript that reads this file (\DesktopModules\Admin\UrlManagement\ClientScripts\dnn.PageSettings.View.js line 205) then sees the 200 StatusCode and thinks that's the current Page URL, and then updates the Page URL field and also the FORM action to post the settings form, which then gives the error because that URL is actually a 301 redirect.    *Steps to Replicate:*  - Deploy the site and navigate to a page in the site ie http://dnndev/campervan-hire-vehicles.aspx  - Under the Edit Page menu, select Page Settings  - Here you'll notice the current URL, which I updated when setting up the site: campervan-hire-vehicles, doesn't match what's in the Page URL textbox (see image attached : http://screencast.com/t/0obry4bqe )  - After clicking the Update Page button, nothing happens. Looking in the Chrome console, it's trying to submit the form to the old URL that's currently displayed in the textbox, which of course redirects to the correct URL (see image attached : http://screencast.com/t/mRwECOw0YK )"""
comments: "Not reproducible on 9.0 and we don't have this js file any more. Marked as Won't Fix." [SEP] "Closing this JIRA as CNR in current builds..."

summary: "Allow releases without an initial incremental file to support incremental upgrades"
description: """When a DNN website has an application domain recycle a check is made to see if an incremental file exists to see if an incremental upgrade may occur (i.e. where the DNN version has not changed but an xx.xx.xx.xx.sqldataprovider exists)    At present this check has a few short-circuits to ensure it doesn't affect performance after app domain recycle. For instance if the dotnetnuke.dll assembly version is less than 7.4.2 then an incremental check is not done as incremental updates did not exist in versions prior to that release.    A second check occurs to see if more than 1 incremental exists - this is based on the assumption that a release that will support incremental updates will have at least one xx.xx.xx.xx.sqldataprovider initially so that the """"increment"""" column in the """"version"""" table is populated. If more than 1 file of that naming structure exists we then run a stored procedure to see if the last applied increment is < than the number of incremental files (and if not then run the code to apply the incremental sql, config and cleanup changes). By doing this second check we can avoid doing the stored procedure call for the case where only 1 incremental file exists.    However the 7.4.2 beta release did not contain a xx.xx.xx.xx.sqldataprovider file so this second check returns false (as only 1 incremental file exists and it assumes that's been applied by the beta). We need to remove this second check to support 7.4.2 beta being upgraded and to support any future releases that do not contain an incremental file when releasing an upgradeable beta/ctp."""

summary: "EVOQCONTENT - Auto Installation/Upgrade > hit the site again during installation makes the IUpgradable runs in parallel with portal 0 creation"
description: """Auto Installation/Upgrade > hit the site again during installation makes the IUpgradable runs in parallel with portal 0 creation.    *Step to reproduce*  * Go to auto install  * Open a new browser tab while the install is in process  * From the new browser tab hit again the site    *Actual result*  * The IUpgradable runs in parallel with the portal creation process making the result of the installation unpredictable or even broken.    *Expected result*  * The installation process should prevent this managing any other request to the site and run IUpgradable only once the installation + portal creation end properly.    Note: this is an issue that likely exists since 7.2 (when the code base was moved to github) or even before.    """
comments: "I have been investigated the Platform code.    The method *Init* in DNN Platform\Library\Common\Initialize.cs have a logic to do not run the Init App (and the IUpgradable) if detects that the system is under install or upgrade. This check relies to the *Globals.Status* property. This property is calculated based on the difference between the DotNetNuke.dll assembly version and the database version.    So when the site is surfed using a new browser tab this check could return Status.None (No Install or Upgrade) if the version has been written in the database but the installation has not complete yet (i.e: still pending to install some modules or create the portal 0). Note: The status of the installation is calculated once per application start, but as the installation makes changes in web.config the new request provokes a new application start and a new calculation of the Global.Status property value.  " [SEP] "Good points Francesco.    We should delay updating the version table until we are truly finished OR

summary: "Change how pages are defined in manifest"
description: """The module manifest was updated to include a method for creating admin/host page when a module is installed.  The method used was to add nodes to the Module component which defined the page.  This should be updated to include all of the page sub-elements in a single Page element.  The component should include the ability to define whether the page is a host page or admin page.  It should include the ability to flag whether a module appears in the common or advanced sections of the menu.  The page name, icon file(s) and description should be included as elements withing the component.  An example entry would look like: {code:title=Page Component|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#EBF2F9|bgColor=#FFFFCE} <page type=""""Admin"""" common=""""true"""">   <name>Configuration Manager</name>   <icon>~/Icons/Sigma/Configuration_16X16_Standard.png</icon>   <largeIcon>~/Icons/Sigma/Configuration_32X32_Standard.png</largeIcon>   <description>Modify configuration settings for your site</description> </page> {code}  A page type must be defined as either admin or host (case insensitive). The common flag will default to false if not defined.  The name is the only required element in the page element.  The """"common"""" flag should be stored as a """"term"""" on the page and the control panel should use this """"term"""" to determine what gets displayed in the common section.  The manifest section of the Wiki should be updated to reflect the manifest changes.  ! Confirmation This requires updating all existing admin modules to use the new format."""

summary: "SI: Adding redirects for the Redirect After Login/logout does not appear in menu"
description: """Adding redirects to external or internal pages, and when using the """"Redirect After Login"""" and """"Redirect After Logout"""" options does not display the page in the drop-down menu    Steps to reproduce:  1) Create a two new pages: RedirectAfterLOGIN & RedirectAfterLOGOUT  2) With the permissions, ensure All User have View permissions for these pages  3) For each new page, ensure the Link drop-down option is set to either the URL or Page option  4) Ensure Open Link In New Browser Window is select if you choose the URL option  5) Update Page when you have made the necessary changes to each page  6) Navigate to Admin > Site Settings > User Account Settings > Login Settings drop-down: Redirect After Login & Redirect After Logout.  7) Try to find the new pages: RedirectAfterLOGIN & RedirectAfterLOGOUT in the drop-down menu    Result:  The new pages DO NOT display in the drop-down menu (when redirects already exist on the page)    Expected Result:  The new pages would display in the drop-down menu, and would redirect as selected in the Link drop-down for each respective page"""
comments: "We don't support redirecting to a page which is itself just a redirect. Login/Logout/Registration redirects are intended to be a redirect to a page within the current website. As such redirecting to a page which is itself is just a redirect is inefficient and the Login/Logout/Registration redirect should point to the ultimate destination page."

summary: "PortalAliasRouteManager function GetRouteName returns wrong Name when ApplicationPath is not empty"
description: """This is related to the way Route names are built from aliases counting segments.    When Routes are initially registered, in ServicesRoutingManager.MapHttpRoute, method PortalAliasRouteManager.GetRoutePrefixCounts is called, and that method uses PortalAliasRouteManager.StripApplicationPath(aliases) before calling PortalAliasRouteManager.CalcAliasPrefixCount() when counting the prefix segments.    Conversely, method PortalAliasRouteManager.GetRouteName(string moduleFolderName, string routeName, PortalAliasInfo portalAlias), makes a direct calls to CalcAliasPrefixCount, which returns an greater integer when ApplicationPath is not empty.    This results in a crash when calling UrlHelperExtensions.DnnLink(this UrlHelper urlHelper, string moduleFolderName, string routeName, object routeValues), which is quite significant.    It shouldn't be too hard to make those 2 alternate code paths consistent. """

summary: "EVOQCONTENT - Password UI issues"
description: """This goal of this issue is to tweak the overall experience of password validation. Everything else in place today (the overall experience and design) are to remain unchanged.     *Password Policy Tooltip*:   Currently, when attempting to set (or change) the password the tooltip shown dispalys as you type (not ideal) and it also displays what makes a strong password; not the policy itself. Users need to be aware of the policy they are to match, so we should only show rules in the tooltip that are relevant to the site's password policy.     *Actual Result*: the password tooltip shows part of the password policy that are not really validated against the server and that are more suggestion than policy password rules (see image below):  !ActualPasswordPolicyTooltip.png!  *Expected Result*: The policy password tooltip should only show rules based on site settings, such as site settings config as:  !20150915094024.png!    Those rules will each be on their own line (as they are today) and we should look to the regex here to see if it makes use of a few rules we should let the user know about. Overall, these are the rules which should display in the tooltip:  - X (count required) non-alphanumeric (special) characters  - X (count required) total characters  - CriteriaValidationExpression.Text or if not set """"Validation Expression: [REGEX]""""      *When the value in the password box doesn't match to this policy we will...*  - The tooltip will be shown when it doesn't match when the user attempts to tab (or click) away (thus, onblur event on client-side)  - The tooltip will show as shown in the screenshot below:  !20150915094816.png!    *Note*: by default the system shows ONLY the """"At least X characters"""", """"the non-alphanumeric"""" and """"validation expression"""" are not shown as them not configured in the web.config file.    * when put mouse on the meter bar, it will always show the tooltip, no matter whether password match the setting.  * typing in Confirm should show “x” in field until it matches password field."""

What I am suggesting is that we remove the regex from the tooltip and instead show one of the above rules (if the regex requires it). summary: "Change default robots.txt to allow client side resources"
description: """+Background+  Google has changed the way that it crawls pages, so that it can properly render and understand the content in a page.    Part of this is accessing the js, css and image files that go into many webpages, particularly if they use a delayed load.    As per the [official guidelines from Google Webmaster tools|http://googlewebmastercentral.blogspot.ro/2014/10/updating-our-technical-webmaster.html]:  {quote}  For optimal rendering and indexing, our new guideline specifies that you should allow Googlebot access to the JavaScript, CSS, and image files that your pages use. This provides you optimal rendering and indexing for your site. Disallowing crawling of Javascript or CSS files in your site’s robots.txt directly harms how well our algorithms render and index your content and can result in suboptimal rankings.  {quote}    The existing standard robots.txt file by default blocks access to specific DNN/CSS paths.    +Change+  * *Remove* the following lines from the googlebot section of the robots.txt file:      This is a simple change which will help with Googlebot crawling Dnn sites still using the default robots.txt file.  """
comments: "Is this change going to be retroactive (i.e. will it update existing {{robots.txt}} files)?  Also, {{DesktopModules}} and {{Resources}} need to be unblocked as well, since they have CSS and JS in them.    Is there value in the other exclusions that the file presently has?  I can see someone (though not everyone) wanting to avoid blocking the user account pages, I guess, but what are we trying to solve by disallowing access to these other paths?" [SEP] "Brian - good point on the DesktopModules folder - perhaps that and the Resources folder as well.  I don't think there is any risk in allowing indexing of those folders.    The user profiles were excluded to remove the 'payload' for registration spam.  By excluding indexing on user profile pages, any spam postings to those user accounts is not going to be indexed." [SEP] "I somehow missed the comments regarding DesktopModules and Resources prior to submitting pull request #957.  If it needs to be updated to remove these two, just let me know." [SEP] "I've merge

summary: "SI: Search looks through the content and tags before the title of the article"
description: """When you search for articles using the title of the article as a search query, that title does not show up as the first one in the search result    *Steps to Replicate:*  # Create a Wiki Article with random content and a title  # Create a few more articles with different titles but contain the terms in the title of the article in step 1 in the content of all the articles as well as the tags of all other articles.   # Search for an article based on the title of the article in step 1    *Expected Results:*  The article whose title matches the search query will the first one in the search results.     *Actual Results:*  The article whose title matches the search query does not show up as the first one in the results.    !image004.png|thumbnail!    !image007.jpg|thumbnail! """
comments: "This behavior has also been tested by upgrading the customer site on my local machine to 8.2 and using the search boost feature to boost the titles as higher priority.    Search results won’t be ordered properly if you have more than 1 tags on the search file and meanwhile Tag Boost greater than 0, the system will then apply tag boost multiply times when indexing files, this causes the tag gets far more weight/score in searching.   !tagboost.jpg|thumbnail! " [SEP] "Lucene has a rule, when a document is added to the index, all the factors are multiplied. If the document has multiple fields with the same name, all their boosts are multiplied together. And in our system, we save each tag in a field with the same name, so when a page has multi tags, the tag field will have a very high boost value so that the document could be shown at the very top of search result improperly. I'll improve our code to avoid that by only applying boost once on the tag field, for now the workaround is that we just give a boost va

summary: "Auto Installation/Upgrade > when client interrupts server communication the installation breaks"
description: """Auto Installation/Upgrade > when client interrumps server communication the installation breaks leaving the site in an unpredictable state.    In auto installation and upgrade if clients closes the browser or for any other action cuases the system stops the communication with the server the installation process stops and the system fires the following exception, leaving the site in inconsistent and unpredictable state:      The main problem here is the the server flush to the client without checking if the client is connected first. So when the client is not connected the system raise an exception no handled by the installation/upgrade process. This leave the installation in a broken and unpredictable state.    Note: This is probably a very old issue that affects the auto install in almos all the last years DNN versions.    *Expected result*  The expected result should be confirmed with PM:  * Leave the installation continue in background and complete  * Stop the installation in a predictable manner (i.e.: complete the installation of the current package and then stop the installation)"""
comments: "[~accountid:<USER> [~accountid:<USER> We're thinking of making {{WriteFeedback}} (which is only called form {{Upgrade}} and {{Install}}) swallow any {{HttpException}} when writing to the response, to avoid killing the install.  We're seeing a lot of these on our sites hosted on Azure.  Do you see any problems with changing that method to swallow those exceptions?  Do we need a flag to throw them optionally?  Should they be logged?    Thanks!" [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER>, I made a small modification to the original PR and would like you to review and merge as you see fit.    *For QA:*  To test the issue you need to follow these steps:  - Install a previous version of DNN (any product)  - Extract the corresponding upgrade package for 9.2.0 in the site's root folder  - Start the *AUTO* installer (not the wizard) and as soon as you see 3 or more modules were installed close the browse window.  - In the meantime, have a Windows Explorer window open for /install/modules of your site to monitor the files there.    *S

summary: "Sproc GetDuplicateEmailCount errors out, if there are more than one duplicate emails"
description: """Stored procedure returns an error, if there are more than one duplicate emails in users table.  Error message: subquery returns more than one record    Steps to reproduce:  - enter site settings on a website like dnnsoftware.com  - set to use email address as login (require unique email addresses)  - click save  - will return error message above  - expected: no error message, but number of items    Besides, the procedure misses to check superusers, who need to be included in the unique check"""
comments: "I'll submit a fix" [SEP] "PR #777 submitted.  Fix is also contained in TurboDNN 0.9.9 rev.a" [SEP] "PR corrected.   Note: It might be more appropriate to exclude soft-deleted users, however, this would require adding a check in DNN code, whenever a user is re-activated." [SEP] "Note:  There are two use cases for this stored procedure:  - when a website user is created and added to a site (PortalID >= 0)  - when a superuser is added to the system (PortalID = -1 or is Null), who needs to be unique across all sites" [SEP] "Hi [~accountid:<USER>, as we commented in the existing pull request, we would need a valid pull request against the development branch before we can merge this. Thanks." [SEP] "@Antonio: sorry, I can't waste additional time with your tooling atm and pulled the request.  FIx is included in TurboDNN for my clients and users.  I'll try to re-submit next year, may be I'll have more luck to get it accepted." [SEP] "Descoped from 8.0.0 release based on [~accountid:<USER>'s comment." [SEP] "improved

summary: "Log4Net configuration not honored for size of logs or number to keep"
description: """I have multiple sites on 7.4.1 and 7.3.4, all of which use the default log4net configuration found in the DotNetNuke.log4net.config file:    <?xml version=""""1.0"""" encoding=""""utf-8"""" ?>  <log4net>    <appender name=""""RollingFile"""" type=""""log4net.Appender.RollingFileAppender"""">      <file value=""""Portals/_default/Logs/"""" />      <datePattern value=""""yyyy.MM.dd'.log.resources'"""" />      <rollingStyle value=""""Date"""" />      <staticLogFileName value=""""false"""" />      <appendToFile value=""""true"""" />      <maximumFileSize value=""""10MB"""" />      <maxSizeRollBackups value=""""5"""" />      <lockingModel type=""""log4net.Appender.FileAppender+MinimalLock""""/>      <layout type=""""log4net.Layout.PatternLayout"""">        <conversionPattern value=""""%date [%property{log4net:HostName}][Thread:%thread][%level] %logger - %message%newline"""" />        <locationInfo value=""""true"""" />      </layout>    </appender>    <root>      <level value=""""Error"""" />      <appender-ref ref=""""RollingFile"""" />    </root>  </log4net>    Based off the definitions of the """"maximumFileSize"""" and """"maxSizeRollBackups"""", there should only be a maximum of 5 total log files and each log file should not exceed 10MB in size. However, I have quite a few sites that won't honor either default setting.     I've attached two screenshots of one site that show the enormous file sizes and how the total number of files allowed is not honored."""

summary: "Multiple Account Login Modules on a Single Page Broken"
description: """When you use more than one Account Login module on a page, the ability to login breaks because the embedded javascript does not have a proper scope. It is a simple fix by adding a scope.    In DNN 07.04.01, line 46 looks like this:    var actionLinks = $(""""a[id$=DNN_cmdLogin]"""");    ...but can be changed to this to fix it:    var actionLinks = $(""""a#dnn_ctr<%=ModuleId%>_Login_Login_DNN_cmdLogin"""");    I have made the change in my code, but I want to ensure that it is not overwritten in subsequent upgrades."""

summary: "Create file type permissions manager"
description: """h2. Card As an super-user, I would like to be able to set upload permissions on file-types.  I should be able to delegate management of individual file types to administrators and lock other file types to only be manageable by super-users.  As an administrator, I want to be able to assign upload permissions to users or roles for file-types which have been delegated by a super-user.   h2. Conversation The system should come with a set of pre-defined file extensions as defined by our current Host Extensions list.  All file types will initially be manageable by super-users with the exception of the following types: jpg, jpeg, mpg, mpeg, mp4, wmv, mov, gif, png, svg, doc, docx, ppt, pptx, xls, xlsx, pdf, txt, xml.  The image and movie file types (jpg, jpeg, mpg, mpeg, mp4, wmv, mov, gif, png) will have an initial permission which includes the registered user role.  h2. Confirmation This should be built as a SPA module."""

summary: "Allow Modules to define host/admin pages as part of manifest"
description: """h2. Card As a module developer, I want to be able to automatically create an admin or host tab during module installation and have the module automatically added to that page.  h2. Conversation The developer should be able to use the DNN manifest to automatically create the appropriate page.  Page permissions should be automatically set based on the menu and should not require configuration in the manifest. The module should be able to set a flag to determine if the page is displayed in the common or advanced settings sections of the Admin/Host menu.  IF an admin page is defined, then it should be created for all portals in the current implementation.  The manifest logic should detect if the page already exists and if the module already exists on the page.  h2. Confirmation Uninstall of the module should delete the associated page if this is the only module on the page.  This will require modifying all of the existing Admin Modules which have already been packaged."""
comments: "Verified and tested in Platform 8.0.0 Build 457 and its passed"

summary: "Extract Vendors into a separate module"
description: """h2. Card  The Vendors Module is used for managing vendors and banners within DNN. The vendors and banners modules, APIs and database structures should be removed from the core platform and extracted into a standalone solution consisting of the Vendors module and a Banners module.  The Banners module will have a dependency on the Vendors module.  The vendors module should create a page on the Admin menu when installed.      h2. Conversation  This initial conversion effort is only focused on conversion to a module and shall not include conversion to a MVC or SPA module.    This module will move to a separate GitHub project in the github.com/dnncommunity organization.  We can drop stored procedures, but should not remove the tables in the core platform.  Install of this module should check to ensure the table is not dropped.    h2.
