summary: "Improve performance of Type Scanning"
description: """Some features require all the types to be scanned on startup, in order to determine if a type implements a specific interface.  This is currently done for Service Route Mapping and the Module Injection filters.    While removing unnecessary assemblies from the bin will improve this, there are a number of necessary assemblies (that we can't remove) that we know do not have any types.    Examples are the Telerik assemblies and the Microsoft assemblies.    We should therefore optimize the scanning of the assemblies so that these are explicitly ignored.    Secondly, we scan the types independently for each interface check - it might be possible to optimize this and scan once for both interfaces.  """
comments: "I did some modifications to the AssemblyLocator class to improve the Type Scanning logic.    Currently this class returns ""ALL"" assemblies and in my default install this consumed nearly 10% of the first page cost, as it returned 242 (121 x 2 executions) assemblies which caused 88,000 types to be scanned.     I modified the scan by eliminating known assemblies in two ways -   # if the assembly starts with Microsoft, System, Telerik or other common known namesapces it was ignored.  # if the assembly was a specific known assembly that had no Service Routes or Module Injection types it was ignored    This reduced the number of assemblies scanned to 80 (2 x 40) and the number of types scanned to just under 14,000, thus reducing the cost to 0.34% of the first page." [SEP] "Cherry-picked code to a new branch (bug/dnn-7458) based on latest development branch, and submitted as a new PR, which was merged into the latest development.    https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/851    " [SEP] "What QA should t

summary: "DNN FB Authentication provider in DNN 7.4.1 does not work with FB API v2.4"
description: """Hello,   I would like to report an issue with DNN 7.4.1 Facebook Authentication and the Facebook API v2.4.   It seems that the current DNN Authentication provider does not work with the new FB API v2.4 and it returns an """"Object reference not set to an instance of an object"""" in Event Viewer with Page Load Exception.  Since I had some old FB apps I did verify that the provider works with FB API v2.0.  Is there anyone else having this problem and is there any resolution to it?    Best,  Spyros"""
comments: "Hello,  Digging the source code of the provider (under Components\FacebookClient.cs) there is an assignment to property MeGraphEndpoint (in the class FacebookClient which is derived from base class OAuthClientBase) of the value: https://graph.facebook.com/me.  This value is then used in the GetCurrentUser<TUserData>() method to return user data in a Json formatted reply that using the Deserialize method will return as Datamembers the needed values.  As you can easily identify the ExecuteWebRequest method will use the MeGraphEndpoint property value with the access_token parameter to get the data from the authentication service. Since this is not constructed in the FacebookClient class but inside the OAuthClientBase class it cannot have any parameters added to it except the access_token parameter.     That is a show stopper and it will not allow to request the data as Facebook’s new API declares.     I have tried to use the value of https://graph.facebook.com/v2.3/me as a temporary fix but it is not working

summary: "Fix DNN-4133 introduces a major bug for all code that call AspNetMembershipProvider.CreateUser and happens to have a parameter named 'state' in the request"
description: """In version 7.2.1 issue DNN-4133 is fixed in an inappropiate manner.    If the AspNetMembershipProvider CreateUser method happens to find a parameter named 'state' in the Request.Params object, it bluntly takes over the user.UserName passed to the CreateUser method and replaces it (!) with whatever it finds in the 'state' parameter, prefixed to the user.Email.    The code is intended to fix a specific (LiveID) OAuth issue, but does not check whether it is excecuted within OAuth context.  Even within OAuth context, it is not correct to make any assumptions about the contents of the 'state' parameter and its suitability for use in the UserName,    A quote from The state parameter according to the Google Oauth 2.0 docs:  _[state] Indicates any state which may be useful to your application upon receipt of the response. The Google Authorization Server roundtrips this parameter, so your application receives the same value it sent. Possible uses include redirecting the user to the correct resource in your site, nonces, and cross-site-request-forgery mitigations._    Please remove this fix. Consider fixing the specific OAuth issue outside of the generic AspNetMembershipProvider.CreateUser method - if your OAuth implementation needs a different UserName, i.m.h.o. it should pass in a different UserName to the CreateUser call."""

summary: "Show DNN Platform version independent of Evoq Versions"

It is not always clear to customers or vendors what platform version is associated with any given Evoq version since they are no longer closely linked. summary: "Password answer not verified for password reset with requiresQuestionAndAnswer=True"
description: """If you set requiresQuestionAndAnswer=true in the web.config, the password question's answer is not verified in order to generate a password reset email.    Steps to reproduce:  * Download and install a fresh copy of DNN Platform 7.4.1  * Alter web.config to use   {quote}<add name=""""AspNetSqlMembershipProvider"""" type=""""System.Web.Security.SqlMembershipProvider"""" connectionStringName=""""SiteSqlServer"""" minRequiredPasswordLength=""""8"""" minRequiredNonalphanumericCharacters=""""1"""" maxInvalidPasswordAttempts=""""3"""" passwordAttemptWindow=""""10"""" enablePasswordReset=""""true"""" requiresQuestionAndAnswer=""""true"""" requiresUniqueEmail=""""true"""" passwordFormat=""""Hashed"""" applicationName=""""DotNetNuke"""" description=""""Stores and retrieves membership data from the local Microsoft SQL Server database"""" />{quote}  * Create a new user, noting password question answer  * Initiate password reset for new user: Login > Reset Password  * Provide username/email address  * Click """"Send Reset Link""""  * You are prompted to provide password question's answer.  * Provide any value for the answer, specifically a value other than provided during account registration, and you will still be sent the password reset link.    In /admin/Security/SendPassword.ascx.cs there is no verification of the password question answer provided.  """
comments: "So there are two problems here.    # 1 The question/answer requirements should not be in the ""Send Password"" control as we have no way to validate the Answer provided.  # 2 The question/answer challenge should be in the Reset Password control (and it is not) so the user cannot reset their password.    The reset token just provides the ability for the user to reset their password - the token itself does not do any validation" [SEP] "I have moved the Question/Answer challenge from SendPassword to ResetPassword where it can actually be validated" [SEP] "Submitted PR : https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/751" [SEP] "This change breaks the Password Reset UI    Steps to Reproduce:  Log in as HOST  Go Admin > User Accounts  Open to Edit any Registered User (create one if needed)  Scroll to the bottom of the ""Manage Account"" tab  Click ""Force Password Change"" button    In a different browser log in as the user     Actual Result:  A blank ""Reset Password"" screen appears    In the log file these e

summary: "SI: URL Query String Encoded Equals Sign Truncated"
description:
"""A query string which has an encoded equals sign will be truncated. Example: www.site.com/test?testValue1=yes&testValue2=no%3Dequals The section """"%3Dequals"""" may or may not get removed from the actual address bar, but the application trying to access it will not get it. Steps to reproduce: 1. Create a new page. 2. Add a Razor Host module to the page. 3. Add the following script content to it:

Razor reading query string value...

@Request.QueryString[""""testValue""""]

4. Test using a query string like ?testValue=test 5. Test using a query string like ?testValue=test%3Dequals Actual result: both will return """"test"""" Expected result: the second one would display """"test=equals"""" *NOTE:* This behavior changed between Evoq 8.0 and 8.1 *NOTE 2* if you are having trouble with the steps above try adding |/testPageName/ to the """"Keep in Querystring Regular Expression:"""" setting in admin > Advanced URL Managment settings so that it keeps your query string with the ?key=value syntax """

It would be nice if this setting was at the task specific level, so that you could stagger the start of tasks. Some tasks are much more resource intensive than others (analytics, search) and would benefit from not all starting at the same time, regardless of when it occurs after a recycle."""

summary: "Mute jQuery Migrate plugin by default"
description: """The jQuery Migrate plugin does not run in the """"mute"""" mode and logs output in the browser console which is not best practice for production sites.    I would recommend to include jQuery Migrate in the muted mode and if a developer is interested in the logs, he/she can disable the mute mode instead of enable logging by default.    Moreover, if jQuery Migrate is included from the CDN (//code.jquery.com/jquery-migrate-1.2.1.min.js), it also runs in the muted mode since the minimized version configures the mute mode by default.    Please see https://jquery.com/download/#jquery-migrate-plugin for more information.    I will create a pull request to enable the mute mode irrespective of the way the library is included (CDN or local). """
comments: "To reproduce the problem: Log in, open the browser console and browse to /Activity-Feed/userId/1. jQuery Migrate will log the message: ""JQMIGRATE: Logging is active"" into the console." summary: "StoredProcedure GetSchedule should ""find"" server that are not pre- and/or postfixed with a comma"
description: """If you create a scheduler programmatically and set the server name to something like """"ServerName1"""" that name is stored in the database table Schedule as """"ServerName1"""". If you create the scheduler using the host admin UI, the server name is pre- and postfixed with a comma and stored as """",ServerName1,"""" in the database.    If you do not use the UI and the server name is stored without commas, you get strange behaviors: At some point the scheduler stops running. IMHO the problem is caused by the stored procedure """"GetSchedule"""" that requires the commas. On the other side DotNetNuke.Services.Scheduling.SchedulingController.CanRunOnThisServer(..) does not require commas.    I will create a pull request that updates """"GetSchedule"""" to behave more like DotNetNuke.Services.Scheduling.SchedulingController.CanRunOnThisServer(...).  """

summary: """Use Captcha"" checkbox will not save in Admin>Extensions>Default Authentication"
description: """*Brief Description of Issue: *  In an attempt to remove the CAPTCHA from my site's Login dialogue but leave it in place on the registration form, I discovered what appears to be a bug in DNN version 7.3.4. This suspicion was confirmed by a moderator on the DNN forums who suggested that I post it here for resolution.     *Clear Steps to Reproduce:*   1. Go to Admin > Site Settings > User Account Settings anc check the """"Use CAPTCHA for registration"""" box, and also un-check the """"Use CAPTCHA for Associating Logins"""" box    2. Go to Admin > Extensions > Default Authentication and un-check the """"Use Captcha?"""" box    3. Click the """"Update Authentication Settings"""" button to save the change      *Actual Result: *  Change is not saved; no changes to CAPTCHA on Login dialogue. Returning to the """"Use Captcha?"""" checkbox under Admin > Extensions > Default Authentication reveals that the box is now checked once again.     *Expected Result: *  The checkbox being unchecked should have stayed unchecked once the """"Update Authentication Settings"""" button was clicked to save that change, and the CAPTCHA should have been removed from the Login dialogue as a result of this setting.     """

summary: "SI: Render blocking Javascript"
description: """Google page speed test suggests to avoid render blocking JavaScript. It also gives a list of JavaScript files those are being blocked.    List includes files from skin and DNN.    In the list of blocking JavaScript, there are files related to DNN.  -- js/dnn.js  -- js/dnn.modalpopup.js  -- js/dnn.servicesframework.js  -- js/dnncore.js  -- Telerik.Web.UI.WebResource.axd  -- Resources/libraries/jQuery/01_09_01/jquery.js  -- Resources/libraries/jQuery-Migrate/01_02_01/jquery-migrate.js    To see these files, right-click view source in Browser for any DNN website. These files are loading well after the <body> tag.    It seems that script tags in random places in the body, between html elements, preferably needs to be moved right before the body tag ends.    If it's not needed to render anything that is what happens is the renderer reads the html sees the script tag waits for the script tag to finish loading /. executing then continues on to more html = render blocking by javascript, if you render all HTML then execute / load all JS at the bottom before the body tag ends = no render blocking """

summary: "Remove outdated Navigation Providers"
description: """DNN Platform contains several Navigation providers which are outdated and no longer supported.  These providers add to the overall memory footprint of the platform and slow down the startup time for the platform.  As a result the following projects and assemblies should be removed:  * ASP2MenuNavigationProviders  * DNNDropDownNavigationProvider  * DNNTreeNavigationProvider  * SolpartMenuNavigationProviders  * SolpartWebControls    *NOTE:* This is a *breaking change*.  Old skins which rely on these menus will break.  We have removed all references to Solpart Menu and any skins or containers which used them are not compatible with DNN 8.  This is by design."""
comments: "AFAIK those providers are no longer installed by default (in all versions of DNN 7)" [SEP] "Merged PR https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/724" [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> Unfortunately, those providers are still installed by default into the \bin\providers directory.  Originally, we created the \providers directory so that assemblies wouldn't get loaded automatically at startup, but were available if called. This worked well until we added WebAPI support, and subsequently started using MEF.  Both of those features scan the entire folder tree under the \bin folder looking for assemblies/classes which implement certain interfaces.  This causes any assembly in the \bin or child folders to get loaded into memory, even if they will never be used.  As a result we have been loading a lot of unneeded assemblies into memory for the past four or five years without realizing it.  This enhancement is part of the cleanup we are doing in DNN 8 to get rid of unused features which add bloat to DNN and cause perfo

summary: "SI: Admin Reset Password - Child Portals"
description: """Forcing a user to reset their password on their next login does not work as expected for child portals. A parent portal functions correctly.    When the a user is forced to change their password, they are taken to a page where they confirm their username and new password. On a child site, the url for the users password reset page is missing the child portal portion. For example, the parent 'localhost' can be reset correctly, but the child 'localhost/test' does not because 'test' is missing from the url. If 'test is added to the url manually and the page is refreshed, a user can change the password.    Steps to reproduce:  1. Create a child site and a test user.  2. Force a password reset on the users next login.  3. Login with the user and attempt to change the password.    Expected behavior: The password is updated and the user can login with the new password.    Actual behavior: The user cannot change the password because the url is pointed to the parent portal where the user does not exist. Manually changing the url to include the child portal and refreshing the page in necessary for the user to update their password."""

summary: "Update UrlRoutingModule handler to use preCondition=""managedHandler"""
description: """In DNN's web.config file, there is a HttpModule definition for the UrlRoutingModule. Due to a bug that affected users on machines without a patch for extensionless url's, this HttpModule had a wide definition where it examined all requests. As url rewriting should only happen on .net mapped requests (e.g. aspx), this affects platform performance.     As the bug previously mentions, a patch has been available since 2010 (and does not apply to Windows 8/10/2012/2012r2/2016).  The module should be set with a precondition=""""managedHandler"""" for both upgrades and new installs."""

summary: "Improve MobileRedirectModule performance by doing ClientCapability checks only when needed"
description: """DNN by default has a definition for the MobileRedirect httpmodule which runs for all .net requests (except for install/upgrade requests).As part of it's logic it checks to see if a redirect rule exists and if so checks if it applies to the page (and a few other pieces of validation). Before this check loop the clientcapabilities are read from 51degrees even though they may not later be checked against (e.g. if the current page does not have a redirect rule). This has been refactored to ensure the capabilities are only loaded if needed"""

summary: "upgrade 51 degrees to 3.2.3.2"
description: """From Pull Request: https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/621    •We have performed load testing and published the results: https://51degrees.com/blog/ArtMID/1641/ArticleID/368/Memory-and-Performance-Impact-of-51Degrees   •We have added multiple unit tests: https://github.com/51Degrees/.NET-Device-Detection/tree/master/UnitTests   •We have been running the updated version of the DLL on our own website (also powered by DNN) for quite some time now.    The design focus of the release is to integrate DNN efficiently with 51Degrees. Improvements include reducing memory consumption when the data file used directly from the disk, and to make use of 51Degrees performance improvements.    List of changes:  •When device detection is disabled the Client Capabilities Provider no longer forces it to start.  •All possible properties and values are no longer stored in a SortedList. Only required properties and values are loaded when needed and cached.  •Embedded data has been removed from the assembly and now must be provided from the App_Data folder.  •.NET 3.5 is no longer supported in order to use memory mapped files and simplify overriding default browser capabilities.  •In stream mode entity data properties that can allocate large arrays only initialise these arrays when needed.  •Caches used with stream operation are now fixed memory size and serviced via the thread pool.  •Automatic update processes uses temporary files rather than main memory to verify integrity of updated files prior to using them.  •Temporary files are now created in the App_Data/51Degrees folder of the web application rather than a UNC path or the master data file folder.  •Values associated with Profiles are now retrieved using a more efficient algorithm.  •DataSet.Properties collection now has a string accesser to make retrieving properties by name simpler.  •Memory mode no longer initialises the entire dataset. Startup time is now significantly improved.  •Memory mode uses less memory than prior versions.  •Supports 51Degrees V3.2 data format using approximately 25% smaller file sizes.  """
comments: "backported to 7.4.2" [SEP] "additional changes checked in directly to fix time-sensitive build issue:  1. update 51degrees test project to use v32 version of 51degrees dat file, and 3.2.3.2 version of dll    2. commented out test that was failed where TC did not respect the ignore directive.    3. removed  FiftyOneClientCapabilityProvider_GetClientCapabilityById_ThrowsException_For_NonExisting_ClientCapabilityId test from test project (as 51degrees logic no longer returns an exception but returns the useragent - this is necessary to support disabling 51degrees without breaking 3rd party integrations)    Note: these were all done in https://dnntracker.atlassian.net/browse/DNN-7433 and then cherrypicked over to the development branch to ensure they were in 8.0.0" [SEP] "Also removed FiftyOne.Foundation.dll from Evoq's repository. This was a copy of the same DLL checked-in into Evoq as was in Platform. Now Evoq simply points to file from Platform. " [SEP] "I profiled the memory use of 51D in both {{Platform 8.

summary: "Performance: No need to refresh the whole page when move layout. "
description: """  - In Content Pane of any default page, I added 2 modules: HTML and a Layout. Layout is on the top of the HTML (I have an image uploaded in HTML module)  - Move the Layout to the bottom of the pane, by Drag-n-Drop style.     Result: The pane got reloaded, not the whole page. (Expected)    - Again, move the layout to the top of the pane, by Menu (Module position menu > Top)    Result: You can see the layout got moved to the top, and then the whole page will get reloaded.   http://screencast.com/t/LTCUJcy7    Expected:   Looks like there is no need to refresh the whole page if we can reload just that changed pane. Better UX and faster. """
